</div>\n      <p class=\"card-desc\">\n        The <code>background-image</code> value on line 32 is missing its closing parenthesis <code>)</code> before the semicolon.\n        The property value reads <code>url('\u2026';</code> instead of <code>url('\u2026');</code>.\n        Because the declaration is malformed, the browser discards the entire <code>.image-container</code> rule block,\n        so the right-hand column renders as a plain black rectangle \u2014 the image never appears.\n      </p>\n      <p class=\"card-location\">Line 32 \u00b7 .image-container { background-image: \u2026 }</p>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Broken</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">background-image: url('https://images.unsplash.com/\u2026%3D%3D';</div>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Fixed</div>\n class=\"section-header\">\n      <div class=\"dot dot-high\"></div>\n      <h2>High</h2>\n      <span class=\"pill pill-high\">1 issue</span>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"card card-high\">\n      <div class=\"card-title\">\n        Wrong CSS class selector in JavaScript \u2014 toggle is completely broken\n        <span class=\"tag tag-functional\">Functional</span>\n      </div>\n      <p class=\"card-desc\">\n        Inside <code>toggleMenuItems()</code> (line 137), <code>document.querySelector('.menu-item')</code>\n        targets a class called <code>menu-item</code> (singular). The actual element has the class\n        <code>menu-items</code> (plural). The selector matches <em>nothing</em>, so\n        <code>menuItems</code> is <code>null</code> and the next line throws\n        <code>TypeError: Cannot set properties of null</code>.\n        Clicking \"Our Menu\" produces a JavaScript error and the list never expands.\n      </p>\n      <p class=\"card-location\">Line 137 \u00b7 function toggleMenuItems()</p>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Broken</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">const menuItems = document.querySelector('.menu-item');</div>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Fixed</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">const menuItems = document.querySelector('.menu-items');</div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <!-- MEDIUM -->\n  <div class=\"section\">\n    <div class=\"section-header\">\n      <div class=\"dot dot-medium\"></div>\n      <h2>Medium</h2>\n      <span class=\"pill pill-medium\">2 issues</span>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"card card-medium\">\n      <div class=\"card-title\">\n        <code>header h1</code> and <code>header p</code> rules target a non-existent element\n        <span class=\"tag tag-structural\">Structural</span>\n      </div>\n      <p class=\"card-desc\">\n        Lines 50 and 58 define CSS rules scoped to a <code>&lt;header&gt;</code> element\n        (<code>header h1 { \u2026 }</code>, <code>header p { \u2026 }</code>).\n        The HTML contains no <code>&lt;header&gt;</code> tag \u2014 the heading and paragraph\n        live inside <code>&lt;div class=\"header-container\"&gt;</code>.\n        As a result, the gold colour, large font-size, and text-shadow for the\n        <code>&lt;h1&gt;</code> and the white colour / text-shadow for the <code>&lt;p&gt;</code>\n        are <em>never applied</em>. Both elements fall back to browser defaults.\n      </p>\n      <p class=\"card-location\">Lines 50\u201363 (CSS) / Lines 117\u2013120 (HTML)</p>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Option A \u2014 change the CSS selectors to match the existing HTML</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">.header-container h1 { \u2026 }\n.header-container p  { \u2026 }</div>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Option B \u2014 replace the div with a semantic header element</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">&lt;header class=\"header-container\"&gt; \u2026 &lt;/header&gt;</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"card card-medium\">\n      <div class=\"card-title\">\n        Conflicting <code>background</code> declarations on <code>.header-container</code>\n        <span class=\"tag tag-syntax\">Syntax</span>\n      </div>\n      <p class=\"card-desc\">\n        Lines 39 and 47 both set a background on <code>.header-container</code>.\n        Line 39 sets <code>background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.1)</code> (semi-transparent white glass effect).\n        Line 47 sets the shorthand <code>background: wheat</code>, which overwrites <em>all</em>\n        background sub-properties, including the colour set four lines earlier.\n        The glass effect is completely lost; the container always renders solid wheat.\n        One of the two declarations must be removed depending on the intended design.\n      </p>\n      <p class=\"card-location\">Lines 39 and 47 \u00b7 .header-container { \u2026 }</p>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Fix \u2014 remove the redundant line that is not wanted</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">/* Keep one of these, remove the other: */\nbackground-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);  /* glass */\nbackground: wheat;                            /* solid */</div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <footer>Made with IBM Bob</footer>\n</div>\n</body>\n</html>\n"
      },
      "isError": false

The selector matches <em>nothing</em>, so\n        <code>menuItems</code> is <code>null</code> and the next line throws\n        <code>TypeError: Cannot set properties of null</code>.\n        Clicking \"Our Menu\" produces a JavaScript error and the list never expands.\n      </p>\n      <p class=\"card-location\">Line 137 \u00b7 function toggleMenuItems()</p>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Broken</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">const menuItems = document.querySelector('.menu-item');</div>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Fixed</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">const menuItems = document.querySelector('.menu-items');</div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <!-- MEDIUM -->\n  <div class=\"section\">\n    <div class=\"section-header\">\n      <div class=\"dot dot-medium\"></div>\n      <h2>Medium</h2>\n      <span class=\"pill pill-medium\">2 issues</span>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"card card-medium\">\n      <div class=\"card-title\">\n        <code>header h1</code> and <code>header p</code> rules target a non-existent element\n        <span class=\"tag tag-structural\">Structural</span>\n      </div>\n      <p class=\"card-desc\">\n        Lines 50 and 58 define CSS rules scoped to a <code>&lt;header&gt;</code> element\n        (<code>header h1 { \u2026 }</code>, <code>header p { \u2026 }</code>).\n        The HTML contains no <code>&lt;header&gt;</code> tag \u2014 the heading and paragraph\n        live inside <code>&lt;div class=\"header-container\"&gt;</code>.\n        As a result, the gold colour, large font-size, and text-shadow for the\n        <code>&lt;h1&gt;</code> and the white colour / text-shadow for the <code>&lt;p&gt;</code>\n        are <em>never applied</em>. Both elements fall back to browser defaults.\n      </p>\n      <p class=\"card-location\">Lines 50\u201363 (CSS) / Lines 117\u2013120 (HTML)</p>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Option A \u2014 change the CSS selectors to match the existing HTML</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">.header-container h1 { \u2026 }\n.header-container p  { \u2026 }</div>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Option B \u2014 replace the div with a semantic header element</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">&lt;header class=\"header-container\"&gt; \u2026 &lt;/header&gt;</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"card card-medium\">\n      <div class=\"card-title\">\n        Conflicting <code>background</code> declarations on <code>.header-container</code>\n        <span class=\"tag tag-syntax\">Syntax</span>\n      </div>\n      <p class=\"card-desc\">\n        Lines 39 and 47 both set a background on <code>.header-container</code>.\n        Line 39 sets <code>background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.1)</code> (semi-transparent white glass effect).\n        Line 47 sets the shorthand <code>background: wheat</code>, which overwrites <em>all</em>\n        background sub-properties, including the colour set four lines earlier.\n        The glass effect is completely lost; the container always renders solid wheat.\n        One of the two declarations must be removed depending on the intended design.\n      </p>\n      <p class=\"card-location\">Lines 39 and 47 \u00b7 .header-container { \u2026 }</p>\n      <div class=\"fix-label\">Fix \u2014 remove the redundant line that is not wanted</div>\n      <div class=\"card-fix\">/* Keep one of these, remove the other: */\nbackground-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);  /* glass */\nbackground: wheat;                            /* solid */</div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <footer>Made with IBM Bob</footer>\n</div>\n</body>\n</html>\n"
      },

**Assistant** (1784240686037):
{
  "role": "assistant",
  "content": "Here is a summary of the **4 issues** found, grouped by severity:\n\n---\n\n### \ud83d\udd34 Critical (1)\n**Line 32 \u2014 Malformed `background-image` URL (Syntax)**\nThe closing `)` is missing from the `url(\u2026)` value, making the entire CSS rule block invalid. The right-side image column will render as a blank black area.\n```css\n/* Broken */\nbackground-image: url('https://\u2026%3D%3D';\n/* Fixed */\nbackground-image: url('https://\u2026%3D%3D');\n```\n\n---\n\n### \ud83d\udfe0 High (1)\n**Line 137 \u2014 Wrong class selector in `toggleMenuItems()` (Functional)**\nThe JavaScript queries `.menu-item` but the element's class is `.menu-items` (plural). `querySelector` returns `null`, causing a `TypeError` crash on every click \u2014 the menu toggle is completely non-functional.\n```js\n// Broken\nconst menuItems = document.querySelector('.menu-item');\n// Fixed\nconst menuItems = document.querySelector('.menu-items');\n```\n\n---\n\n### \ud83d\udfe1 Medium (2)\n1. **Lines 50\u201363 CSS / Lines 117\u2013120 HTML \u2014 `header h1` / `header p` selectors target a non-existent element (Structural)**\n   The CSS rules are scoped to a `<header>` tag that doesn't exist in the HTML. The heading's gold colour, large font-size, and text-shadow are never applied.\n\n2. **Lines 39 & 47 \u2014 Conflicting `background` declarations on `.header-container` (Syntax)**\n   `background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.1)` is immediately overridden by `background: wheat` later in the same rule. The intended glass-effect is silently lost; the box always renders solid wheat.\n\n---\n\nThe full report with exact line references and fix suggestions is in the artifact above.",
  "id": "98b2a19fdc720c867da4dffeac2cbb04",

        "before": "<!DOCTYPE html>\r\n<html lang=\"en\">\r\n<head>\r\n   <title>Roast Toast Coast Cafe</title>\r\n   <style>\r\n       @font-face {\r\n           font-family: 'Bogart';\r\n           src: url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/s/24702/Bogart-Light.woff') format('woff');\r\n           font-weight: normal;\r\n           font-style: normal;\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       body {\r\n           background-color: black;\r\n           font-family: 'Bogart', sans-serif;\r\n           margin: 0;\r\n           padding: 0;\r\n           display: grid;\r\n           grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; /* Two columns of equal width */\r\n           height: 100vh; /* Full height */\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       .content {\r\n           display: flex;\r\n           flex-direction: column;\r\n           justify-content: center;\r\n           align-items: center;\r\n           padding: 20px;\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       .image-container {\r\n           background-image: url('https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1514066558159-fc8c737ef259?q=80&w=2574&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.0.3&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8MHx8fA%3D%3D';\r\n           background-size: cover;\r\n           background-position: center;\r\n           height: 100%;\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       .header-container {\r\n           background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); /* Semi-transparent white */\r\n           border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3); /* Light border for a sleek look */\r\n           border-radius: 15px;\r\n           padding: 20px;\r\n           margin-bottom: 20px;\r\n           width: 90%;\r\n           box-shadow: 0px 4px 15px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.7); /* Soft shadow */\r\n           text-align: center;\r\n           background: wheat;\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       header h1 {\r\n           color: #FFCC00; /* Bright gold for contrast */\r\n           font-size: 3em;\r\n           margin-bottom: 10px;\r\n           text-shadow: 2px 2px 5px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8); /* Subtle shadow for depth */\r\n           line-height: 1.2; /* Adjust spacing between lines */\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       header p {\r\n           font-size: 1.3em;\r\n           color: #FFFFFF; /* White for good readability */\r\n           margin: 0;\r\n           text-shadow: 1px 1px 3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6); /* Subtle shadow for depth */\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       /* .menu-section {\r\n           background-color: #000000;\r\n           color: #FFFFFF;\r\n           padding: 20px;\r\n           margin: 0;\r\n           width: 90%;\r\n           border-radius: 15px;\r\n           box-shadow: 0px 4px 10px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.7);\r\n       } */\r\n       .menu-section {\r\n           background-color: #000000;\r\n           color: #FFFFFF;\r\n           padding: 10px;\r\n           margin: 20px 0 20px 10%; /* Align section to the left */\r\n           width: 60%;\r\n           border-radius: 10px;\r\n           box-shadow: 0px 4px 10px rgba(189, 180, 180, 0.7);\r\n           text-align: center;\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       .menu-section h2 {\r\n           color: #FFCC00;\r\n           font-size: 2em;\r\n           margin-bottom: 20px;\r\n           cursor: pointer;\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       .menu-items {\r\n           list-style-type: none;\r\n           padding: 0;\r\n           display: none; /* Hidden by default */\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       .menu-items li {\r\n           background-color: #222222;\r\n           color: #F7F7F7;\r\n           padding: 10px;\r\n           border-radius: 5px;\r\n           margin: 10px 0;\r\n           text-align: center;\r\n           font-size: 1.1em;\r\n           box-shadow: 0px 2px 5px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       .menu-toggle:hover {\r\n           color: #FF9933;\r\n           cursor: pointer;\r\n       }\r\n   </style>\r\n</head>\r\n<body>\r\n   <div class=\"content\">\r\n       <div class=\"header-container\">\r\n           <h1>Welcome to the<br>Roast Toast Coast Cafe!</h1>\r\n           <p>Where flavor meets the shore!</p>\r\n       </div>\r\n\r\n       <section class=\"menu-section\">\r\n           <h2 class=\"menu-toggle\" onclick=\"toggleMenuItems()\">Our Menu</h2>\r\n           <ul class=\"menu-items\">\r\n               <li>Breezy Brews</li>\r\n               <li>Toasty Delights</li>\r\n               <li>Coastal Bites</li>\r\n               <li>Sunset Sips</li>\r\n           </ul>\r\n       </section>\r\n   </div>\r\n\r\n   <div class=\"image-container\"></div>\r\n\r\n   <script>\r\n       function toggleMenuItems() {\r\n           const menuItems = document.querySelector('.menu-item');\r\n           menuItems.style.display = menuItems.style.display === 'block' ? /* Hidden by default */\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       .menu-items li {\r\n           background-color: #222222;\r\n           color: #F7F7F7;\r\n           padding: 10px;\r\n           border-radius: 5px;\r\n           margin: 10px 0;\r\n           text-align: center;\r\n           font-size: 1.1em;\r\n           box-shadow: 0px 2px 5px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);\r\n       }\r\n\r\n       .menu-toggle:hover {\r\n           color: #FF9933;\r\n           cursor: pointer;\r\n       }\r\n   </style>\r\n</head>\r\n<body>\r\n   <div class=\"content\">\r\n       <div class=\"header-container\">\r\n           <h1>Welcome to the<br>Roast Toast Coast Cafe!</h1>\r\n           <p>Where flavor meets the shore!</p>\r\n       </div>\r\n\r\n       <section class=\"menu-section\">\r\n           <h2 class=\"menu-toggle\" onclick=\"toggleMenuItems()\">Our Menu</h2>\r\n           <ul class=\"menu-items\">\r\n               <li>Breezy Brews</li>\r\n               <li>Toasty Delights</li>\r\n               <li>Coastal Bites</li>\r\n               <li>Sunset Sips</li>\r\n           </ul>\r\n       </section>\r\n   </div>\r\n\r\n   <div class=\"image-container\"></div>\r\n\r\n   <script>\r\n       function toggleMenuItems() {\r\n           const menuItems = document.querySelector('.menu-items');\r\n           menuItems.style.display = menuItems.style.display === 'block' ? 'none' : 'block';\r\n       }\r\n   </script>\r\n</body>\r\n</html>\r\n",

Do not start messages with \"Great\", \"Certainly\", \"Okay\", or \"Sure\".\n- Follow existing code style and project conventions.\n- Use available tools to accomplish tasks efficiently. **Tool** (1784245774683):
{
  "role": "tool",
  "content": "# Create Plan\n\nFollow this structured workflow to produce a high-quality, actionable plan.\n\n## Step 1 \u2014 Gather Requirements\n\nAsk the user clarifying questions to fully understand the task:\n- What is the goal and expected outcome?\n- Are there constraints, preferences, or non-goals?\n- Which parts of the codebase are likely involved?\n- Any related issues or requirement documents?\n\nDo not proceed until you have enough context to scope the work, be critical of vague intent or requirements.\n\n## Step 2 \u2014 Code Search via Sub-Agent\n\nSpawn a `spawn_subagent` of type `\"explore\"` to research the codebase. The sub-agent should:\n- Locate relevant files, symbols, and patterns related to the task\n- Identify existing utilities or abstractions that should be reused\n- Identify existing design patterns\n- Surface any constraints or conventions (e.g. patterns in similar files)\n\nUse the sub-agent's findings to ground your plan in the actual code.\n\n## Step 3 \u2014 Clarify Design Intention\n\nBased on the requirements and code research:\n- Always confirm your understanding of the intended design with the user\n- Raise any open questions or trade-offs that need a decision\n- Adjust scope if the code research revealed complexity or simplifications\n\nDo not begin writing the plan document until design intent is confirmed.\n\n## Step 4 \u2014 Write the Plan File\n\nWrite a structured plan to a markdown file (e.g. `{short-plan-name}-plan.md`). The plan must NOT go into low-level code detail \u2014 focus on *what* needs to happen and *why*.\n\nThe plan file must include:\n\n### Top-Level Overview\nA concise summary of the goal, scope, and approach.\n\n### Sub-Tasks\nBreak the work into sub-tasks. Each sub-task must have:\n- **Intent** \u2014 what this sub-task achieves and why\n- **Expected Outcomes** \u2014 observable results when this sub-task is complete\n- **Todo List** \u2014 ordered, specific steps to achieve the outcome\n- **Relevant Context** \u2014 pointers to relevant files, symbols, or patterns\n- **Status** \u2014 `[ ] pending` (updated to `[x] done` after completion)\n\nDesign each sub-task to be processed independently, one at a time, so changes stay focused and reviewable.\n\n## Step 5 \u2014 Plan Validation\n\nBefore moving to implementation, ensure that the user has fully read the plan. Always ask the user targeted questions to verify the plan is correct and complete:\n- Does this plan capture the full scope of the task?\n- Are the sub-task boundaries and ordering correct?\n- Is any context missing that would be needed during implementation?\n\nUse Mermaid diagrams in chat responses where they clarify architecture or workflow, but do not place Mermaid diagrams directly in the plan file. Avoid double quotes and parentheses inside square brackets in Mermaid syntax.\n\nRefine the plan file based on the user's answers.\n\n## Step 6 \u2014 Implementation\n\nOnly after the user confirms the plan, recommend implementation by calling the `switch_mode` tool to `agent`.\n\nWhen explaining how to implement the plan in agent mode:\n- Using the `start_subtask` tool, create a new task for each subtask in the <plan-file>.\n- In the prompt for the subtask make sure it reads the plan-file to gain the full context around the task.\n- After each sub task is complete update the task status in the <plan-file>\n- Add any context needed for the next subtask in the <plan-file>\n- Wait for the user to check and ok the changes before moving on to the next subtask.\n\n## Reminders\n\n- Ask questions always using the \"ask_followup_question\" tool\n- Never include information without evidence\n- Don't include time estimates\n",

**Assistant** (1784245876946):
{
  "role": "assistant",
  "content": "Here's a full analysis of the **EchoDraft** project spec:\n\n---\n\n## \ud83d\udcc4 EchoDraft \u2014 Project Spec Analysis\n\n### What It Is\n**EchoDraft** is a voice-preserving content repurposing tool built for the **IBM AI Builders Challenge (July 2026)**, theme: *Reimagine Creative Industries with AI*. Deadline: **July 31, 2026**.\n\nThe core idea: paste one long-form piece of content \u2192 get platform-native versions (LinkedIn, X/Twitter, Instagram, YouTube, Email) that still sound like *you*, not generic AI.\n\n---\n\n### The Problem It Solves\nThree pain points identified:\n1. **Repurposing rarely works** \u2014 copy-pasting the same message across platforms fails; each needs its own hook and pacing.\n2. **AI flattens your voice** \u2014 generic AI output hurts creators because 2026 audiences reward authentic, on-brand content.\n3. **Disconnected tools = manual labor** \u2014 creators are the human bridge between research, writing, and publishing.\n\n---\n\n### Core MVP Features\n| Feature | Description |\n|---|---|\n| **Voice Profile Builder** | Paste 3\u20135 writing samples; AI extracts tone, vocabulary, rhythm, and do/don't traits |\n| **Pillar Input** | Paste a blog post, transcript, or long caption |\n| **Platform-native generation** | LinkedIn, X/Twitter thread, Instagram caption, YouTube description, Email newsletter blurb |\n| **Voice-match rewrite** | Every output is conditioned on the saved voice profile |\n| **Copy / export** | One-click copy per platform + \"export all\" |\n\n**Nice-to-haves** (if time permits): Authenticity check, \"regenerate with tweak,\" multiple saved voice profiles.\n\n---\n\n### Tech Stack\n| Layer | Choice |\n|---|---|\n| **Frontend** | Next.js + React + Tailwind |\n| **AI** | IBM Granite / watsonx *or* hosted LLM API (Gemini Flash recommended) |\n| **Orchestration** | LangChain (optional) |\n| **Storage** | LocalStorage or SQLite/Supabase |\n| **Hosting** | Vercel (free tier) |\n\n---\n\n### Architecture (Simple Flow)\n```\nUser samples \u2192 [Voice Profile Builder] \u2192 voiceProfile (JSON style descriptor)\n                                                  \u2502\nPillar text \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n                                    \u2193\n              [Generation Engine: pillar + platform rules + voiceProfile]\n                    \u2502         \u2502         \u2502          \u2502          \u2502\n                LinkedIn   X thread  Instagram  YouTube  Newsletter\n```\n\n---\n\n### Cost Strategy \u2014 $0 Total\n| Item | Cost |\n|---|---|\n| IBM Bob (dev tool) | Free \u2014 40 Bobcoins / 30-day trial |\n| App's LLM API | Free \u2014 Gemini Flash (1.5M tokens/day, no credit card) |\n| Hosting | Free \u2014 Vercel |\n| Domain | Free \u2014 `.vercel.app` |\n| GitHub repo | Free |\n\n**Recommended approach:** Build with **Gemini Flash free API**, state in README that EchoDraft is designed to run on IBM Granite (swappable). This gives $0 cost + a working public demo + \"uses IBM tech\" narrative for judges.\n\n---\n\n### Build Timeline (2 Weeks)\n| Dates | Focus |\n|---|---|\n| Jul 17\u201318 | Register, start Bob trial, SkillsBuild cert, scaffold Next.js repo |\n| Jul 19\u201321 | Voice Profile Builder UI + first platform (LinkedIn) |\n| Jul 22\u201324 | Remaining platforms + prompt tuning |\n| Jul 25\u201326 | Voice-match refinement, copy/export, styling |\n| Jul 27\u201328 | Vercel deploy, README, testing |\n| Jul 29 | Record demo video, publish Project Page |\n| Jul 30 | Buffer: retest all links in incognito, final polish |\n| Jul 31 | Emergency buffer only |\n\n---\n\n### Key Risks\n| Risk | Mitigation |\n|---|---|\n| Bobcoin/trial limits | Push to GitHub constantly; have a second email ready |\n| Voice match feels weak | Pick a distinctive sample voice for the demo video |\n| Scope creep | Ship 5-platform MVP first; authenticity check is bonus only |\n\n---\n\n### How IBM Bob Is Used (for judges)\n- Plan mode: understanding requirements and planning the app\n- Code mode: scaffolding Next.js project and components\n- Designing the voice-profile data structure\n- Writing and refining generation prompts\n- Debugging API calls and state handling\n- Generating tests and improving the README\n\n---\n\n### Summary Assessment\n\nThis is a **well-scoped, judging-aligned spec**. The idea is differentiated (voice preservation vs. generic \"generate a post\" tools), the tech stack is achievable solo in 2 weeks, and the $0 cost path is clearly mapped. The biggest execution risk is the **voice-match quality** feeling weak in the demo \u2014 the spec itself flags this and recommends using a distinctive sample voice. The timeline is tight but realistic if scope is held to the 5-platform MVP.\n\n---\n\nWould you like me to create a **plan file** for implementing EchoDraft using this spec?",

  "_meta": {

      "input": 14372,
      "output": 3414,
      "cacheRead": 14222,
      "cacheWrite": 149,
      "cost": 0.044465,
      "contextTokens": 17786,
      "reasoningTokens": 0
    }
  },
  "id": "860aa3f094d6392ead15cbfc3dad2c64",
  "toolCalls": [
    {
      "id": "tooluse_O5u8wyUC2S9moKFiqPJsul",
      "name": "list_files",
      "arguments": {
        "path": "."
      },
      "isError": false
    },
    {
      "id": "tooluse_hDTrQgAssR8SQIRTNNR89I",
      "name": "write_file",
      "arguments": {
        "path": "echodraft-build-plan.md",
        "line_count": 310,
        "content": "# EchoDraft \u2014 Build Plan\n> IBM AI Builders Challenge \u00b7 July 2026 \u00b7 Theme: Reimagine Creative Industries with AI  \n> Deadline: **July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET**\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nEchoDraft is a voice-preserving content repurposing tool. One long-form input \u2192 platform-native posts (LinkedIn, X/Twitter, Instagram, YouTube, Email) that still sound like the creator \u2014 not generic AI.\n\nThis plan breaks the build into **6 phases**, each with concrete steps and a test checkpoint to confirm it works before moving on.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1 \u2014 Project Setup & Scaffold\n\n**Goal:** A running Next.js app deployed to Vercel with a public GitHub repo.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. Register for the IBM AI Builders Challenge platform and start the IBM Bob 30-day free trial.\n2. Complete at least one IBM SkillsBuild activity and save the certificate (required for submission).\n3. Create a new **public** GitHub repository named `echodraft`.\n4. Scaffold the project locally:\n   ```bash\n   npx create-next-app@latest echodraft --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app\n   cd echodraft\n   ```\n5. Push the initial scaffold to GitHub:\n   ```bash\n   git add .\n   git commit -m \"chore: scaffold Next.js project\"\n   git push origin main\n   ```\n6. Connect the GitHub repo to **Vercel** (import project \u2192 auto-deploy).\n7. Create a `.env.local` file for API keys (add to `.gitignore`):\n   ```\n   GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here\n   ```\n8. Get a free **Gemini Flash API key** from [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/) \u2014 no credit card required.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 1 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] `npm run dev` starts the app at `http://localhost:3000` with no errors.\n- [ ] The Vercel dashboard shows a live public URL (e.g. `echodraft.vercel.app`).\n- [ ] The GitHub repo is public and shows the scaffold commit.\n- [ ] `.env.local` is listed in `.gitignore` (API key is NOT committed).\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2 \u2014 Voice Profile Builder UI\n\n**Goal:** A page where the user pastes 3\u20135 writing samples, submits them, and receives a saved `voiceProfile` JSON object extracted by AI.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. Create the page route `app/voice-profile/page.tsx`.\n2. Build a form UI with:\n   - A title: \"Build Your Voice Profile\"\n   - A `<textarea>` for Sample 1, with an \"Add another sample\" button that appends up to 5 textarea fields.\n   - A \"Build My Voice Profile\" submit button.\n3. Create an API route `app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts`.\n4. In the API route, send the samples to the Gemini Flash API with a prompt like:\n   ```\n   Analyze these writing samples and extract a voice profile as JSON with these fields:\n   - tone (e.g. \"conversational, direct, warm\")\n   - vocabulary (e.g. \"plain English, avoids jargon, uses analogies\")\n   - sentence_rhythm (e.g. \"short punchy sentences, varies length\")\n   - dos (array of style traits to keep)\n   - donts (array of things to avoid)\n   \n   Samples:\n   {samples}\n   ```\n5. Return the extracted `voiceProfile` JSON to the frontend.\n6. On success, save the `voiceProfile` to `localStorage` under the key `echodraft_voice_profile`.\n7. Display the saved profile on screen in a readable card format (tone, vocabulary, rhythm, dos, don'ts).\n\n### \u2705 Phase 2 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] Pasting 3 writing samples and clicking \"Build My Voice Profile\" returns a populated JSON profile.\n- [ ] The profile card renders correctly on screen with all fields visible.\n- [ ] Refreshing the page still shows the saved profile (loaded from `localStorage`).\n- [ ] The API route returns a `400` error gracefully if no samples are submitted.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 3 \u2014 Pillar Input & First Platform Generation (LinkedIn)\n\n**Goal:** A page where the user pastes their pillar content and generates a single LinkedIn post that matches their voice profile.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. Create the main page at `app/page.tsx` (home route).\n2. Build the UI with:\n   - A `<textarea>` labeled \"Paste your pillar content\" (blog post, transcript, or long caption).\n   - A \"Generate Posts\" button.\n   - A results area (initially empty).\n3. On submit, load the `voiceProfile` from `localStorage`.\n4. Create an API route `app/api/generate/route.ts`.\n5. Start with **LinkedIn only** \u2014 send this prompt to Gemini Flash:\n   ```\n   You are rewriting content for a creator. Use the voice profile below to match their writing style exactly.\n   \n   Voice Profile:\n   {voiceProfile}\n   \n   Platform: LinkedIn\n   Rules: Professional but human tone. 1200\u20131500 characters. Strong opening hook. \n   End with a question or call to action. Use line breaks for readability. No hashtag spam (max 3).\n   \n   Pillar Content:\n   {pillarText}\n   \n   Write the LinkedIn post now:\n   ```\n6. Display the generated LinkedIn post in a result card with a \"Copy\" button.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 3 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] Pasting a blog post and clicking \"Generate Posts\" returns a LinkedIn post.\n- [ ] The output reads differently from the raw input (it's adapted, not copy-pasted).\n- [ ] The \"Copy\" button copies the text to the clipboard.\n- [ ] If no voice profile is saved, the user sees a prompt: \"Build your voice profile first.\"\n- [ ] If the pillar input is empty, the button is disabled or shows a validation message.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 4 \u2014 All 5 Platforms\n\n**Goal:** Extend the generation engine to produce all 5 platform-native outputs in one click.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. Update the API route `app/api/generate/route.ts` to run 5 parallel generation calls (or one batch call) for:\n   - **LinkedIn** \u2014 (already done in Phase 3)\n   - **X / Twitter Thread** \u2014 3\u20135 tweets, each under 280 chars, strong first tweet as hook, number each tweet (1/5, 2/5\u2026)\n   - **Instagram Caption** \u2014 conversational, 150\u2013300 words, hook in first line, relevant emojis, 5\u201310 hashtags at the end\n   - **YouTube Description** \u2014 200\u2013300 words, keyword-rich first paragraph, timestamps placeholder, links section\n   - **Email Newsletter Blurb** \u2014 100\u2013150 words, subject line suggestion, personal opener, one clear CTA\n2. Each platform prompt must include the `voiceProfile` to enforce voice matching.\n3. Update the results UI to show 5 cards \u2014 one per platform \u2014 each with:\n   - Platform name + icon\n   - Generated text\n   - \"Copy\" button\n4. Add an **\"Export All\"** button that downloads a `.txt` file containing all 5 outputs.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 4 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] One click produces all 5 platform outputs simultaneously.\n- [ ] Each output is visibly different in length, tone, and structure from the others.\n- [ ] Twitter/X output is split into numbered tweets, each \u2264 280 characters.\n- [ ] Instagram output ends with hashtags.\n- [ ] YouTube output has a keyword-rich first paragraph.\n- [ ] \"Export All\" downloads a `.txt` file with all 5 outputs clearly labeled.\n- [ ] All 5 \"Copy\" buttons work correctly.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 5 \u2014 Voice Match Refinement, Styling & Polish\n\n**Goal:** The app looks good, voice matching is noticeably effective, and the UX is smooth enough to demo.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. **Voice match tuning** \u2014 test with a distinctive voice (e.g. very casual/Gen-Z, or very formal/academic). Adjust prompts if outputs feel generic.\n2. **Navigation** \u2014 add a simple top nav with links: \"Home (Generate)\" and \"Voice Profile.\"\n3. **Loading states** \u2014 show a spinner or skeleton while AI is generating.\n4. **Error states** \u2014 show a user-friendly message if the API call fails (e.g. rate limit hit).\n5. **Mobile responsiveness** \u2014 ensure the layout works on a phone screen (Tailwind responsive classes).\n6. **Nice-to-have (if time):** Add a \"Regenerate with tweak\" input per card \u2014 a small text field where the user types \"make it shorter\" and re-runs just that platform's generation.\n7. **Nice-to-have (if time):** Authenticity check \u2014 after generation, run a second AI call that flags any phrases that sound \"generic AI\" and suggests alternatives.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 5 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] Test with two very different voice profiles \u2014 outputs should feel noticeably different from each other.\n- [ ] The app works and looks good on a mobile screen (375px width).\n- [ ] Loading spinner appears while generating and disappears when done.\n- [ ] A simulated API failure (wrong key) shows a readable error message \u2014 not a blank screen or console error.\n- [ ] Ask a friend or colleague to use the app without guidance \u2014 they can complete the full workflow without help.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 6 \u2014 Deploy, README, Demo Video & Submit\n\n**Goal:** Everything is live, documented, and submitted before the deadline.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. **Final deploy to Vercel:**\n   - Add `GEMINI_API_KEY` to Vercel environment variables (Settings \u2192 Environment Variables).\n   - Push final code \u2014 Vercel auto-deploys.\n   - Test the live URL end-to-end in an **incognito browser**.\n\n2. **Write the README** (required \u2014 must cover the Creative Industries theme):\n   ```markdown\n   # EchoDraft\n   ## Problem\n   ## Solution & How It Works\n   ## Challenge Theme: Reimagine Creative Industries with AI\n   ## AI & Technical Approach\n   ## Architecture\n   ## How IBM Bob Was Used\n   ## Tech Stack\n   ## Install & Run Locally\n   ## Live Demo\n   ## Demo Video\n   ```\n\n3. **Record the demo video (\u2264 3 minutes):**\n   - Show the Voice Profile Builder \u2014 paste samples, show the extracted profile.\n   - Show the main generate flow \u2014 paste a pillar piece, click generate, show all 5 outputs.\n   - Highlight one output side-by-side with the raw input to show the voice match.\n   - Upload to YouTube (unlisted is fine) or Loom.\n\n4. **Publish the Project Page** on the challenge platform:\n   - Fill in all required sections (do NOT leave as draft).\n   - Add the GitHub repo link, live demo link, and demo video link.\n\n5. **Final submission checklist:**\n   - [ ] Functioning prototype (core workflow works end-to-end).\n   - [ ] IBM Bob used as primary dev tool (documented in README).\n   - [ ] AI is a central, user-facing function.\n   - [ ] IBM SkillsBuild certificate completed and saved.\n   - [ ] Public GitHub repo with full README.\n   - [ ] Public demo video (\u2264 3 minutes).\n   - [ ] Project Page published (not draft), all sections complete.\n   - [ ] All links tested in incognito/private browser.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 6 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] The live Vercel URL works end-to-end in incognito (no login, no local env vars).\n- [ ] The GitHub repo is public and the README renders correctly on GitHub.\n- [ ] The demo video link is public and plays without sign-in.\n- [ ] The Project Page is published (not draft) and all links open correctly.\n- [ ] Every item in the submission checklist above is ticked.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference \u2014 File Structure\n\n```\nechodraft/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 app/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 page.tsx                        # Main generate page (home)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 voice-profile/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 page.tsx                    # Voice Profile Builder page\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 api/\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 build-voice-profile/\n\u2502       \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts                # AI: extract voice profile from samples\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 generate/\n\u2502           \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts                # AI: generate all 5 platform outputs\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 components/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 VoiceProfileCard.tsx            # Displays the saved voice profile\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 PlatformOutputCard.tsx          # One result card (text + copy button)\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 Navbar.tsx                      # Top navigation\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 lib/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 gemini.ts                       # Gemini API client helper\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 .env.local                          # API keys (gitignored)\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 README.md\n```\n\n---\n\n## Risk Watch List\n\n| Risk | What to watch for | Action |\n|---|---|---|\n| Bobcoin / trial runs out | Bob stops responding mid-build | Push to GitHub constantly; have a backup email ready |\n| Gemini rate limits during demo | Generation fails mid-video | Pre-run and screenshot all demo examples before recording |\n| Voice match feels generic | Outputs sound like each other | Use a very distinctive sample voice; tighten the voice profile prompt |\n| Scope creep | Running out of time | Ship the 5-platform MVP first \u2014 authenticity check and \"regenerate with tweak\" are bonus only |\n\n---\n\n*Built with IBM Bob \u00b7 Powered by Gemini Flash \u00b7 Hosted on Vercel*\n"
      },
      "isError": false
    }
  ]
}

Strong opening hook. \n   End with a question or call to action. Use line breaks for readability. No hashtag spam (max 3).\n   \n   Pillar Content:\n   {pillarText}\n   \n   Write the LinkedIn post now:\n   ```\n6. Display the generated LinkedIn post in a result card with a \"Copy\" button.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 3 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] Pasting a blog post and clicking \"Generate Posts\" returns a LinkedIn post.\n- [ ] The output reads differently from the raw input (it's adapted, not copy-pasted).\n- [ ] The \"Copy\" button copies the text to the clipboard.\n- [ ] If no voice profile is saved, the user sees a prompt: \"Build your voice profile first.\"\n- [ ] If the pillar input is empty, the button is disabled or shows a validation message.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 4 \u2014 All 5 Platforms\n\n**Goal:** Extend the generation engine to produce all 5 platform-native outputs in one click.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. Update the API route `app/api/generate/route.ts` to run 5 parallel generation calls (or one batch call) for:\n   - **LinkedIn** \u2014 (already done in Phase 3)\n   - **X / Twitter Thread** \u2014 3\u20135 tweets, each under 280 chars, strong first tweet as hook, number each tweet (1/5, 2/5\u2026)\n   - **Instagram Caption** \u2014 conversational, 150\u2013300 words, hook in first line, relevant emojis, 5\u201310 hashtags at the end\n   - **YouTube Description** \u2014 200\u2013300 words, keyword-rich first paragraph, timestamps placeholder, links section\n   - **Email Newsletter Blurb** \u2014 100\u2013150 words, subject line suggestion, personal opener, one clear CTA\n2. Each platform prompt must include the `voiceProfile` to enforce voice matching.\n3. Update the results UI to show 5 cards \u2014 one per platform \u2014 each with:\n   - Platform name + icon\n   - Generated text\n   - \"Copy\" button\n4. Add an **\"Export All\"** button that downloads a `.txt` file containing all 5 outputs.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 4 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] One click produces all 5 platform outputs simultaneously.\n- [ ] Each output is visibly different in length, tone, and structure from the others.\n- [ ] Twitter/X output is split into numbered tweets, each \u2264 280 characters.\n- [ ] Instagram output ends with hashtags.\n- [ ] YouTube output has a keyword-rich first paragraph.\n- [ ] \"Export All\" downloads a `.txt` file with all 5 outputs clearly labeled.\n- [ ] All 5 \"Copy\" buttons work correctly.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 5 \u2014 Voice Match Refinement, Styling & Polish\n\n**Goal:** The app looks good, voice matching is noticeably effective, and the UX is smooth enough to demo.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. **Voice match tuning** \u2014 test with a distinctive voice (e.g. very casual/Gen-Z, or very formal/academic). Adjust prompts if outputs feel generic.\n2. **Navigation** \u2014 add a simple top nav with links: \"Home (Generate)\" and \"Voice Profile.\"\n3. **Loading states** \u2014 show a spinner or skeleton while AI is generating.\n4. **Error states** \u2014 show a user-friendly message if the API call fails (e.g. rate limit hit).\n5. **Mobile responsiveness** \u2014 ensure the layout works on a phone screen (Tailwind responsive classes).\n6. **Nice-to-have (if time):** Add a \"Regenerate with tweak\" input per card \u2014 a small text field where the user types \"make it shorter\" and re-runs just that platform's generation.\n7. **Nice-to-have (if time):** Authenticity check \u2014 after generation, run a second AI call that flags any phrases that sound \"generic AI\" and suggests alternatives.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 5 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] Test with two very different voice profiles \u2014 outputs should feel noticeably different from each other.\n- [ ] The app works and looks good on a mobile screen (375px width).\n- [ ] Loading spinner appears while generating and disappears when done.\n- [ ] A simulated API failure (wrong key) shows a readable error message \u2014 not a blank screen or console error.\n- [ ] Ask a friend or colleague to use the app without guidance \u2014 they can complete the full workflow without help.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 6 \u2014 Deploy, README, Demo Video & Submit\n\n**Goal:** Everything is live, documented, and submitted before the deadline.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. **Final deploy to Vercel:**\n   - Add `GEMINI_API_KEY` to Vercel environment variables (Settings \u2192 Environment Variables).\n   - Push final code \u2014 Vercel auto-deploys.\n   - Test the live URL end-to-end in an **incognito browser**.\n\n2. **Write the README** (required \u2014 must cover the Creative Industries theme):\n   ```markdown\n   # EchoDraft\n   ## Problem\n   ## Solution & How It Works\n   ## Challenge Theme: Reimagine Creative Industries with AI\n   ## AI & Technical Approach\n   ## Architecture\n   ## How IBM Bob Was Used\n   ## Tech Stack\n   ## Install & Run Locally\n   ## Live Demo\n   ## Demo Video\n   ```\n\n3. **Record the demo video (\u2264 3 minutes):**\n   - Show the Voice Profile Builder \u2014 paste samples, show the extracted profile.\n   - Show the main generate flow \u2014 paste a pillar piece, click generate, show all 5 outputs.\n   - Highlight one output side-by-side with the raw input to show the voice match.\n   - Upload to YouTube (unlisted is fine) or Loom.\n\n4. **Publish the Project Page** on the challenge platform:\n   - Fill in all required sections (do NOT leave as draft).\n   - Add the GitHub repo link, live demo link, and demo video link.\n\n5. **Final submission checklist:**\n   - [ ] Functioning prototype (core workflow works end-to-end).\n   - [ ] IBM Bob used as primary dev tool (documented in README).\n   - [ ] AI is a central, user-facing function.\n   - [ ] IBM SkillsBuild certificate completed and saved.\n   - [ ] Public GitHub repo with full README.\n   - [ ] Public demo video (\u2264 3 minutes).\n   - [ ] Project Page published (not draft), all sections complete.\n   - [ ] All links tested in incognito/private browser.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 6 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] The live Vercel URL works end-to-end in incognito (no login, no local env vars).\n- [ ] The GitHub repo is public and the README renders correctly on GitHub.\n- [ ] The demo video link is public and plays without sign-in.\n- [ ] The Project Page is published (not draft) and all links open correctly.\n- [ ] Every item in the submission checklist above is ticked.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference \u2014 File Structure\n\n```\nechodraft/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 app/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 page.tsx                        # Main generate page (home)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 voice-profile/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 page.tsx                    # Voice Profile Builder page\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 api/\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 build-voice-profile/\n\u2502       \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts                # AI: extract voice profile from samples\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 generate/\n\u2502           \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts                # AI: generate all 5 platform outputs\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 components/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 VoiceProfileCard.tsx            # Displays the saved voice profile\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 PlatformOutputCard.tsx          # One result card (text + copy button)\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 Navbar.tsx                      # Top navigation\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 lib/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 gemini.ts                       # Gemini API client helper\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 .env.local                          # API keys (gitignored)\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 README.md\n```\n\n---\n\n## Risk Watch List\n\n| Risk | What to watch for | Action |\n|---|---|---|\n| Bobcoin / trial runs out | Bob stops responding mid-build | Push to GitHub constantly; have a backup email ready |\n| Gemini rate limits during demo | Generation fails mid-video | Pre-run and screenshot all demo examples before recording |\n| Voice match feels generic | Outputs sound like each other | Use a very distinctive sample voice; tighten the voice profile prompt |\n| Scope creep | Running out of time | Ship the 5-platform MVP first \u2014 authenticity check and \"regenerate with tweak\" are bonus only |\n\n---\n\n*Built with IBM Bob \u00b7 Powered by Gemini Flash \u00b7 Hosted on Vercel*\n"
      },
      "isError": false
    },

  "_meta": {
    "changes": {
      "file:///c%3A/Users/linda/skillsbuild/echodraft-build-plan.md": {
        "before": "",
        "after": "# EchoDraft \u2014 Build Plan\n> IBM AI Builders Challenge \u00b7 July 2026 \u00b7 Theme: Reimagine Creative Industries with AI  \n> Deadline: **July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET**\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nEchoDraft is a voice-preserving content repurposing tool. One long-form input \u2192 platform-native posts (LinkedIn, X/Twitter, Instagram, YouTube, Email) that still sound like the creator \u2014 not generic AI.\n\nThis plan breaks the build into **6 phases**, each with concrete steps and a test checkpoint to confirm it works before moving on.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1 \u2014 Project Setup & Scaffold\n\n**Goal:** A running Next.js app deployed to Vercel with a public GitHub repo.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. Register for the IBM AI Builders Challenge platform and start the IBM Bob 30-day free trial.\n2. Complete at least one IBM SkillsBuild activity and save the certificate (required for submission).\n3. Create a new **public** GitHub repository named `echodraft`.\n4. Scaffold the project locally:\n   ```bash\n   npx create-next-app@latest echodraft --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app\n   cd echodraft\n   ```\n5. Push the initial scaffold to GitHub:\n   ```bash\n   git add .\n   git commit -m \"chore: scaffold Next.js project\"\n   git push origin main\n   ```\n6. Connect the GitHub repo to **Vercel** (import project \u2192 auto-deploy).\n7. Create a `.env.local` file for API keys (add to `.gitignore`):\n   ```\n   GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here\n   ```\n8. Get a free **Gemini Flash API key** from [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/) \u2014 no credit card required.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 1 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] `npm run dev` starts the app at `http://localhost:3000` with no errors.\n- [ ] The Vercel dashboard shows a live public URL (e.g. `echodraft.vercel.app`).\n- [ ] The GitHub repo is public and shows the scaffold commit.\n- [ ] `.env.local` is listed in `.gitignore` (API key is NOT committed).\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2 \u2014 Voice Profile Builder UI\n\n**Goal:** A page where the user pastes 3\u20135 writing samples, submits them, and receives a saved `voiceProfile` JSON object extracted by AI.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. Create the page route `app/voice-profile/page.tsx`.\n2. Build a form UI with:\n   - A title: \"Build Your Voice Profile\"\n   - A `<textarea>` for Sample 1, with an \"Add another sample\" button that appends up to 5 textarea fields.\n   - A \"Build My Voice Profile\" submit button.\n3. Create an API route `app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts`.\n4. In the API route, send the samples to the Gemini Flash API with a prompt like:\n   ```\n   Analyze these writing samples and extract a voice profile as JSON with these fields:\n   - tone (e.g. \"conversational, direct, warm\")\n   - vocabulary (e.g. \"plain English, avoids jargon, uses analogies\")\n   - sentence_rhythm (e.g. \"short punchy sentences, varies length\")\n   - dos (array of style traits to keep)\n   - donts (array of things to avoid)\n   \n   Samples:\n   {samples}\n   ```\n5. Return the extracted `voiceProfile` JSON to the frontend.\n6. On success, save the `voiceProfile` to `localStorage` under the key `echodraft_voice_profile`.\n7. Display the saved profile on screen in a readable card format (tone, vocabulary, rhythm, dos, don'ts).\n\n### \u2705 Phase 2 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] Pasting 3 writing samples and clicking \"Build My Voice Profile\" returns a populated JSON profile.\n- [ ] The profile card renders correctly on screen with all fields visible.\n- [ ] Refreshing the page still shows the saved profile (loaded from `localStorage`).\n- [ ] The API route returns a `400` error gracefully if no samples are submitted.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 3 \u2014 Pillar Input & First Platform Generation (LinkedIn)\n\n**Goal:** A page where the user pastes their pillar content and generates a single LinkedIn post that matches their voice profile.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. Create the main page at `app/page.tsx` (home route).\n2. Build the UI with:\n   - A `<textarea>` labeled \"Paste your pillar content\" (blog post, transcript, or long caption).\n   - A \"Generate Posts\" button.\n   - A results area (initially empty).\n3. On submit, load the `voiceProfile` from `localStorage`.\n4. Create an API route `app/api/generate/route.ts`.\n5. Start with **LinkedIn only** \u2014 send this prompt to Gemini Flash:\n   ```\n   You are rewriting content for a creator. Use the voice profile below to match their writing style exactly.\n   \n   Voice Profile:\n   {voiceProfile}\n   \n   Platform: LinkedIn\n   Rules: Professional but human tone. 1200\u20131500 characters. Strong opening hook. \n   End with a question or call to action. Use line breaks for readability. No hashtag spam (max 3).\n   \n   Pillar Content:\n   {pillarText}\n   \n   Write the LinkedIn post now:\n   ```\n6. Display the generated LinkedIn post in a result card with a \"Copy\" button.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 3 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] Pasting a blog post and clicking \"Generate Posts\" returns a LinkedIn post.\n- [ ] The output reads differently from the raw input (it's adapted, not copy-pasted).\n- [ ] The \"Copy\" button copies the text to the clipboard.\n- [ ] If no voice profile is saved, the user sees a prompt: \"Build your voice profile first.\"\n- [ ] If the pillar input is empty, the button is disabled or shows a validation message.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 4 \u2014 All 5 Platforms\n\n**Goal:** Extend the generation engine to produce all 5 platform-native outputs in one click.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. Update the API route `app/api/generate/route.ts` to run 5 parallel generation calls (or one batch call) for:\n   - **LinkedIn** \u2014 (already done in Phase 3)\n   - **X / Twitter Thread** \u2014 3\u20135 tweets, each under 280 chars, strong first tweet as hook, number each tweet (1/5, 2/5\u2026)\n   - **Instagram Caption** \u2014 conversational, 150\u2013300 words, hook in first line, relevant emojis, 5\u201310 hashtags at the end\n   - **YouTube Description** \u2014 200\u2013300 words, keyword-rich first paragraph, timestamps placeholder, links section\n   - **Email Newsletter Blurb** \u2014 100\u2013150 words, subject line suggestion, personal opener, one clear CTA\n2. Each platform prompt must include the `voiceProfile` to enforce voice matching.\n3. Update the results UI to show 5 cards \u2014 one per platform \u2014 each with:\n   - Platform name + icon\n   - Generated text\n   - \"Copy\" button\n4. Add an **\"Export All\"** button that downloads a `.txt` file containing all 5 outputs.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 4 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] One click produces all 5 platform outputs simultaneously.\n- [ ] Each output is visibly different in length, tone, and structure from the others.\n- [ ] Twitter/X output is split into numbered tweets, each \u2264 280 characters.\n- [ ] Instagram output ends with hashtags.\n- [ ] YouTube output has a keyword-rich first paragraph.\n- [ ] \"Export All\" downloads a `.txt` file with all 5 outputs clearly labeled.\n- [ ] All 5 \"Copy\" buttons work correctly.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 5 \u2014 Voice Match Refinement, Styling & Polish\n\n**Goal:** The app looks good, voice matching is noticeably effective, and the UX is smooth enough to demo.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. **Voice match tuning** \u2014 test with a distinctive voice (e.g. very casual/Gen-Z, or very formal/academic). Adjust prompts if outputs feel generic.\n2. **Navigation** \u2014 add a simple top nav with links: \"Home (Generate)\" and \"Voice Profile.\"\n3. **Loading states** \u2014 show a spinner or skeleton while AI is generating.\n4. **Error states** \u2014 show a user-friendly message if the API call fails (e.g. rate limit hit).\n5. **Mobile responsiveness** \u2014 ensure the layout works on a phone screen (Tailwind responsive classes).\n6. **Nice-to-have (if time):** Add a \"Regenerate with tweak\" input per card \u2014 a small text field where the user types \"make it shorter\" and re-runs just that platform's generation.\n7. **Nice-to-have (if time):** Authenticity check \u2014 after generation, run a second AI call that flags any phrases that sound \"generic AI\" and suggests alternatives.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 5 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] Test with two very different voice profiles \u2014 outputs should feel noticeably different from each other.\n- [ ] The app works and looks good on a mobile screen (375px width).\n- [ ] Loading spinner appears while generating and disappears when done.\n- [ ] A simulated API failure (wrong key) shows a readable error message \u2014 not a blank screen or console error.\n- [ ] Ask a friend or colleague to use the app without guidance \u2014 they can complete the full workflow without help.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 6 \u2014 Deploy, README, Demo Video & Submit\n\n**Goal:** Everything is live, documented, and submitted before the deadline.\n\n### Steps\n\n1. **Final deploy to Vercel:**\n   - Add `GEMINI_API_KEY` to Vercel environment variables (Settings \u2192 Environment Variables).\n   - Push final code \u2014 Vercel auto-deploys.\n   - Test the live URL end-to-end in an **incognito browser**.\n\n2. **Write the README** (required \u2014 must cover the Creative Industries theme):\n   ```markdown\n   # EchoDraft\n   ## Problem\n   ## Solution & How It Works\n   ## Challenge Theme: Reimagine Creative Industries with AI\n   ## AI & Technical Approach\n   ## Architecture\n   ## How IBM Bob Was Used\n   ## Tech Stack\n   ## Install & Run Locally\n   ## Live Demo\n   ## Demo Video\n   ```\n\n3. **Record the demo video (\u2264 3 minutes):**\n   - Show the Voice Profile Builder \u2014 paste samples, show the extracted profile.\n   - Show the main generate flow \u2014 paste a pillar piece, click generate, show all 5 outputs.\n   - Highlight one output side-by-side with the raw input to show the voice match.\n   - Upload to YouTube (unlisted is fine) or Loom.\n\n4. **Publish the Project Page** on the challenge platform:\n   - Fill in all required sections (do NOT leave as draft).\n   - Add the GitHub repo link, live demo link, and demo video link.\n\n5. **Final submission checklist:**\n   - [ ] Functioning prototype (core workflow works end-to-end).\n   - [ ] IBM Bob used as primary dev tool (documented in README).\n   - [ ] AI is a central, user-facing function.\n   - [ ] IBM SkillsBuild certificate completed and saved.\n   - [ ] Public GitHub repo with full README.\n   - [ ] Public demo video (\u2264 3 minutes).\n   - [ ] Project Page published (not draft), all sections complete.\n   - [ ] All links tested in incognito/private browser.\n\n### \u2705 Phase 6 Test Checkpoint\n\n- [ ] The live Vercel URL works end-to-end in incognito (no login, no local env vars).\n- [ ] The GitHub repo is public and the README renders correctly on GitHub.\n- [ ] The demo video link is public and plays without sign-in.\n- [ ] The Project Page is published (not draft) and all links open correctly.\n- [ ] Every item in the submission checklist above is ticked.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Reference \u2014 File Structure\n\n```\nechodraft/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 app/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 page.tsx                        # Main generate page (home)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 voice-profile/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 page.tsx                    # Voice Profile Builder page\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 api/\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 build-voice-profile/\n\u2502       \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts                # AI: extract voice profile from samples\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 generate/\n\u2502           \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts                # AI: generate all 5 platform outputs\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 components/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 VoiceProfileCard.tsx            # Displays the saved voice profile\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 PlatformOutputCard.tsx          # One result card (text + copy button)\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 Navbar.tsx                      # Top navigation\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 lib/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 gemini.ts                       # Gemini API client helper\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 .env.local                          # API keys (gitignored)\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 README.md\n```\n\n---\n\n## Risk Watch List\n\n| Risk | What to watch for | Action |\n|---|---|---|\n| Bobcoin / trial runs out | Bob stops responding mid-build | Push to GitHub constantly; have a backup email ready |\n| Gemini rate limits during demo | Generation fails mid-video | Pre-run and screenshot all demo examples before recording |\n| Voice match feels generic | Outputs sound like each other | Use a very distinctive sample voice; tighten the voice profile prompt |\n| Scope creep | Running out of time | Ship the 5-platform MVP first \u2014 authenticity check and \"regenerate with tweak\" are bonus only |\n\n---\n\n*Built with IBM Bob \u00b7 Powered by Gemini Flash \u00b7 Hosted on Vercel*\n",

One long-form input \u2192 platform-native posts (LinkedIn, X/Twitter, Instagram, YouTube, Email) that still sound like the creator \u2014 not generic AI.\n+\n+This plan breaks the build into **6 phases**, each with concrete steps and a test checkpoint to confirm it works before moving on.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Phase 1 \u2014 Project Setup & Scaffold\n+\n+**Goal:** A running Next.js app deployed to Vercel with a public GitHub repo.\n+\n+### Steps\n+\n+1. Get a free **Gemini Flash API key** from [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/) \u2014 no credit card required.\n+\n+### \u2705 Phase 1 Test Checkpoint\n+\n+- [ ] `npm run dev` starts the app at `http://localhost:3000` with no errors.\n+- [ ] The Vercel dashboard shows a live public URL (e.g. `echodraft.vercel.app`).\n+- [ ] The GitHub repo is public and shows the scaffold commit.\n+- [ ] `.env.local` is listed in `.gitignore` (API key is NOT committed).\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Phase 2 \u2014 Voice Profile Builder UI\n+\n+**Goal:** A page where the user pastes 3\u20135 writing samples, submits them, and receives a saved `voiceProfile` JSON object extracted by AI.\n+\n+### Steps\n+\n+1. Create the page route `app/voice-profile/page.tsx`.\n+2. Build a form UI with:\n+   - A title: \"Build Your Voice Profile\"\n+   - A `<textarea>` for Sample 1, with an \"Add another sample\" button that appends up to 5 textarea fields.\n+   - A \"Build My Voice Profile\" submit button.\n+3. Return the extracted `voiceProfile` JSON to the frontend.\n+6. On success, save the `voiceProfile` to `localStorage` under the key `echodraft_voice_profile`.\n+7. Display the saved profile on screen in a readable card format (tone, vocabulary, rhythm, dos, don'ts).\n+\n+### \u2705 Phase 2 Test Checkpoint\n+\n+- [ ] Pasting 3 writing samples and clicking \"Build My Voice Profile\" returns a populated JSON profile.\n+- [ ] The profile card renders correctly on screen with all fields visible.\n+- [ ] Refreshing the page still shows the saved profile (loaded from `localStorage`).\n+- [ ] The API route returns a `400` error gracefully if no samples are submitted.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Phase 3 \u2014 Pillar Input & First Platform Generation (LinkedIn)\n+\n+**Goal:** A page where the user pastes their pillar content and generates a single LinkedIn post that matches their voice profile.\n+\n+### Steps\n+\n+1. Create the main page at `app/page.tsx` (home route).\n+2. Build the UI with:\n+   - A `<textarea>` labeled \"Paste your pillar content\" (blog post, transcript, or long caption).\n+   - A \"Generate Posts\" button.\n+   - A results area (initially empty).\n+3. On submit, load the `voiceProfile` from `localStorage`.\n+4. Create an API route `app/api/generate/route.ts`.\n+5. Start with **LinkedIn only** \u2014 send this prompt to Gemini Flash:\n+   ```\n+   You are rewriting content for a creator. Use the voice profile below to match their writing style exactly.\n+   \n+   Voice Profile:\n+   {voiceProfile}\n+   \n+   Platform: LinkedIn\n+   Rules: Professional but human tone. 1200\u20131500 characters. Strong opening hook. \n+   End with a question or call to action. Use line breaks for readability. No hashtag spam (max 3).\n+   \n+   Pillar Content:\n+   {pillarText}\n+   \n+   Write the LinkedIn post now:\n+   ```\n+6. Display the generated LinkedIn post in a result card with a \"Copy\" button.\n+\n+### \u2705 Phase 3 Test Checkpoint\n+\n+- [ ] Pasting a blog post and clicking \"Generate Posts\" returns a LinkedIn post.\n+- [ ] The output reads differently from the raw input (it's adapted, not copy-pasted).\n+- [ ] The \"Copy\" button copies the text to the clipboard.\n+- [ ] If no voice profile is saved, the user sees a prompt: \"Build your voice profile first.\"\n+- [ ] If the pillar input is empty, the button is disabled or shows a validation message.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Phase 4 \u2014 All 5 Platforms\n+\n+**Goal:** Extend the generation engine to produce all 5 platform-native outputs in one click.\n+\n+### Steps\n+\n+1. Update the API route `app/api/generate/route.ts` to run 5 parallel generation calls (or one batch call) for:\n+   - **LinkedIn** \u2014 (already done in Phase 3)\n+   - **X / Twitter Thread** \u2014 3\u20135 tweets, each under 280 chars, strong first tweet as hook, number each tweet (1/5, 2/5\u2026)\n+   - **Instagram Caption** \u2014 conversational, 150\u2013300 words, hook in first line, relevant emojis, 5\u201310 hashtags at the end\n+   - **YouTube Description** \u2014 200\u2013300 words, keyword-rich first paragraph, timestamps placeholder, links section\n+   - **Email Newsletter Blurb** \u2014 100\u2013150 words, subject line suggestion, personal opener, one clear CTA\n+2. Each platform prompt must include the `voiceProfile` to enforce voice matching.\n+3. Update the results UI to show 5 cards \u2014 one per platform \u2014 each with:\n+   - Platform name + icon\n+   - Generated text\n+   - \"Copy\" button\n+4. Add an **\"Export All\"** button that downloads a `.txt` file containing all 5 outputs.\n+\n+### \u2705 Phase 4 Test Checkpoint\n+\n+- [ ] One click produces all 5 platform outputs simultaneously.\n+- [ ] Each output is visibly different in length, tone, and structure from the others.\n+- [ ] Twitter/X output is split into numbered tweets, each \u2264 280 characters.\n+- [ ] Instagram output ends with hashtags.\n+- [ ] YouTube output has a keyword-rich first paragraph.\n+- [ ] \"Export All\" downloads a `.txt` file with all 5 outputs clearly labeled.\n+- [ ] All 5 \"Copy\" buttons work correctly.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Phase 5 \u2014 Voice Match Refinement, Styling & Polish\n+\n+**Goal:** The app looks good, voice matching is noticeably effective, and the UX is smooth enough to demo.\n+\n+### Steps\n+\n+1. **Voice match tuning** \u2014 test with a distinctive voice (e.g. very casual/Gen-Z, or very formal/academic). Adjust prompts if outputs feel generic.\n+2. **Navigation** \u2014 add a simple top nav with links: \"Home (Generate)\" and \"Voice Profile.\"\n+3. **Loading states** \u2014 show a spinner or skeleton while AI is generating.\n+4. **Error states** \u2014 show a user-friendly message if the API call fails (e.g. rate limit hit).\n+5. **Mobile responsiveness** \u2014 ensure the layout works on a phone screen (Tailwind responsive classes).\n+6. **Nice-to-have (if time):** Add a \"Regenerate with tweak\" input per card \u2014 a small text field where the user types \"make it shorter\" and re-runs just that platform's generation.\n+7. **Nice-to-have (if time):** Authenticity check \u2014 after generation, run a second AI call that flags any phrases that sound \"generic AI\" and suggests alternatives.\n+\n+### \u2705 Phase 5 Test Checkpoint\n+\n+- [ ] Test with two very different voice profiles \u2014 outputs should feel noticeably different from each other.\n+- [ ] The app works and looks good on a mobile screen (375px width).\n+- [ ] Loading spinner appears while generating and disappears when done.\n+- [ ] A simulated API failure (wrong key) shows a readable error message \u2014 not a blank screen or console error.\n+- [ ] Ask a friend or colleague to use the app without guidance \u2014 they can complete the full workflow without help.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Phase 6 \u2014 Deploy, README, Demo Video & Submit\n+\n+**Goal:** Everything is live, documented, and submitted before the deadline.\n+\n+### Steps\n+\n+1. **Final deploy to Vercel:**\n+   - Add `GEMINI_API_KEY` to Vercel environment variables (Settings \u2192 Environment Variables).\n+   - Push final code \u2014 Vercel auto-deploys.\n+   - Test the live URL end-to-end in an **incognito browser**.\n+\n+2. **Write the README** (required \u2014 must cover the Creative Industries theme):\n+   ```markdown\n+   # EchoDraft\n+   ## Problem\n+   ## Solution & How It Works\n+   ## Challenge Theme: Reimagine Creative Industries with AI\n+   ## AI & Technical Approach\n+   ## Architecture\n+   ## How IBM Bob Was Used\n+   ## Tech Stack\n+   ## Install & Run Locally\n+   ## Live Demo\n+   ## Demo Video\n+   ```\n+\n+3. **Record the demo video (\u2264 3 minutes):**\n+   - Show the Voice Profile Builder \u2014 paste samples, show the extracted profile.\n+   - Show the main generate flow \u2014 paste a pillar piece, click generate, show all 5 outputs.\n+   - Highlight one output side-by-side with the raw input to show the voice match.\n+   - Upload to YouTube (unlisted is fine) or Loom.\n+\n+4. **Publish the Project Page** on the challenge platform:\n+   - Fill in all required sections (do NOT leave as draft).\n+   - Add the GitHub repo link, live demo link, and demo video link.\n+\n+5. **Final submission checklist:**\n+   - [ ] Functioning prototype (core workflow works end-to-end).\n+   - [ ] IBM Bob used as primary dev tool (documented in README).\n+   - [ ] AI is a central, user-facing function.\n+   - [ ] IBM SkillsBuild certificate completed and saved.\n+   - [ ] Public GitHub repo with full README.\n+   - [ ] Public demo video (\u2264 3 minutes).\n+   - [ ] Project Page published (not draft), all sections complete.\n+   - [ ] All links tested in incognito/private browser.\n+\n+### \u2705 Phase 6 Test Checkpoint\n+\n+- [ ] The live Vercel URL works end-to-end in incognito (no login, no local env vars).\n+- [ ] The GitHub repo is public and the README renders correctly on GitHub.\n+- [ ] The demo video link is public and plays without sign-in.\n+- [ ] The Project Page is published (not draft) and all links open correctly.\n+- [ ] Every item in the submission checklist above is ticked.\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Quick Reference \u2014 File Structure\n+\n+```\n+echodraft/\n+\u251c\u2500\u2500 app/\n+\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 page.tsx                        # Main generate page (home)\n+\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 voice-profile/\n+\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 page.tsx                    # Voice Profile Builder page\n+\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 api/\n+\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 build-voice-profile/\n+\u2502       \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts                # AI: extract voice profile from samples\n+\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 generate/\n+\u2502           \u2514\u2500\u2500 route.ts                # AI: generate all 5 platform outputs\n+\u251c\u2500\u2500 components/\n+\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 VoiceProfileCard.tsx            # Displays the saved voice profile\n+\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 PlatformOutputCard.tsx          # One result card (text + copy button)\n+\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 Navbar.tsx                      # Top navigation\n+\u251c\u2500\u2500 lib/\n+\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 gemini.ts                       # Gemini API client helper\n+\u251c\u2500\u2500 .env.local                          # API keys (gitignored)\n+\u2514\u2500\u2500 README.md\n+```\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Risk Watch List\n+\n+| Risk | What to watch for | Action |\n+|---|---|---|\n+| Bobcoin / trial runs out | Bob stops responding mid-build | Push to GitHub constantly; have a backup email ready |\n+| Gemini rate limits during demo | Generation fails mid-video | Pre-run and screenshot all demo examples before recording |\n+| Voice match feels generic | Outputs sound like each other | Use a very distinctive sample voice; tighten the voice profile prompt |\n+| Scope creep | Running out of time | Ship the 5-platform MVP first \u2014 authenticity check and \"regenerate with tweak\" are bonus only |\n+\n+---\n+\n+*Built with IBM Bob \u00b7 Powered by Gemini Flash \u00b7 Hosted on Vercel*\n"
      }
    },

  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/lib/gemini.ts:\n\n1 | const GEMINI_API_URL =\n2 |   \"https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash:generateContent\";\n3 | \n4 | export type VoiceProfile = {\n5 |   tone: string;\n6 |   vocabulary: string;\n7 |   sentence_rhythm: string;\n8 |   dos: string[];\n9 |   donts: string[];\n10 | };\n11 | \n12 | export function buildVoiceProfilePrompt(samples: string[]): string {\n13 |   return `Analyze these writing samples and extract a voice profile as a JSON object with exactly these fields:\n14 | - tone (string, e.g. \"conversational, direct, warm\")\n15 | - vocabulary (string, e.g. \"plain English, avoids jargon, uses analogies\")\n16 | - sentence_rhythm (string, e.g. \"short punchy sentences, varies length\")\n17 | - dos (array of strings, style traits to keep)\n18 | - donts (array of strings, things to avoid)\n19 | \n20 | Be specific and concise. Return only valid JSON. Do not wrap it in markdown code fences.\n21 | \n22 | Samples:\n23 | ${samples.map((s, i) => `--- Sample ${i + 1} ---\\n${s}`).join(\"\\n\\n\")}`;\n24 | }\n25 | \n26 | export function extractJsonFromText(text: string): unknown {\n27 |   // Try to find a JSON object inside the text if the model adds extra prose\n28 |   const firstBrace = text.indexOf(\"{\");\n29 |   const lastBrace = text.lastIndexOf(\"}\");\n30 |   if (firstBrace !== -1 && lastBrace !== -1 && lastBrace > firstBrace) {\n31 |     const candidate = text.slice(firstBrace, lastBrace + 1);\n32 |     try {\n33 |       return JSON.parse(candidate);\n34 |     } catch {\n35 |       // fall through to raw parse\n36 |     }\n37 |   }\n38 |   return JSON.parse(text);\n39 | }\n40 | \n41 | export async function callGeminiForVoiceProfile(\n42 |   samples: string[],\n43 |   apiKey: string | undefined\n44 | ): Promise<VoiceProfile> {\n45 |   if (!apiKey) {\n46 |     return mockVoiceProfile(samples);\n47 |   }\n48 | \n49 |   const prompt = buildVoiceProfilePrompt(samples);\n50 |   const res = await fetch(`${GEMINI_API_URL}?key=${apiKey}`, {\n51 |     method: \"POST\",\n52 |     headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n53 |     body: JSON.stringify({\n54 |       contents: [{ parts: [{ text: prompt }] }],\n55 |       generationConfig: {\n56 |         responseMimeType: \"application/json\",\n57 |         temperature: 0.4,\n58 |       },\n59 |     }),\n60 |   });\n61 | \n62 |   if (!res.ok) {\n63 |     const text = await res.text();\n64 |     throw new Error(`Gemini API error (${res.status}): ${text.slice(0, 200)}`);\n65 |   }\n66 | \n67 |   const data = await res.json();\n68 |   const text = data?.candidates?.[0]?.content?.parts?.[0]?.text ?? \"\";\n69 |   if (!text) {\n70 |     throw new Error(\"Gemini returned an empty response.\");\n71 |   }\n72 | \n73 |   const parsed = extractJsonFromText(text) as Partial<VoiceProfile>;\n74 |   return normalizeVoiceProfile(parsed, samples);\n75 | }\n76 | \n77 | function normalizeVoiceProfile(parsed: Partial<VoiceProfile>, samples: string[]): VoiceProfile {\n78 |   const safeString = (val: unknown): string =>\n79 |     typeof val === \"string\" ? val : Array.isArray(val) ? val.join(\" \") : \"\";\n80 | \n81 |   const safeArray = (val: unknown): string[] => {\n82 |     if (Array.isArray(val)) return val.filter((v) => typeof v === \"string\") as string[];\n83 |     if (typeof val === \"string\") return [val];\n84 |     return [];\n85 |   };\n86 | \n87 |   return {\n88 |     tone: safeString(parsed.tone) || \"Conversational and authentic\",\n89 |     vocabulary: safeString(parsed.vocabulary) || \"Plain, accessible language\",\n90 |     sentence_rhythm:\n91 |       safeString(parsed.sentence_rhythm) || \"Mixed sentence length with natural pauses\",\n92 |     dos: safeArray(parsed.dos).length ? safeArray(parsed.dos) : [\"Keep it personal\", \"Use concrete examples\"],\n93 |     donts: safeArray(parsed.donts).length\n94 |       ? safeArray(parsed.donts)\n95 |       : [\"Avoid generic buzzwords\", \"Don\u2019t over-polish\"],\n96 |   };\n97 | }\n98 | \n99 | function mockVoiceProfile(samples: string[]): VoiceProfile {\n100 |   const totalLength = samples.reduce((acc, s) => acc + s.length, 0);\n101 |   const avgLength = totalLength / samples.length;\n102 |   const hasQuestions = samples.some((s) => s.includes(\"?\"));\n103 | \n104 |   return {\n105 |     tone: hasQuestions\n106 |       ? \"Conversational, curious, and warm \u2014 asks the reader to think along\"\n107 |       : \"Direct, clear, and confident \u2014 gets to the point without fluff\",\n108 |     vocabulary:\n109 |       avgLength > 400\n110 |         ? \"Detailed vocabulary with full sentences; prefers explanation over shorthand\"\n111 |         : \"Plain, punchy words; favors clarity over jargon\",\n112 |     sentence_rhythm:\n113 |       avgLength > 400\n114 |         ? \"Longer, flowing sentences with occasional short breaks for emphasis\"\n115 |         : \"Short to medium sentences with natural line breaks for readability\",\n116 |     dos: [\n117 |       \"Lead with the main point\",\n118 |       \"Use the same everyday words found in your samples\",\n119 |       \"Write like you're talking to one person\",\n120 |     ],\n121 |     donts: [\n122 |       \"Don't switch to corporate-speak\",\n123 |       \"Don't pad the message with filler\",\n124 |       \"Don't lose the warm, human edge\",\n125 |     ],\n126 |   };\n127 | }\n128 | ",
