\"@/components/VoiceProfileCard\";\n5 | import { FiPlus, FiTrash2, FiRefreshCw, FiAlertCircle, FiCheck } from \"react-icons/fi\";\n6 | \n7 | type VoiceProfile = {\n8 |   tone: string;\n9 |   vocabulary: string;\n10 |   sentence_rhythm: string;\n11 |   dos: string[];\n12 |   donts: string[];\n13 | };\n14 | \n15 | const STORAGE_KEY = \"echodraft_voice_profile\";\n16 | \n17 | export default function VoiceProfilePage() {\n18 |   const [samples, setSamples] = useState<string[]>([\"\"]);\n19 |   const [profile, setProfile] = useState<VoiceProfile | null>(null);\n20 |   const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n21 |   const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n22 | \n23 |   // Load saved profile on mount\n24 |   useEffect(() => {\n25 |     try {\n26 |       const saved = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY);\n27 |       if (saved) setProfile(JSON.parse(saved));\n28 |     } catch {\n29 |       // ignore corrupted localStorage\n30 |     }\n31 |   }, []);\n32 | \n33 |   const updateSample = (index: number, value: string) => {\n34 |     const next = [...samples];\n35 |     next[index] = value;\n36 |     setSamples(next);\n37 |   };\n38 | \n39 |   const addSample = () => {\n40 |     if (samples.length < 5) setSamples([...samples, \"\"]);\n41 |   };\n42 | \n43 |   const removeSample = (index: number) => {\n44 |     if (samples.length <= 1) return;\n45 |     const next = samples.filter((_, i) => i !== index);\n46 |     setSamples(next);\n47 |   };\n48 | \n49 |   const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n50 |     e.preventDefault();\n51 |     setError(null);\n52 | \n53 |     const nonEmpty = samples.map((s) => s.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n54 |     if (nonEmpty.length < 3) {\n55 |       setError(\"Please paste at least 3 writing samples so we can capture your voice.\");\n56 |       return;\n57 |     }\n58 | \n59 |     setLoading(true);\n60 |     try {\n61 |       const res = await fetch(\"/api/build-voice-profile\", {\n62 |         method: \"POST\",\n63 |         headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n64 |         body: JSON.stringify({ samples: nonEmpty }),\n65 |       });\n66 | \n67 |       const data = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));\n68 |       if (!res.ok) {\n69 |         throw new Error(data.error || `Request failed with status ${res.status}`);\n70 |       }\n71 | \n72 |       if (!data.voiceProfile) {\n73 |         throw new Error(\"The AI returned an unexpected shape. Please try again.\");\n74 |       }\n75 | \n76 |       localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(data.voiceProfile));\n77 |       setProfile(data.voiceProfile);\n78 |     } catch (err) {\n79 |       setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n80 |     } finally {\n81 |       setLoading(false);\n82 |     }\n83 |   };\n84 | \n85 |   const clearProfile = () => {\n86 |     localStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY);\n87 |     setProfile(null);\n88 |     setSamples([\"\"]);\n89 |   };\n90 | \n91 |   return (\n92 |     <div className=\"max-w-3xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 py-10 sm:py-16\">\n93 |       <div className=\"mb-8\">\n94 |         <h1 className=\"text-3xl sm:text-4xl font-bold text-primary-text tracking-tight mb-3\">\n95 |           Build Your Voice Profile\n96 |         </h1>\n97 |         <p className=\"text-secondary-text text-lg leading-relaxed\">\n98 |           Paste 3\u20135 samples of your writing. EchoDraft will extract the tone, vocabulary, and rhythm that make your voice yours.\n99 |         </p>\n100 |       </div>\n101 | \n102 |       <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"bg-canvas rounded-xl border border-border shadow-sm p-6 sm:p-8\">\n103 |         <div className=\"space-y-4\">\n104 |           {samples.map((sample, index) => (\n105 |             <div key={index}>\n106 |               <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between mb-2\">\n107 |                 <label htmlFor={`sample-${index}`} className=\"text-sm font-semibold text-primary-text\">\n108 |                   Sample {index + 1}\n109 |                 </label>\n110 |                 {samples.length > 1 && (\n111 |                   <button\n112 |                     type=\"button\"\n113 |                     onClick={() => removeSample(index)}\n114 |                     className=\"text-secondary-text hover:text-accent-red transition-colors\"\n115 |                     aria-label={`Remove sample ${index + 1}`}\n116 |                   >\n117 |                     <FiTrash2 className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n118 |                   </button>\n119 |                 )}\n120 |               </div>\n121 |               <textarea\n122 |                 id={`sample-${index}`}\n123 |                 value={sample}\n124 |                 onChange={(e) => updateSample(index, e.target.value)}\n125 |                 placeholder=\"Paste a paragraph you\u2019ve written \u2014 a post, email, or caption...\"\n126 |                 className=\"w-full min-h-[140px] p-4 rounded-lg bg-surface border border-border text-primary-text placeholder:text-secondary-text focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent-blue focus:border-transparent resize-y transition-shadow\"\n127 |               />\n128 |             </div>\n129 |           ))}\n130 |         </div>\n131 | \n132 |         <div className=\"flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-start sm:items-center justify-between gap-4 mt-6\">\n133 |           <button\n134 |             type=\"button\"\n135 |             onClick={addSample}\n136 |             disabled={samples.length >= 5}\n137 |             className=\"inline-flex items-center gap-2 text-sm font-medium text-accent-blue hover:text-accent-blue/80 disabled:text-secondary-text disabled:cursor-not-allowed transition-colors\"\n138 |           >\n139 |             <FiPlus className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n140 |             Add another sample {samples.length >= 5 && \"(max 5)\"}\n141 |           </button>\n142 | \n143 |           <button\n144 |             type=\"submit\"\n145 |             disabled={loading}\n146 |             className=\"inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 bg-accent-blue text-white font-semibold px-6 py-3 rounded-lg hover:bg-accent-blue/90 disabled:opacity-60 disabled:cursor-not-allowed transition-colors min-w-[200px]\"\n147 |           >\n148 |             {loading ? (\n149 |               <>\n150 |                 <FiRefreshCw className=\"w-4 h-4 animate-spin\" />\n151 |                 Analyzing\u2026\n152 |               </>\n153 |             ) : (\n154 |               \"Build My Voice Profile\"\n155 |             )}\n156 |           </button>\n157 |         </div>\n158 | \n159 |         {error && (\n160 |           <div className=\"mt-6 flex items-start gap-3 rounded-lg bg-accent-red-soft p-4 text-accent-red\">\n161 |             <FiAlertCircle className=\"w-5 h-5 shrink-0 mt-0.5\" />\n162 |             <p className=\"text-sm\">{error}</p>\n163 |           </div>\n164 |         )}\n165 |       </form>\n166 | \n167 |       {profile && (\n168 |         <>\n169 |           <VoiceProfileCard profile={profile} />\n170 |           <div className=\"mt-6 flex items-center justify-between text-sm\">\n171 |             <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 text-accent-green\">\n172 |               <FiCheck className=\"w-4 h-4\" />\n173 |               <span>Saved to localStorage</span>\n174 |             </div>\n175 |             <button\n176 |               onClick={clearProfile}\n177 |               className=\"text-secondary-text hover:text-accent-red transition-colors\"\n178 |             >\n179 |               Clear profile\n180 |             </button>\n181 |           </div>\n182 |         </>\n183 |       )}\n184 |     </div>\n185 |   );\n186 | }\n187 | ",

  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/run-phase2-tests.ts:\n\n1 | import { callGeminiForVoiceProfile } from \"./lib/gemini\";\n2 | \n3 | const samples = [\n4 |   \"Hey friends \u2014 I\u2019ve been thinking about how we all pretend to have it together online. The truth? Most of us are winging it. And that\u2019s okay.\",\n5 |   \"Three things I learned this week: 1) sleep is non-negotiable, 2) your audience can smell authenticity, and 3) done is better than perfect.\",\n6 |   \"Stop writing like you\u2019re pitching a VC. Write like you\u2019re explaining it to the person next to you at a coffee shop. That\u2019s the post that actually connects.\",\n7 | ];\n8 | \n9 | async function run() {\n10 |   console.log(\"Running Phase 2 tests...\\n\");\n11 | \n12 |   // Test 1: mock profile without API key\n13 |   console.log(\"Test 1: callGeminiForVoiceProfile with no API key returns mock profile\");\n14 |   const profile = await callGeminiForVoiceProfile(samples, undefined);\n15 |   assert(typeof profile.tone === \"string\" && profile.tone.length > 0, \"tone should be a non-empty string\");\n16 |   assert(typeof profile.vocabulary === \"string\" && profile.vocabulary.length > 0, \"vocabulary should be a non-empty string\");\n17 |   assert(typeof profile.sentence_rhythm === \"string\" && profile.sentence_rhythm.length > 0, \"sentence_rhythm should be a non-empty string\");\n18 |   assert(Array.isArray(profile.dos) && profile.dos.length > 0, \"dos should be a non-empty array\");\n19 |   assert(Array.isArray(profile.donts) && profile.donts.length > 0, \"donts should be a non-empty array\");\n20 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n21 | \n22 |   // Test 2: JSON extraction helper\n23 |   console.log(\"Test 2: extractJsonFromText handles markdown wrapping\");\n24 |   const { extractJsonFromText } = await import(\"./lib/gemini\");\n25 |   const wrapped = `Here is the profile:\\n\\n\\`\\`\\`json\\n{\"tone\":\"friendly\",\"vocabulary\":\"simple\",\"sentence_rhythm\":\"short\",\"dos\":[\"be honest\"],\"donts\":[\"be generic\"]}\\n\\`\\`\\``;\n26 |   const extracted = extractJsonFromText(wrapped) as Record<string, unknown>;\n27 |   assert(extracted.tone === \"friendly\", \"extracted tone should be 'friendly'\");\n28 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n29 | \n30 |   // Test 3: validation simulation (same logic as API route)\n31 |   console.log(\"Test 3: API route validation rejects <3 samples\");\n32 |   const badBody = { samples: [\"only one sample\"] };\n33 |   const nonEmpty = Array.isArray(badBody?.samples)\n34 |     ? badBody.samples.filter((s: unknown) => typeof s === \"string\" && s.trim().length > 0)\n35 |     : [];\n36 |   assert(nonEmpty.length < 3, \"should detect fewer than 3 samples\");\n37 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n38 | \n39 |   console.log(\"All Phase 2 logic tests passed.\");\n40 | }\n41 | \n42 | function assert(condition: boolean, message: string) {\n43 |   if (!condition) {\n44 |     console.error(\"Assertion failed:\", message);\n45 |     process.exit(1);\n46 |   }\n47 | }\n48 | \n49 | run().catch((err) => {\n50 |   console.error(\"Test failed:\", err);\n51 |   process.exit(1);\n52 | });\n53 | ",

  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/test-server.js:\n\n1 | // Minimal test server that mimics the Phase 2 API route and serves the preview.\n2 | const http = require(\"http\");\n3 | const fs = require(\"fs\");\n4 | const path = require(\"path\");\n5 | \n6 | const PORT = 3456;\n7 | \n8 | const server = http.createServer((req, res) => {\n9 |   if (req.url === \"/api/build-voice-profile\" && req.method === \"POST\") {\n10 |     let body = \"\";\n11 |     req.on(\"data\", (chunk) => (body += chunk));\n12 |     req.on(\"end\", () => {\n13 |       try {\n14 |         const { samples = [] } = JSON.parse(body);\n15 |         const nonEmpty = samples.filter((s) => typeof s === \"string\" && s.trim().length > 0);\n16 |         if (nonEmpty.length < 3) {\n17 |           res.writeHead(400, { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" });\n18 |           res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: \"Please provide at least 3 writing samples.\" }));\n19 |           return;\n20 |         }\n21 |         res.writeHead(200, { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" });\n22 |         res.end(\n23 |           JSON.stringify({\n24 |             voiceProfile: {\n25 |               tone: \"Conversational, warm, and slightly self-deprecating\",\n26 |               vocabulary: \"Plain English, short words, occasional slang\",\n27 |               sentence_rhythm: \"Short to medium sentences, line breaks for emphasis\",\n28 |               dos: [\"Lead with a personal hook\", \"Use contractions\", \"Ask a question at the end\"],\n29 |               donts: [\"Avoid corporate jargon\", \"Don't over-explain\", \"Don't bury the point\"],\n30 |             },\n31 |           })\n32 |         );\n33 |       } catch (e) {\n34 |         res.writeHead(500, { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" });\n35 |         res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: \"Internal server error\" }));\n36 |       }\n37 |     });\n38 |     return;\n39 |   }\n40 | \n41 |   // Serve the static preview for any other GET request\n42 |   if (req.method === \"GET\") {\n43 |     const filePath = path.join(__dirname, \"design-preview.html\");\n44 |     fs.readFile(filePath, \"utf8\", (err, data) => {\n45 |       if (err) {\n46 |         res.writeHead(500, { \"Content-Type\": \"text/plain\" });\n47 |         res.end(\"Could not load preview.\");\n48 |       } else {\n49 |         res.writeHead(200, { \"Content-Type\": \"text/html\" });\n50 |         res.end(data);\n51 |       }\n52 |     });\n53 |     return;\n54 |   }\n55 | \n56 |   res.writeHead(404, { \"Content-Type\": \"text/plain\" });\n57 |   res.end(\"Not found\");\n58 | });\n59 | \n60 | server.listen(PORT, () => {\n61 |   console.log(`Test server running at http://localhost:${PORT}`);\n62 | });\n63 | ",

        "html": "<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head>\n<meta charset=\"UTF-8\" />\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\" />\n<title>EchoDraft Phase 2 \u2014 Code Quality Analysis</title>\n<style>\n  *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }\n  body {\n    font-family: -apple-system, \"Segoe UI\", system-ui, sans-serif;\n    font-size: 14px;\n    line-height: 1.6;\n    background: #ffffff;\n    color: #1f2328;\n    padding: 32px 16px 48px;\n  }\n  .page { max-width: 760px; margin: 0 auto; }\n\n  h1 { font-size: 22px; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 4px; }\n  .subtitle { color: #57606a; font-size: 13px; margin-bottom: 28px; }\n\n  /* Score bar */\n  .score-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 16px; background: #f7f8fa; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 10px; padding: 16px 20px; margin-bottom: 28px; }\n  .score-circle { width: 64px; height: 64px; border-radius: 50%; border: 4px solid #3b82d4; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0; }\n  .score-num { font-size: 22px; font-weight: 700; color: #3b82d4; }\n  .score-label { font-size: 13px; color: #57606a; }\n  .score-desc { font-size: 13px; color: #1f2328; }\n\n  /* Summary pills */\n  .pills { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 28px; }\n  .pill { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 999px; }\n  .pill-green  { background: #e6f4ea; color: #1a7f37; }\n  .pill-yellow { background: #fff8c5; color: #7d4e00; }\n  .pill-red    { background: #ffebe9; color: #cf222e; }\n  .pill-blue   { background: #ddf4ff; color: #0969da; }\n\n  /* Section */\n  section { margin-bottom: 32px; }\n  h2 { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 12px; padding-bottom: 6px; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; }\n\n  /* Finding cards */\n  .finding { border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 8px; padding: 14px 16px; margin-bottom: 10px; }\n  .finding-header { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 6px; }\n  .badge { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px; flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 1px; text-transform: uppercase; }\n  .badge-bug    { background: #ffebe9; color: #cf222e; }\n  .badge-warn   { background: #fff8c5; color: #7d4e00; }\n  .badge-info   { background: #ddf4ff; color: #0969da; }\n  .badge-good   { background: #e6f4ea; color: #1a7f37; }\n  .finding-title { font-weight: 600; font-size: 13px; }\n  .finding-body { font-size: 13px; color: #1f2328; }\n  .finding-body code { background: #f7f8fa; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; padding: 1px 5px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 12px; font-family: \"Cascadia Code\", \"Fira Mono\", monospace; }\n  .finding-file { font-size: 11px; color: #57606a; margin-top: 6px; font-family: \"Cascadia Code\", \"Fira Mono\", monospace; }\n\n  /* Table */\n  table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 13px; }\n  th { text-align: left; font-weight: 600; color: #57606a; font-size: 12px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; padding: 6px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; }\n  td { padding: 8px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid #f0f0f0; vertical-align: top; }\n  tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }\n  .dim { color: #57606a; }\n\n  footer { margin-top: 48px; padding-top: 12px; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; text-align: center; font-size: 11px; color: #57606a; }\n</style>\n</head>\n<body>\n<div class=\"page\">\n\n  <h1>EchoDraft Phase 2 \u2014 Code Quality Analysis</h1>\n  <p class=\"subtitle\">Early-stage review \u00b7 7 source files \u00b7 Next.js 15 \u00b7 React 19 \u00b7 TypeScript \u00b7 Tailwind \u00b7 Gemini API</p>\n\n  <div class=\"score-row\">\n    <div class=\"score-circle\"><span class=\"score-num\">72</span></div>\n    <div>\n      <div style=\"font-weight:700;font-size:15px;\">Good for early-phase work</div>\n      <div class=\"score-label\">Overall score out of 100</div>\n      <div class=\"score-desc\" style=\"margin-top:4px;\">The foundation is clean and coherent. The one shipped bug (wrong icon name) is already fixed. Main gaps are type duplication, missing input sanitisation, and zero test infrastructure for the UI layer.</div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <div class=\"pills\">\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Clean architecture</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Graceful AI fallback</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Good error UX</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Strict mode on</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-yellow\">\u26a0 Duplicate type definitions</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-yellow\">\u26a0 No input length cap</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-yellow\">\u26a0 No test runner wired up</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-red\">\u2715 API key in fetch URL (log risk)</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-red\">\u2715 localStorage \u2014 no SSR guard</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-blue\">i Phase 3 nav link points to /</span>\n  </div>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 BUGS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udc1b Bugs &amp; Runtime Risks</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-bug\">Fixed</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>FiMic2</code> does not exist in react-icons/fi</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>FiMic2</code> is <code>undefined</code> at runtime \u2014 React throws when trying to render it as a component. Fixed in the current session by renaming to <code>FiMic</code>.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">components/Navbar.tsx \u00b7 line 5 &amp; 19</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-bug\">Risk</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">API key appended as a plain query-string parameter</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>fetch(`${GEMINI_API_URL}?key=${apiKey}`)</code> \u2014 the key appears in browser network logs, server access logs, and any proxy/CDN logs. The API route already runs server-side (no CORS), so the client never sees the key, but server-side fetch logs and error stacks may expose it. Consider using a custom header or moving the key through an environment-only path.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 line 50</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">localStorage read inside <code>useEffect</code> is safe, but write is not guarded</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The <code>useEffect</code> correctly defers the read until the client. However <code>localStorage.setItem</code> on line 76 and <code>clearProfile</code> on line 86 are called directly from event handlers \u2014 fine in a client component, but if this file is ever imported in a server context without <code>\"use client\"</code>, it will crash. The directive is already present, so this is low-risk today, but worth a comment noting the dependency.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 lines 76, 86</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 TYPE SAFETY \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udd37 Type Safety</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>VoiceProfile</code> type is defined twice</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The type is declared in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> (exported) and then copy-pasted verbatim into <code>app/voice-profile/page.tsx</code> (local). If the shape ever changes, only one definition will be updated. Fix: remove the local declaration and import from <code>lib/gemini</code>.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 line 4 \u00b7 app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 line 7 \u00b7 components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx \u00b7 line 1</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>VoiceProfileCard</code> has its own inline <code>VoiceProfile</code> type</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Same issue \u2014 three files each define the same shape. The canonical export in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> should be the single source of truth.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx \u00b7 line 1\u20137</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>extractJsonFromText</code> returns <code>unknown</code> \u2014 cast happens at call site</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The <code>as Partial&lt;VoiceProfile&gt;</code> cast on line 73 of <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> is fine for now because <code>normalizeVoiceProfile</code> defensively handles every field. No immediate risk, but the cast bypasses TypeScript's structural check \u2014 acceptable at this stage.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 line 73</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 SECURITY / INPUT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udd10 Security &amp; Input Validation</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No maximum length enforced on writing samples</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Both the client and API route accept samples of arbitrary length. A user could send megabytes of text to Gemini, causing very large token bills or a 400 from the API. A simple limit (e.g. 2 000 characters per sample) should be enforced server-side in the route and echoed to the user in the UI.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts \u00b7 line 7\u20139 \u00b7 app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 line 53</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No rate-limiting on the API route</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The <code>/api/build-voice-profile</code> endpoint has no rate limiting. Acceptable for a prototype, but should be addressed before any public deployment, especially given the API key cost model.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 CODE ORGANISATION \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\uddc2 Code Organisation</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-good\">Good</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">Clean separation of concerns</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        AI logic lives entirely in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code>, the API boundary is a thin route in <code>app/api/</code>, and the UI components only render data they receive. This is a well-structured pattern that will scale to Phase 3 without restructuring.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts \u00b7 components/</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-good\">Good</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">Mock fallback makes development API-key-free</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>callGeminiForVoiceProfile</code> falls back to <code>mockVoiceProfile()</code> when <code>GEMINI_API_KEY</code> is absent. The mock is even content-aware (checks for question marks, average length). This is a solid DX decision.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 lines 45\u201346, 99\u2013127</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">Nav item \"Generate (Phase 3+)\" links to <code>/</code></span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The placeholder href is <code>/</code> which is the home page. Clicking it gives no \"coming soon\" feedback and is easily confused with the logo link. Consider using <code>#</code> with a <code>disabled</code> visual, or a dedicated stub page.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">components/Navbar.tsx \u00b7 line 9</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>key={index}</code> used in list rendering</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Both the samples list in <code>VoiceProfilePage</code> and the dos/donts lists in <code>VoiceProfileCard</code> use the array index as React key. For static lists this is harmless, but when items are reordered or deleted (which happens in samples), index keys cause React to mis-reconcile controlled inputs. Use a stable ID (e.g. <code>crypto.randomUUID()</code> assigned at creation time) for the samples array.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 line 105 \u00b7 components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx \u00b7 lines 41, 52</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 TESTING \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83e\uddea Testing</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No test runner configured \u2014 tests cannot be executed via <code>npm test</code></span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>run-phase2-tests.ts</code> exists and covers the core AI logic, but there is no <code>\"test\"</code> script in <code>package.json</code> and no test framework (Vitest, Jest) installed. The file also uses bare <code>import()</code> to re-import an already-loaded module, which only works in certain Node ESM setups. At minimum, add <code>\"test\": \"tsx run-phase2-tests.ts\"</code> to <code>package.json</code> and install <code>tsx</code> as a dev dependency.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">package.json \u00b7 run-phase2-tests.ts</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No UI or integration tests yet</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Expected for Phase 2. When Phase 3 adds content generation, consider adding at least smoke tests with React Testing Library or Playwright to cover the happy path.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">\u2014</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 SUMMARY TABLE \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udccb Priority Summary</h2>\n    <table>\n      <thead>\n        <tr>\n          <th>Priority</th>\n          <th>Issue</th>\n          <th>File</th>\n        </tr>\n   <td class=\"dim\">Navbar.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-red\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">High</span></td>\n          <td>Unify VoiceProfile type \u2014 3 duplicate definitions</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">gemini.ts / page.tsx / VoiceProfileCard.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-red\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">High</span></td>\n          <td>Enforce max length on samples (prevent token abuse)</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">route.ts \u00b7 page.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-yellow\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Medium</span></td>\n          <td>Wire up test runner (<code>tsx</code> + npm script)</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">package.json</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-yellow\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Medium</span></td>\n          <td>Use stable keys instead of index for samples list</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">voice-profile/page.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-yellow\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Medium</span></td>\n          <td>API key in query string \u2014 move to header or server-only path</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">lib/gemini.ts</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-blue\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Low</span></td>\n          <td>Phase 3 nav link href=\"/\" is confusing</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">Navbar.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-blue\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Low</span></td>\n          <td>Add rate limiting before any public deploy</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">route.ts</td>\n        </tr>\n      </tbody>\n    </table>\n  </section>\n\n  <footer>Made with IBM Bob</footer>\n</div>\n</body>\n</html>\n"
      },
      "isError": false
    }
  ]
}

**Tool** (1784501010481):

  "role": "tool",
  "content": "Created artifact \"EchoDraft Phase 2 \u2014 Code Quality Analysis\" (id: echodraft_code_quality).",
  "toolUsage": {
    "signature": {
      "id": "tooluse_cfiDc3ORL6lp5jE8JYwiuB",
      "name": "create_html_artifact",
      "arguments": {
        "id": "echodraft_code_quality",
        "title": "EchoDraft Phase 2 \u2014 Code Quality Analysis",
        "description": "Early-stage review covering architecture, bugs, type safety, security, testing, and actionable next steps.",
        "html": "<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head>\n<meta charset=\"UTF-8\" />\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\" />\n<title>EchoDraft Phase 2 \u2014 Code Quality Analysis</title>\n<style>\n  *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }\n  body {\n    font-family: -apple-system, \"Segoe UI\", system-ui, sans-serif;\n    font-size: 14px;\n    line-height: 1.6;\n    background: #ffffff;\n    color: #1f2328;\n    padding: 32px 16px 48px;\n  }\n  .page { max-width: 760px; margin: 0 auto; }\n\n  h1 { font-size: 22px; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 4px; }\n  .subtitle { color: #57606a; font-size: 13px; margin-bottom: 28px; }\n\n  /* Score bar */\n  .score-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 16px; background: #f7f8fa; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 10px; padding: 16px 20px; margin-bottom: 28px; }\n  .score-circle { width: 64px; height: 64px; border-radius: 50%; border: 4px solid #3b82d4; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0; }\n  .score-num { font-size: 22px; font-weight: 700; color: #3b82d4; }\n  .score-label { font-size: 13px; color: #57606a; }\n  .score-desc { font-size: 13px; color: #1f2328; }\n\n  /* Summary pills */\n  .pills { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 28px; }\n  .pill { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 999px; }\n  .pill-green  { background: #e6f4ea; color: #1a7f37; }\n  .pill-yellow { background: #fff8c5; color: #7d4e00; }\n  .pill-red    { background: #ffebe9; color: #cf222e; }\n  .pill-blue   { background: #ddf4ff; color: #0969da; }\n\n  /* Section */\n  section { margin-bottom: 32px; }\n  h2 { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 12px; padding-bottom: 6px; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; }\n\n  /* Finding cards */\n  .finding { border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 8px; padding: 14px 16px; margin-bottom: 10px; }\n  .finding-header { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 6px; }\n  .badge { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px; flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 1px; text-transform: uppercase; }\n  .badge-bug    { background: #ffebe9; color: #cf222e; }\n  .badge-warn   { background: #fff8c5; color: #7d4e00; }\n  .badge-info   { background: #ddf4ff; color: #0969da; }\n  .badge-good   { background: #e6f4ea; color: #1a7f37; }\n  .finding-title { font-weight: 600; font-size: 13px; }\n  .finding-body { font-size: 13px; color: #1f2328; }\n  .finding-body code { background: #f7f8fa; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; padding: 1px 5px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 12px; font-family: \"Cascadia Code\", \"Fira Mono\", monospace; }\n  .finding-file { font-size: 11px; color: #57606a; margin-top: 6px; font-family: \"Cascadia Code\", \"Fira Mono\", monospace; }\n\n  /* Table */\n  table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 13px; }\n  th { text-align: left; font-weight: 600; color: #57606a; font-size: 12px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; padding: 6px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; }\n  td { padding: 8px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid #f0f0f0; vertical-align: top; }\n  tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }\n  .dim { color: #57606a; }\n\n  footer { margin-top: 48px; padding-top: 12px; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; text-align: center; font-size: 11px; color: #57606a; }\n</style>\n</head>\n<body>\n<div class=\"page\">\n\n  <h1>EchoDraft Phase 2 \u2014 Code Quality Analysis</h1>\n  <p class=\"subtitle\">Early-stage review \u00b7 7 source files \u00b7 Next.js 15 \u00b7 React 19 \u00b7 TypeScript \u00b7 Tailwind \u00b7 Gemini API</p>\n\n  <div class=\"score-row\">\n    <div class=\"score-circle\"><span class=\"score-num\">72</span></div>\n    <div>\n      <div style=\"font-weight:700;font-size:15px;\">Good for early-phase work</div>\n      <div class=\"score-label\">Overall score out of 100</div>\n      <div class=\"score-desc\" style=\"margin-top:4px;\">The foundation is clean and coherent. The one shipped bug (wrong icon name) is already fixed. Main gaps are type duplication, missing input sanitisation, and zero test infrastructure for the UI layer.</div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <div class=\"pills\">\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Clean architecture</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Graceful AI fallback</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Good error UX</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Strict mode on</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-yellow\">\u26a0 Duplicate type definitions</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-yellow\">\u26a0 No input length cap</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-yellow\">\u26a0 No test runner wired up</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-red\">\u2715 API key in fetch URL (log risk)</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-red\">\u2715 localStorage \u2014 no SSR guard</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-blue\">i Phase 3 nav link points to /</span>\n  </div>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 BUGS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udc1b Bugs &amp; Runtime Risks</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-bug\">Fixed</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>FiMic2</code> does not exist in react-icons/fi</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>FiMic2</code> is <code>undefined</code> at runtime \u2014 React throws when trying to render it as a component. Fixed in the current session by renaming to <code>FiMic</code>.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">components/Navbar.tsx \u00b7 line 5 &amp; 19</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-bug\">Risk</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">API key appended as a plain query-string parameter</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>fetch(`${GEMINI_API_URL}?key=${apiKey}`)</code> \u2014 the key appears in browser network logs, server access logs, and any proxy/CDN logs. The API route already runs server-side (no CORS), so the client never sees the key, but server-side fetch logs and error stacks may expose it. Consider using a custom header or moving the key through an environment-only path.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 line 50</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">localStorage read inside <code>useEffect</code> is safe, but write is not guarded</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The <code>useEffect</code> correctly defers the read until the client. However <code>localStorage.setItem</code> on line 76 and <code>clearProfile</code> on line 86 are called directly from event handlers \u2014 fine in a client component, but if this file is ever imported in a server context without <code>\"use client\"</code>, it will crash. The directive is already present, so this is low-risk today, but worth a comment noting the dependency.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 lines 76, 86</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 TYPE SAFETY \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udd37 Type Safety</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>VoiceProfile</code> type is defined twice</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The type is declared in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> (exported) and then copy-pasted verbatim into <code>app/voice-profile/page.tsx</code> (local). If the shape ever changes, only one definition will be updated. Fix: remove the local declaration and import from <code>lib/gemini</code>.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 line 4 \u00b7 app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 line 7 \u00b7 components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx \u00b7 line 1</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>VoiceProfileCard</code> has its own inline <code>VoiceProfile</code> type</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Same issue \u2014 three files each define the same shape. The canonical export in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> should be the single source of truth.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx \u00b7 line 1\u20137</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>extractJsonFromText</code> returns <code>unknown</code> \u2014 cast happens at call site</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The <code>as Partial&lt;VoiceProfile&gt;</code> cast on line 73 of <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> is fine for now because <code>normalizeVoiceProfile</code> defensively handles every field. No immediate risk, but the cast bypasses TypeScript's structural check \u2014 acceptable at this stage.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 line 73</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 SECURITY / INPUT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udd10 Security &amp; Input Validation</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No maximum length enforced on writing samples</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Both the client and API route accept samples of arbitrary length. A user could send megabytes of text to Gemini, causing very large token bills or a 400 from the API. A simple limit (e.g. 2 000 characters per sample) should be enforced server-side in the route and echoed to the user in the UI.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts \u00b7 line 7\u20139 \u00b7 app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 line 53</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No rate-limiting on the API route</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The <code>/api/build-voice-profile</code> endpoint has no rate limiting. Acceptable for a prototype, but should be addressed before any public deployment, especially given the API key cost model.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 CODE ORGANISATION \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\uddc2 Code Organisation</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-good\">Good</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">Clean separation of concerns</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        AI logic lives entirely in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code>, the API boundary is a thin route in <code>app/api/</code>, and the UI components only render data they receive. This is a well-structured pattern that will scale to Phase 3 without restructuring.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts \u00b7 components/</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-good\">Good</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">Mock fallback makes development API-key-free</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>callGeminiForVoiceProfile</code> falls back to <code>mockVoiceProfile()</code> when <code>GEMINI_API_KEY</code> is absent. The mock is even content-aware (checks for question marks, average length). This is a solid DX decision.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 lines 45\u201346, 99\u2013127</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">Nav item \"Generate (Phase 3+)\" links to <code>/</code></span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The placeholder href is <code>/</code> which is the home page. Clicking it gives no \"coming soon\" feedback and is easily confused with the logo link. Consider using <code>#</code> with a <code>disabled</code> visual, or a dedicated stub page.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">components/Navbar.tsx \u00b7 line 9</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>key={index}</code> used in list rendering</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Both the samples list in <code>VoiceProfilePage</code> and the dos/donts lists in <code>VoiceProfileCard</code> use the array index as React key. For static lists this is harmless, but when items are reordered or deleted (which happens in samples), index keys cause React to mis-reconcile controlled inputs. Use a stable ID (e.g. <code>crypto.randomUUID()</code> assigned at creation time) for the samples array.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 line 105 \u00b7 components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx \u00b7 lines 41, 52</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 TESTING \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83e\uddea Testing</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No test runner configured \u2014 tests cannot be executed via <code>npm test</code></span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>run-phase2-tests.ts</code> exists and covers the core AI logic, but there is no <code>\"test\"</code> script in <code>package.json</code> and no test framework (Vitest, Jest) installed. The file also uses bare <code>import()</code> to re-import an already-loaded module, which only works in certain Node ESM setups. At minimum, add <code>\"test\": \"tsx run-phase2-tests.ts\"</code> to <code>package.json</code> and install <code>tsx</code> as a dev dependency.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">package.json \u00b7 run-phase2-tests.ts</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No UI or integration tests yet</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Expected for Phase 2. When Phase 3 adds content generation, consider adding at least smoke tests with React Testing Library or Playwright to cover the happy path.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">\u2014</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  <!-- \u2500\u2500\u2500 SUMMARY TABLE \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 -->\n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udccb Priority Summary</h2>\n    <table>\n      <thead>\n        <tr>\n          <th>Priority</th>\n          <th>Issue</th>\n          <th>File</th>\n        </tr>\n      </thead>\n      <tbody>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-green\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Done</span></td>\n          <td>FiMic2 \u2192 FiMic (runtime crash fix)</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">Navbar.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-red\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">High</span></td>\n          <td>Unify VoiceProfile type \u2014 3 duplicate definitions</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">gemini.ts / page.tsx / VoiceProfileCard.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-red\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">High</span></td>\n          <td>Enforce max length on samples (prevent token abuse)</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">route.ts \u00b7 page.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-yellow\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Medium</span></td>\n          <td>Wire up test runner (<code>tsx</code> + npm script)</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">package.json</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-yellow\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Medium</span></td>\n          <td>Use stable keys instead of index for samples list</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">voice-profile/page.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-yellow\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Medium</span></td>\n          <td>API key in query string \u2014 move to header or server-only path</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">lib/gemini.ts</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-blue\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Low</span></td>\n          <td>Phase 3 nav link href=\"/\" is confusing</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">Navbar.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-blue\" style=\"font-size:11px;\">Low</span></td>\n          <td>Add rate limiting before any public deploy</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">route.ts</td>\n        </tr>\n      </tbody>\n    </table>\n  </section>\n\n  <footer>Made with IBM Bob</footer>\n</div>\n</body>\n</html>\n"
      },
      "isError": false
    },

      "displayName": "Create artifact {title}",
      "error": "Failed to create artifact {title}",

      "success": "Created artifact {title}",

  "_meta": {

      "echodraft_code_quality": {

        "content": "\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head>\n<meta charset=\"UTF-8\" />\n<meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\" />\n<title>EchoDraft Phase 2 \u2014 Code Quality Analysis</title>\n<style>\n  *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }\n  body {\n    font-family: -apple-system, \"Segoe UI\", system-ui, sans-serif;\n    font-size: 14px;\n    line-height: 1.6;\n    background: #ffffff;\n    color: #1f2328;\n    padding: 32px 16px 48px;\n  }\n  .page { max-width: 760px; margin: 0 auto; }\n\n  h1 { font-size: 22px; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 4px; }\n  .subtitle { color: #57606a; font-size: 13px; margin-bottom: 28px; }\n\n  /* Score bar */\n  .score-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 16px; background: #f7f8fa; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 10px; padding: 16px 20px; margin-bottom: 28px; }\n  .score-circle { width: 64px; height: 64px; border-radius: 50%; border: 4px solid #3b82d4; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0; }\n  .score-num { font-size: 22px; font-weight: 700; color: #3b82d4; }\n  .score-label { font-size: 13px; color: #57606a; }\n  .score-desc { font-size: 13px; color: #1f2328; }\n\n  /* Summary pills */\n  .pills { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 28px; }\n  .pill { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; padding: 3px 10px; border-radius: 999px; }\n  .pill-green  { background: #e6f4ea; color: #1a7f37; }\n  .pill-yellow { background: #fff8c5; color: #7d4e00; }\n  .pill-red    { background: #ffebe9; color: #cf222e; }\n  .pill-blue   { background: #ddf4ff; color: #0969da; }\n\n  /* Section */\n  section { margin-bottom: 32px; }\n  h2 { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 12px; padding-bottom: 6px; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; }\n\n  /* Finding cards */\n  .finding { border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 8px; padding: 14px 16px; margin-bottom: 10px; }\n  .finding-header { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 6px; }\n  .badge { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px; flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 1px; text-transform: uppercase; }\n  .badge-bug    { background: #ffebe9; color: #cf222e; }\n  .badge-warn   { background: #fff8c5; color: #7d4e00; }\n  .badge-info   { background: #ddf4ff; color: #0969da; }\n  .badge-good   { background: #e6f4ea; color: #1a7f37; }\n  .finding-title { font-weight: 600; font-size: 13px; }\n  .finding-body { font-size: 13px; color: #1f2328; }\n  .finding-body code { background: #f7f8fa; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; padding: 1px 5px; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 12px; font-family: \"Cascadia Code\", \"Fira Mono\", monospace; }\n  .finding-file { font-size: 11px; color: #57606a; margin-top: 6px; font-family: \"Cascadia Code\", \"Fira Mono\", monospace; }\n\n  /* Table */\n  table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 13px; }\n  th { text-align: left; font-weight: 600; color: #57606a; font-size: 12px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; padding: 6px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; }\n  td { padding: 8px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid #f0f0f0; vertical-align: top; }\n  tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }\n  .dim { color: #57606a; }\n\n  footer { margin-top: 48px; padding-top: 12px; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; text-align: center; font-size: 11px; color: #57606a; }\n</style>\n</head>\n<body>\n<div class=\"page\">\n\n  <h1>EchoDraft Phase 2 \u2014 Code Quality Analysis</h1>\n  <p class=\"subtitle\">Early-stage review \u00b7 7 source files \u00b7 Next.js 15 \u00b7 React 19 \u00b7 TypeScript \u00b7 Tailwind \u00b7 Gemini API</p>\n\n  <div class=\"score-row\">\n    <div class=\"score-circle\"><span class=\"score-num\">72</span></div>\n    <div>\n      <div style=\"font-weight:700;font-size:15px\">Good for early-phase work</div>\n      <div class=\"score-label\">Overall score out of 100</div>\n      <div class=\"score-desc\" style=\"margin-top:4px\">The foundation is clean and coherent. The one shipped bug (wrong icon name) is already fixed. Main gaps are type duplication, missing input sanitisation, and zero test infrastructure for the UI layer.</div>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <div class=\"pills\">\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Clean architecture</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Graceful AI fallback</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Good error UX</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-green\">\u2713 Strict mode on</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-yellow\">\u26a0 Duplicate type definitions</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-yellow\">\u26a0 No input length cap</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-yellow\">\u26a0 No test runner wired up</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-red\">\u2715 API key in fetch URL (log risk)</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-red\">\u2715 localStorage \u2014 no SSR guard</span>\n    <span class=\"pill pill-blue\">i Phase 3 nav link points to /</span>\n  </div>\n\n  \n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udc1b Bugs &amp; Runtime Risks</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-bug\">Fixed</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>FiMic2</code> does not exist in react-icons/fi</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>FiMic2</code> is <code>undefined</code> at runtime \u2014 React throws when trying to render it as a component. Fixed in the current session by renaming to <code>FiMic</code>.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">components/Navbar.tsx \u00b7 line 5 &amp; 19</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-bug\">Risk</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">API key appended as a plain query-string parameter</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>fetch(`${GEMINI_API_URL}?key=${apiKey}`)</code> \u2014 the key appears in browser network logs, server access logs, and any proxy/CDN logs. The API route already runs server-side (no CORS), so the client never sees the key, but server-side fetch logs and error stacks may expose it. Consider using a custom header or moving the key through an environment-only path.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 line 50</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">localStorage read inside <code>useEffect</code> is safe, but write is not guarded</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The <code>useEffect</code> correctly defers the read until the client. However <code>localStorage.setItem</code> on line 76 and <code>clearProfile</code> on line 86 are called directly from event handlers \u2014 fine in a client component, but if this file is ever imported in a server context without <code>\"use client\"</code>, it will crash. The directive is already present, so this is low-risk today, but worth a comment noting the dependency.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 lines 76, 86</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  \n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udd37 Type Safety</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>VoiceProfile</code> type is defined twice</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The type is declared in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> (exported) and then copy-pasted verbatim into <code>app/voice-profile/page.tsx</code> (local). If the shape ever changes, only one definition will be updated. Fix: remove the local declaration and import from <code>lib/gemini</code>.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 line 4 \u00b7 app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 line 7 \u00b7 components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx \u00b7 line 1</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>VoiceProfileCard</code> has its own inline <code>VoiceProfile</code> type</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Same issue \u2014 three files each define the same shape. The canonical export in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> should be the single source of truth.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx \u00b7 line 1\u20137</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>extractJsonFromText</code> returns <code>unknown</code> \u2014 cast happens at call site</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The <code>as Partial&lt;VoiceProfile&gt;</code> cast on line 73 of <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> is fine for now because <code>normalizeVoiceProfile</code> defensively handles every field. No immediate risk, but the cast bypasses TypeScript's structural check \u2014 acceptable at this stage.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 line 73</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  \n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udd10 Security &amp; Input Validation</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No maximum length enforced on writing samples</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Both the client and API route accept samples of arbitrary length. A user could send megabytes of text to Gemini, causing very large token bills or a 400 from the API. A simple limit (e.g. 2 000 characters per sample) should be enforced server-side in the route and echoed to the user in the UI.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts \u00b7 line 7\u20139 \u00b7 app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 line 53</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No rate-limiting on the API route</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The <code>/api/build-voice-profile</code> endpoint has no rate limiting. Acceptable for a prototype, but should be addressed before any public deployment, especially given the API key cost model.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  \n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\uddc2 Code Organisation</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-good\">Good</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">Clean separation of concerns</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        AI logic lives entirely in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code>, the API boundary is a thin route in <code>app/api/</code>, and the UI components only render data they receive. This is a well-structured pattern that will scale to Phase 3 without restructuring.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts \u00b7 components/</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-good\">Good</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">Mock fallback makes development API-key-free</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>callGeminiForVoiceProfile</code> falls back to <code>mockVoiceProfile()</code> when <code>GEMINI_API_KEY</code> is absent. The mock is even content-aware (checks for question marks, average length). This is a solid DX decision.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">lib/gemini.ts \u00b7 lines 45\u201346, 99\u2013127</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">Nav item \"Generate (Phase 3+)\" links to <code>/</code></span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        The placeholder href is <code>/</code> which is the home page. Clicking it gives no \"coming soon\" feedback and is easily confused with the logo link. Consider using <code>#</code> with a <code>disabled</code> visual, or a dedicated stub page.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">components/Navbar.tsx \u00b7 line 9</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\"><code>key={index}</code> used in list rendering</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Both the samples list in <code>VoiceProfilePage</code> and the dos/donts lists in <code>VoiceProfileCard</code> use the array index as React key. For static lists this is harmless, but when items are reordered or deleted (which happens in samples), index keys cause React to mis-reconcile controlled inputs. Use a stable ID (e.g. <code>crypto.randomUUID()</code> assigned at creation time) for the samples array.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">app/voice-profile/page.tsx \u00b7 line 105 \u00b7 components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx \u00b7 lines 41, 52</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  \n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83e\uddea Testing</h2>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-warn\">Warning</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No test runner configured \u2014 tests cannot be executed via <code>npm test</code></span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        <code>run-phase2-tests.ts</code> exists and covers the core AI logic, but there is no <code>\"test\"</code> script in <code>package.json</code> and no test framework (Vitest, Jest) installed. The file also uses bare <code>import()</code> to re-import an already-loaded module, which only works in certain Node ESM setups. At minimum, add <code>\"test\": \"tsx run-phase2-tests.ts\"</code> to <code>package.json</code> and install <code>tsx</code> as a dev dependency.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">package.json \u00b7 run-phase2-tests.ts</div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"finding\">\n      <div class=\"finding-header\">\n        <span class=\"badge badge-info\">Info</span>\n        <span class=\"finding-title\">No UI or integration tests yet</span>\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-body\">\n        Expected for Phase 2. When Phase 3 adds content generation, consider adding at least smoke tests with React Testing Library or Playwright to cover the happy path.\n      </div>\n      <div class=\"finding-file\">\u2014</div>\n    </div>\n  </section>\n\n  \n  <section>\n    <h2>\ud83d\udccb Priority Summary</h2>\n    <table>\n      <thead>\n        <tr>\n          <th>Priority</th>\n          <th>Issue</th>\n          <th>File</th>\n        </tr>\n      </thead>\n      <tbody>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-green\" style=\"font-size:11px\">Done</span></td>\n          <td>FiMic2 \u2192 FiMic (runtime crash fix)</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">Navbar.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-red\" style=\"font-size:11px\">High</span></td>\n          <td>Unify VoiceProfile type \u2014 3 duplicate definitions</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">gemini.ts / page.tsx / VoiceProfileCard.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-red\" style=\"font-size:11px\">High</span></td>\n          <td>Enforce max length on samples (prevent token abuse)</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">route.ts \u00b7 page.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-yellow\" style=\"font-size:11px\">Medium</span></td>\n          <td>Wire up test runner (<code>tsx</code> + npm script)</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">package.json</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-yellow\" style=\"font-size:11px\">Medium</span></td>\n          <td>Use stable keys instead of index for samples list</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">voice-profile/page.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-yellow\" style=\"font-size:11px\">Medium</span></td>\n          <td>API key in query string \u2014 move to header or server-only path</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">lib/gemini.ts</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-blue\" style=\"font-size:11px\">Low</span></td>\n          <td>Phase 3 nav link href=\"/\" is confusing</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">Navbar.tsx</td>\n        </tr>\n        <tr>\n          <td><span class=\"pill pill-blue\" style=\"font-size:11px\">Low</span></td>\n          <td>Add rate limiting before any public deploy</td>\n          <td class=\"dim\">route.ts</td>\n        </tr>\n      </tbody>\n    </table>\n  </section>\n\n  <footer>Made with IBM Bob</footer>\n</div>\n</body>\n</html>\n",
        "createdAt": 1784501010469
      }
    },

Key takeaways for an early-stage project:\n\n**Overall: solid foundation \u2014 72/100.** The architecture is clean, the AI mock fallback is smart, and error handling on both client and server is well done. The main issues to fix before Phase 3:\n\n1. **High \u2014 Duplicate `VoiceProfile` type** across [`lib/gemini.ts`](echodraft/lib/gemini.ts:4), [`app/voice-profile/page.tsx`](echodraft/app/voice-profile/page.tsx:7), and [`components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx`](echodraft/components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx:1). Delete the two local copies and import the exported one from `lib/gemini`.\n\n2. **High \u2014 No input length cap** on writing samples. A user can send unlimited text straight to the Gemini API, which means unbounded token cost. Add a `MAX_CHARS = 2000` guard in the route and reflect it in the textarea.\n\n3. **Medium \u2014 No test runner wired up.** [`run-phase2-tests.ts`](echodraft/run-phase2-tests.ts) exists and covers the right logic, but there's no `\"test\"` script in [`package.json`](echodraft/package.json) and no `tsx` installed to run it.\n\n4. **Medium \u2014 `key={index}` on the samples list** in [`voice-profile/page.tsx`](echodraft/app/voice-profile/page.tsx:105). When a user removes a middle sample, React mis-reconciles the controlled textareas. Use a stable ID at creation time.\n\n5. **Medium \u2014 API key in query string** in [`lib/gemini.ts:50`](echodraft/lib/gemini.ts:50) gets written into server access logs.",

The root cause is identical:\n\n**[`Navbar.tsx:5`](echodraft/components/Navbar.tsx:5)** \u2014 `FiMic2` **does not exist** in `react-icons/fi`. The installed package only exports `FiMic` and `FiMicOff`. Importing a non-existent named export makes it `undefined` at build time, which Next.js catches as a build error when it tries to resolve the module graph.\n\nTwo lines changed:\n| Line | Before | After |\n|------|--------|-------|\n| 5 | `import { FiMic2 } from \"react-icons/fi\"` | `import { FiMic } from \"react-icons/fi\"` |\n| 19 | `<FiMic2 className=\u2026>` | `<FiMic className=\u2026>` |",
  "id": "3da2d215f8c77e70b0bc7e73c3b11e67",

    "timestamp": 1784503181946,

      "reasoningTokens": 0
    }
  },
  "stop": true
}
