state\n- stale closures\n- null reference risks\n- undefined values\n- incorrect imports\n- dead code\n- unreachable code\n- duplicated logic\n- missing returns\n- edge case failures\n\n---\n\n## Security Vulnerabilities\n\nCheck for:\n\nOWASP Top 10\n\n- SQL Injection\n- NoSQL Injection\n- XSS\n- Stored XSS\n- Reflected XSS\n- DOM XSS\n- CSRF\n- SSRF\n- XXE\n- Path Traversal\n- File Upload vulnerabilities\n- Broken Authentication\n- Broken Authorization\n- IDOR\n- Mass Assignment\n- Command Injection\n- Remote Code Execution\n- Open Redirects\n- Clickjacking\n- Sensitive Data Exposure\n- Hardcoded Secrets\n- API Key Exposure\n- Weak Password Policies\n- Session Fixation\n- Session Hijacking\n- JWT vulnerabilities\n- Token leakage\n- Rate limiting issues\n- Missing input validation\n- Missing output encoding\n- Weak CORS configuration\n- Missing CSP\n- Missing security headers\n\n---\n\n## Authentication\n\nAttempt to discover:\n\n- authentication bypass\n- privilege escalation\n- admin bypass\n- broken login\n- broken logout\n- weak password handling\n- session persistence issues\n- cookie problems\n- insecure remember-me implementation\n- token reuse\n- role validation flaws\n\n---\n\n## Authorization\n\nVerify:\n\n- users cannot access other users' data\n- admins are properly protected\n- routes are protected\n- APIs enforce permissions\n- hidden endpoints cannot be accessed\n- client-side checks are backed by server-side checks\n\n---\n\n## API Testing\n\nInspect every endpoint.\n\nLook for:\n\n- missing validation\n- improper status codes\n- missing authentication\n- inconsistent responses\n- data leakage\n- excessive information disclosure\n- rate limiting\n- injection opportunities\n\n---\n\n## Database\n\nCheck:\n\n- schema consistency\n- constraints\n- indexes\n- foreign keys\n- cascading deletes\n- duplicate records\n- validation\n- transaction safety\n- rollback safety\n\n---\n\n## Frontend\n\nInspect:\n\n- navigation\n- routing\n- responsiveness\n- accessibility\n- keyboard navigation\n- broken buttons\n- broken links\n- broken forms\n- loading states\n- error states\n- empty states\n- validation\n- user feedback\n- visual inconsistencies\n\n---\n\n## Performance\n\nLook for:\n\n- N+1 queries\n- unnecessary renders\n- duplicate API calls\n- large bundles\n- blocking operations\n- expensive computations\n- memory usage\n- caching opportunities\n- lazy loading issues\n- image optimization issues\n\n---\n\n## Code Quality\n\nReview:\n\n- architecture\n- maintainability\n- readability\n- separation of concerns\n- SOLID principles\n- DRY violations\n- code duplication\n- naming consistency\n- project structure\n\n---\n\n# DYNAMIC TESTING\n\nRun or simulate testing where possible.\n\nTest normal usage.\n\nTest invalid usage.\n\nTest malicious usage.\n\nAttempt to break the application.\n\nTest:\n\n- registration\n- login\n- logout\n- password reset\n- CRUD operations\n- uploads\n- downloads\n- search\n- filters\n- pagination\n- forms\n- API endpoints\n- protected routes\n- browser refreshes\n- direct URL access\n- expired sessions\n- duplicate submissions\n- refresh spam\n- rapid clicking\n- concurrent requests\n\n---\n\n# EDGE CASE TESTING\n\nTest unusual scenarios.\n\nExamples:\n\n- empty inputs\n- maximum length inputs\n- minimum length inputs\n- Unicode\n- emojis\n- SQL payloads\n- HTML payloads\n- JavaScript payloads\n- invalid JSON\n- huge files\n- unsupported file types\n- duplicate requests\n- slow network\n- offline mode\n- browser refresh during requests\n- multiple tabs\n- simultaneous users\n\n---\n\n# PENETRATION TESTING\n\nAttempt to identify:\n\n- ways to bypass authentication\n- privilege escalation\n- data leakage\n- hidden routes\n- hidden APIs\n- exposed files\n- exposed environment variables\n- debug endpoints\n- admin panels\n- backup files\n- insecure defaults\n\nDocument every successful and unsuccessful attempt.\n\n---\n\n# DOCUMENTATION REVIEW\n\nReview all Markdown files.\n\nIdentify:\n\n- inaccurate information\n- missing setup instructions\n- outdated documentation\n- inconsistencies\n- misleading information\n- undocumented features\n\nDo NOT rewrite them.\n\nOnly report issues.\n\n---\n\n# TEST RESULTS FORMAT\n\nFor every issue provide:\n\n## Title\n\n## Severity\n\nCritical / High / Medium / Low / Informational\n\n## Category\n\nBug\nSecurity\nPerformance\nArchitecture\nDocumentation\nAccessibility\nUX\nCode Quality\n\n## Location\n\nFile name\n\nLine number(s)\n\nFunction/Class\n\n## Description\n\nExplain the issue.\n\n## Evidence\n\nShow the exact code or behavior that demonstrates the issue.\n\n## Impact\n\nExplain what could happen.\n\n## Reproduction Steps\n\nDescribe exactly how to reproduce it.\n\n## Confidence\n\nHigh / Medium / Low\n\n---\n\n# FINAL REPORT\n\nProvide:\n\n1. Executive Summary\n\n2. Overall System Health Score (0\u2013100)\n\n3. Security Score\n\n4. Code Quality Score\n\n5. Performance Score\n\n6. Reliability Score\n\n7. Accessibility Score\n\n8. Documentation Score\n\n9. Total Bugs Found\n\n10. Total Vulnerabilities Found\n\n11. Critical Issues\n\n12. High Priority Issues\n\n13. Medium Priority Issues\n\n14. Low Priority Issues\n\n15. Positive Findings\n\n16. Risks Before Production\n\n17. Recommended Testing Still Required (without implementing fixes)\n\n---\n\nIMPORTANT RULES\n\n- Do NOT modify any files.\n- Do NOT generate fixes.\n- Do NOT refactor code.\n- Do NOT improve documentation.\n- Do NOT optimize anything.\n- Do NOT skip any directory.\n- Inspect every file in the repository.\n- Clearly distinguish verified findings from assumptions.\n- If you cannot verify something, explicitly state why.\n- Be exhaustive rather than brief. Treat this as a professional production-readiness audit. ---\n\n# FUNCTIONAL & UX TESTING\n\nVerify the following through testing or simulation where possible.\n\n## Voice Generation Testing\n\nTest the application with at least **two very different voice profiles**.\n\nFor example:\n- Calm, professional narrator\n- Energetic, expressive storyteller\n\nVerify that:\n- The generated outputs are noticeably different.\n- Voice characteristics (tone, pacing, style, personality, expression) are clearly distinguishable.\n- The selected voice profile is actually respected by the generation process.\n- If outputs are nearly identical despite different profiles, report this as a functional issue.\n\nDocument:\n- Voice profiles tested\n- Generated output differences\n- Whether the test passed or failed\n\n---\n\n## Mobile Responsiveness\n\nVerify that the application functions correctly on a mobile viewport.\n\nTest using approximately **375px screen width**.\n\nCheck:\n\n- No horizontal scrolling\n- No overlapping UI elements\n- No clipped text\n- Navigation remains usable\n- Buttons remain accessible\n- Forms remain usable\n- Modals fit correctly\n- Images scale properly\n- Typography remains readable\n- Touch targets are appropriately sized\n- Inputs remain accessible\n- Layout does not break\n\nReport any visual or functional issues.\n\n---\n\n## Loading State Testing\n\nWhile generating content, verify that:\n\n- A loading spinner, progress indicator, or equivalent loading state appears immediately.\n- Users receive clear visual feedback that processing is occurring.\n- The loading indicator disappears once generation completes.\n- Users cannot accidentally trigger duplicate generation requests while loading (unless intentionally supported).\n- The UI returns to its normal interactive state after completion.\n\nReport:\n- Whether loading feedback appeared correctly.\n- Any flickering, delays, or missing loading states.\n- Any duplicate request issues.\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling Testing\n\nSimulate an API failure (for example, by using an invalid or incorrect API key).\n\nVerify that:\n\n- The application does not crash.\n- The UI does not become blank.\n- Users receive a clear, human-readable error message.\n- Error messages avoid exposing stack traces, secrets, API keys, or internal implementation details.\n- The application recovers gracefully after the failure.\n- Users can retry the operation without refreshing the application.\n\nReport:\n- Error message shown\n- Whether the application remained usable\n- Any console errors\n- Any unhandled exceptions\n- Any security information exposed during the failure\n\n---\n\nFor every functional test above, include:\n\n- Test name\n- Test scenario\n- Expected result\n- Actual result\n- Pass / Fail\n- Evidence (screenshots, logs, or observed behavior where available)\n- Severity if failed\n</user_query>",

    "mask": "# ROLE\n\nYou are a Senior Software Architect, Security Engineer, QA Lead, Penetration Tester, DevOps Engineer, and Code Reviewer with 20+ years of experience.\n\nYour job is NOT to improve or modify the project.\n\nYour only objective is to inspect every part of the system and produce a comprehensive audit report.\n\nDo NOT fix anything.\nDo NOT rewrite code.\nDo NOT optimize code.\nDo NOT suggest implementation until every issue has been identified.\n\nAssume this project is about to go into production and your responsibility is to find everything that could cause failure.\n\n---\n\n# SCOPE\n\nAudit the ENTIRE repository.\n\nThis includes but is not limited to:\n\n- Source code\n- Frontend\n- Backend\n- API\n- Database\n- Authentication\n- Authorization\n- File uploads\n- Environment variables\n- Configuration\n- Routing\n- Middleware\n- Validation\n- Error handling\n- Logging\n- Sessions\n- Cookies\n- JWT (if applicable)\n- Dependencies\n- Packages\n- Build configuration\n- Deployment configuration\n- Docker\n- CI/CD\n- GitHub Actions\n- Markdown files\n- README\n- Documentation\n- Assets\n- Images\n- Public files\n- Hidden configuration files\n- Scripts\n- Tests\n- Utilities\n- Services\n- Components\n- Hooks\n- Context providers\n- Models\n- Controllers\n- Database schema\n- Migrations\n- Seeders\n\nInspect EVERYTHING.\n\n---\n\n# DO NOT ASSUME\n\nIf information is missing:\n\n- explain what cannot be verified\n- explain why\n- explain what additional files or access would be needed\n\nNever invent findings.\n\nEvery finding must be supported by evidence from the code or testing.\n\n---\n\n# STATIC ANALYSIS\n\nReview every file.\n\nLook for:\n\n## Bugs\n\n- logic errors\n- race conditions\n- infinite loops\n- broken conditions\n- incorrect calculations\n- broken async code\n- state bugs\n- lifecycle bugs\n- memory leaks\n- event leaks\n- stale state\n- stale closures\n- null reference risks\n- undefined values\n- incorrect imports\n- dead code\n- unreachable code\n- duplicated logic\n- missing returns\n- edge case failures\n\n---\n\n## Security Vulnerabilities\n\nCheck for:\n\nOWASP Top 10\n\n- SQL Injection\n- NoSQL Injection\n- XSS\n- Stored XSS\n- Reflected XSS\n- DOM XSS\n- CSRF\n- SSRF\n- XXE\n- Path Traversal\n- File Upload vulnerabilities\n- Broken Authentication\n- Broken Authorization\n- IDOR\n- Mass Assignment\n- Command Injection\n- Remote Code Execution\n- Open Redirects\n- Clickjacking\n- Sensitive Data Exposure\n- Hardcoded Secrets\n- API Key Exposure\n- Weak Password Policies\n- Session Fixation\n- Session Hijacking\n- JWT vulnerabilities\n- Token leakage\n- Rate limiting issues\n- Missing input validation\n- Missing output encoding\n- Weak CORS configuration\n- Missing CSP\n- Missing security headers\n\n---\n\n## Authentication\n\nAttempt to discover:\n\n- authentication bypass\n- privilege escalation\n- admin bypass\n- broken login\n- broken logout\n- weak password handling\n- session persistence issues\n- cookie problems\n- insecure remember-me implementation\n- token reuse\n- role validation flaws\n\n---\n\n## Authorization\n\nVerify:\n\n- users cannot access other users' data\n- admins are properly protected\n- routes are protected\n- APIs enforce permissions\n- hidden endpoints cannot be accessed\n- client-side checks are backed by server-side checks\n\n---\n\n## API Testing\n\nInspect every endpoint.\n\nLook for:\n\n- missing validation\n- improper status codes\n- missing authentication\n- inconsistent responses\n- data leakage\n- excessive information disclosure\n- rate limiting\n- injection opportunities\n\n---\n\n## Database\n\nCheck:\n\n- schema consistency\n- constraints\n- indexes\n- foreign keys\n- cascading deletes\n- duplicate records\n- validation\n- transaction safety\n- rollback safety\n\n---\n\n## Frontend\n\nInspect:\n\n- navigation\n- routing\n- responsiveness\n- accessibility\n- keyboard navigation\n- broken buttons\n- broken links\n- broken forms\n- loading states\n- error states\n- empty states\n- validation\n- user feedback\n- visual inconsistencies\n\n---\n\n## Performance\n\nLook for:\n\n- N+1 queries\n- unnecessary renders\n- duplicate API calls\n- large bundles\n- blocking operations\n- expensive computations\n- memory usage\n- caching opportunities\n- lazy loading issues\n- image optimization issues\n\n---\n\n## Code Quality\n\nReview:\n\n- architecture\n- maintainability\n- readability\n- separation of concerns\n- SOLID principles\n- DRY violations\n- code duplication\n- naming consistency\n- project structure\n\n---\n\n# DYNAMIC TESTING\n\nRun or simulate testing where possible.\n\nTest normal usage.\n\nTest invalid usage.\n\nTest malicious usage.\n\nAttempt to break the application.\n\nTest:\n\n- registration\n- login\n- logout\n- password reset\n- CRUD operations\n- uploads\n- downloads\n- search\n- filters\n- pagination\n- forms\n- API endpoints\n- protected routes\n- browser refreshes\n- direct URL access\n- expired sessions\n- duplicate submissions\n- refresh spam\n- rapid clicking\n- concurrent requests\n\n---\n\n# EDGE CASE TESTING\n\nTest unusual scenarios.\n\nExamples:\n\n- empty inputs\n- maximum length inputs\n- minimum length inputs\n- Unicode\n- emojis\n- SQL payloads\n- HTML payloads\n- JavaScript payloads\n- invalid JSON\n- huge files\n- unsupported file types\n- duplicate requests\n- slow network\n- offline mode\n- browser refresh during requests\n- multiple tabs\n- simultaneous users\n\n---\n\n# PENETRATION TESTING\n\nAttempt to identify:\n\n- ways to bypass authentication\n- privilege escalation\n- data leakage\n- hidden routes\n- hidden APIs\n- exposed files\n- exposed environment variables\n- debug endpoints\n- admin panels\n- backup files\n- insecure defaults\n\nDocument every successful and unsuccessful attempt.\n\n---\n\n# DOCUMENTATION REVIEW\n\nReview all Markdown files.\n\nIdentify:\n\n- inaccurate information\n- missing setup instructions\n- outdated documentation\n- inconsistencies\n- misleading information\n- undocumented features\n\nDo NOT rewrite them.\n\nOnly report issues.\n\n---\n\n# TEST RESULTS FORMAT\n\nFor every issue provide:\n\n## Title\n\n## Severity\n\nCritical / High / Medium / Low / Informational\n\n## Category\n\nBug\nSecurity\nPerformance\nArchitecture\nDocumentation\nAccessibility\nUX\nCode Quality\n\n## Location\n\nFile name\n\nLine number(s)\n\nFunction/Class\n\n## Description\n\nExplain the issue.\n\n## Evidence\n\nShow the exact code or behavior that demonstrates the issue.\n\n## Impact\n\nExplain what could happen.\n\n## Reproduction Steps\n\nDescribe exactly how to reproduce it.\n\n## Confidence\n\nHigh / Medium / Low\n\n---\n\n# FINAL REPORT\n\nProvide:\n\n1. Executive Summary\n\n2. Overall System Health Score (0\u2013100)\n\n3. Security Score\n\n4. Code Quality Score\n\n5. Performance Score\n\n6. Reliability Score\n\n7. Accessibility Score\n\n8. Documentation Score\n\n9. Total Bugs Found\n\n10. Total Vulnerabilities Found\n\n11. Critical Issues\n\n12. High Priority Issues\n\n13. Medium Priority Issues\n\n14. Low Priority Issues\n\n15. Positive Findings\n\n16. Risks Before Production\n\n17. Recommended Testing Still Required (without implementing fixes)\n\n---\n\nIMPORTANT RULES\n\n- Do NOT modify any files.\n- Do NOT generate fixes.\n- Do NOT refactor code.\n- Do NOT improve documentation.\n- Do NOT optimize anything.\n- Do NOT skip any directory.\n- Inspect every file in the repository.\n- Clearly distinguish verified findings from assumptions.\n- If you cannot verify something, explicitly state why.\n- Be exhaustive rather than brief. Treat this as a professional production-readiness audit. ---\n\n# FUNCTIONAL & UX TESTING\n\nVerify the following through testing or simulation where possible.\n\n## Voice Generation Testing\n\nTest the application with at least **two very different voice profiles**.\n\nFor example:\n- Calm, professional narrator\n- Energetic, expressive storyteller\n\nVerify that:\n- The generated outputs are noticeably different.\n- Voice characteristics (tone, pacing, style, personality, expression) are clearly distinguishable.\n- The selected voice profile is actually respected by the generation process.\n- If outputs are nearly identical despite different profiles, report this as a functional issue.\n\nDocument:\n- Voice profiles tested\n- Generated output differences\n- Whether the test passed or failed\n\n---\n\n## Mobile Responsiveness\n\nVerify that the application functions correctly on a mobile viewport.\n\nTest using approximately **375px screen width**.\n\nCheck:\n\n- No horizontal scrolling\n- No overlapping UI elements\n- No clipped text\n- Navigation remains usable\n- Buttons remain accessible\n- Forms remain usable\n- Modals fit correctly\n- Images scale properly\n- Typography remains readable\n- Touch targets are appropriately sized\n- Inputs remain accessible\n- Layout does not break\n\nReport any visual or functional issues.\n\n---\n\n## Loading State Testing\n\nWhile generating content, verify that:\n\n- A loading spinner, progress indicator, or equivalent loading state appears immediately.\n- Users receive clear visual feedback that processing is occurring.\n- The loading indicator disappears once generation completes.\n- Users cannot accidentally trigger duplicate generation requests while loading (unless intentionally supported).\n- The UI returns to its normal interactive state after completion.\n\nReport:\n- Whether loading feedback appeared correctly.\n- Any flickering, delays, or missing loading states.\n- Any duplicate request issues.\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling Testing\n\nSimulate an API failure (for example, by using an invalid or incorrect API key).\n\nVerify that:\n\n- The application does not crash.\n- The UI does not become blank.\n- Users receive a clear, human-readable error message.\n- Error messages avoid exposing stack traces, secrets, API keys, or internal implementation details.\n- The application recovers gracefully after the failure.\n- Users can retry the operation without refreshing the application.\n\nReport:\n- Error message shown\n- Whether the application remained usable\n- Any console errors\n- Any unhandled exceptions\n- Any security information exposed during the failure\n\n---\n\nFor every functional test above, include:\n\n- Test name\n- Test scenario\n- Expected result\n- Actual result\n- Pass / Fail\n- Evidence (screenshots, logs, or observed behavior where available)\n- Severity if failed",

  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/test-server.js:\n\n1 | // Minimal test server that mimics the Phase 2 API route and serves the preview.\n2 | const http = require(\"http\");\n3 | const fs = require(\"fs\");\n4 | const path = require(\"path\");\n5 | \n6 | const PORT = 3456;\n7 | \n8 | const server = http.createServer((req, res) => {\n9 |   if (req.url === \"/api/build-voice-profile\" && req.method === \"POST\") {\n10 |     let body = \"\";\n11 |     req.on(\"data\", (chunk) => (body += chunk));\n12 |     req.on(\"end\", () => {\n13 |       try {\n14 |         const { samples = [] } = JSON.parse(body);\n15 |         const nonEmpty = samples.filter((s) => typeof s === \"string\" && s.trim().length > 0);\n16 |         if (nonEmpty.length < 3) {\n17 |           res.writeHead(400, { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" });\n18 |           res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: \"Please provide at least 3 writing samples.\" }));\n19 |           return;\n20 |         }\n21 |         res.writeHead(200, { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" });\n22 |         res.end(\n23 |           JSON.stringify({\n24 |             voiceProfile: {\n25 |               tone: \"Conversational, warm, and slightly self-deprecating\",\n26 |               vocabulary: \"Plain English, short words, occasional slang\",\n27 |               sentence_rhythm: \"Short to medium sentences, line breaks for emphasis\",\n28 |               dos: [\"Lead with a personal hook\", \"Use contractions\", \"Ask a question at the end\"],\n29 |               donts: [\"Avoid corporate jargon\", \"Don't over-explain\", \"Don't bury the point\"],\n30 |             },\n31 |           })\n32 |         );\n33 |       } catch (e) {\n34 |         res.writeHead(500, { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" });\n35 |         res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: \"Internal server error\" }));\n36 |       }\n37 |     });\n38 |     return;\n39 |   }\n40 | \n41 |   // Serve the static preview for any other GET request\n42 |   if (req.method === \"GET\") {\n43 |     const filePath = path.join(__dirname, \"design-preview.html\");\n44 |     fs.readFile(filePath, \"utf8\", (err, data) => {\n45 |       if (err) {\n46 |         res.writeHead(500, { \"Content-Type\": \"text/plain\" });\n47 |         res.end(\"Could not load preview.\");\n48 |       } else {\n49 |         res.writeHead(200, { \"Content-Type\": \"text/html\" });\n50 |         res.end(data);\n51 |       }\n52 |     });\n53 |     return;\n54 |   }\n55 | \n56 |   res.writeHead(404, { \"Content-Type\": \"text/plain\" });\n57 |   res.end(\"Not found\");\n58 | });\n59 | \n60 | server.listen(PORT, () => {\n61 |   console.log(`Test server running at http://localhost:${PORT}`);\n62 | });\n63 | ",

**Tool** (1784507410122):
{
  "role": "tool",
  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/README.md:\n\n1 | # EchoDraft \u2014 Phase 5\n2 | \n3 | Phase 5 adds stronger voice matching, local authenticity checks, per-platform refinement, and polished loading/error/mobile states.\n4 | \n5 | ## New in Phase 5\n6 | \n7 | - **Voice Lock prompting:** tone, vocabulary, rhythm, dos, and don'ts are treated as hard constraints.\n8 | - **Authenticity score:** each result receives a local 35\u2013100 score and flags common generic-AI phrasing. This uses no API call.\n9 | - **Regenerate with tweak:** open **Tweak** on one card and enter instructions such as \u201cmake it punchier,\u201d \u201ccut 20%,\u201d or \u201cmake it more casual.\u201d Only that platform is regenerated, using one additional API call.\n10 | - **Cost-aware UX:** the initial five drafts still use one batched request; local scoring is free; refinements are opt-in.\n11 | - **Polish:** skeleton loading cards, clearer error recovery, responsive card controls, and accessible 44px action targets.\n12 | \n13 | ## Run\n14 | \n15 | Keep your existing `.env.local`:\n16 | \n17 | ```env\n18 | GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here\n19 | GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash\n20 | ```\n21 | \n22 | Then:\n23 | \n24 | ```bash\n25 | npm test\n26 | npm run dev\n27 | ```\n28 | \n29 | No new dependency was added in Phase 5.\n30 | \n31 | ## Phase 5 manual test\n32 | \n33 | 1. Generate all five drafts from the same pillar content.\n34 | 2. Confirm each card shows an authenticity score.\n35 | 3. Expand any suggestions and confirm generic phrases are identified when present.\n36 | 4. Open **Tweak** on LinkedIn and enter `make it 20% shorter and more direct`.\n37 | 5. Confirm only LinkedIn changes and the other four cards remain unchanged.\n38 | 6. Test with two contrasting voice profiles; compare hooks, vocabulary, and sentence rhythm.\n39 | 7. Temporarily use an invalid key to confirm a readable error appears and the pillar content remains intact.\n40 | 8. Check the app at 390px width.\n41 | \n42 | ## API usage\n43 | \n44 | - Generate all five: **1 call**\n45 | - Authenticity scoring: **0 calls**\n46 | - Each optional card tweak: **1 call**\n47 | \n48 | Never commit `.env.local` or expose the key through a `NEXT_PUBLIC_` variable.\n49 | ",
  "toolUsage": {
    "signature": {
      "id": "tooluse_1EHPN9ZyXNuYEaOpmDjsTi",
      "name": "read_file",
      "arguments": {
        "path": "echodraft/README.md"

if (saved) setVoiceProfile(JSON.parse(saved)); }\n25 |     catch { localStorage.removeItem(PROFILE_KEY); }\n26 |     finally { setLoaded(true); }\n27 |   }, []);\n28 | \n29 |   const generate = async (event: React.FormEvent) => {\n30 |     event.preventDefault(); setError(null); setOutputs(null); setMode(null); setModel(null);\n31 |     if (!voiceProfile) return setError(\"Build your voice profile first.\");\n32 |     if (!pillarText.trim()) return setError(\"Paste your pillar content before generating.\");\n33 |     setLoading(true);\n34 |     try {\n35 |       const response = await fetch(\"/api/generate\", { method: \"POST\", headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" }, body: JSON.stringify({ pillarText, voiceProfile }) });\n36 |       const data = await response.json().catch(() => ({}));\n37 |       if (!response.ok) throw new Error(data.error || `Request failed with status ${response.status}`);\n38 |       setOutputs(normalizePlatformOutputs(data.outputs));\n39 |       setMode(data.mode === \"ai\" ? \"ai\" : \"mock\");\n40 |       setModel(typeof data.model === \"string\" ? data.model : null);\n41 |     } catch (err) { setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Generation failed. Please try again.\"); }\n42 |     finally { setLoading(false); }\n43 |   };\n44 | \n45 |   const regenerate = async (platform: PlatformId, tweak: string) => {\n46 |     if (!outputs || !voiceProfile) throw new Error(\"Generate the initial drafts first.\");\n47 |     const key = platform === \"x\" ? \"xThread\" : platform;\n48 |     const response = await fetch(\"/api/regenerate\", { method: \"POST\", headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" }, body: JSON.stringify({ platform, tweak, currentOutput: outputs[key], pillarText, voiceProfile }) });\n49 |     const data = await response.json().catch(() => ({}));\n50 |     if (!response.ok) throw new Error(data.error || \"Could not regenerate this draft.\");\n51 |     setOutputs(previous => previous ? { ...previous, [key]: data.output } : previous);\n52 |   };\n53 | \n54 |   const exportAll = () => {\n55 |     if (!outputs) return;\n56 |     const url = URL.createObjectURL(new Blob([formatAllOutputs(outputs)], { type: \"text/plain;charset=utf-8\" }));\n57 |     const anchor = document.createElement(\"a\"); anchor.href = url; anchor.download = \"echodraft-platform-outputs.txt\"; document.body.appendChild(anchor); anchor.click(); anchor.remove(); URL.revokeObjectURL(url);\n58 |   };\n59 | \n60 |   return <div className=\"max-w-6xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 py-10 sm:py-16\">\n61 |     <div className=\"max-w-3xl mb-8\"><div className=\"inline-flex items-center gap-2 text-sm font-medium text-accent-blue mb-3\"><FiEdit3/>Phase 5 \u00b7 Voice polish</div><h1 className=\"text-3xl sm:text-4xl font-bold tracking-tight mb-3\">Five native drafts. Tuned until they sound like you.</h1><p className=\"text-secondary-text text-lg\">Generate once, check authenticity locally, then refine only the platform that needs work.</p></div>\n62 | \n63 |     {loaded && !voiceProfile && <section className=\"rounded-xl border border-accent-orange bg-accent-orange-soft p-5 mb-8 flex flex-col sm:flex-row sm:items-center justify-between gap-4\"><div className=\"flex gap-3\"><FiAlertCircle className=\"text-accent-orange mt-1\"/><div><h2 className=\"font-semibold\">Build your voice profile first</h2><p className=\"text-sm text-secondary-text\">Your voice traits drive every draft.</p></div></div><Link href=\"/voice-profile\" className=\"min-h-11 inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-primary-text text-white px-4 font-semibold text-sm\">Build voice profile</Link></section>}\n64 | \n65 |     <div className=\"grid lg:grid-cols-[minmax(0,1.1fr)_minmax(280px,.55fr)] gap-6 items-start\">\n66 |       <form onSubmit={generate} className=\"bg-canvas rounded-xl border border-border shadow-sm p-6 sm:p-8\"><div className=\"flex justify-between gap-4 mb-2\"><label htmlFor=\"pillar\" className=\"font-semibold\">Pillar content</label><span className=\"text-xs text-secondary-text tabular-nums\">{pillarText.length}/{MAX_PILLAR_CHARS}</span></div><p className=\"text-sm text-secondary-text mb-4\">Paste a blog post, transcript, or long caption.</p><textarea id=\"pillar\" value={pillarText} maxLength={MAX_PILLAR_CHARS} onChange={e => setPillarText(e.target.value.slice(0, MAX_PILLAR_CHARS))} placeholder=\"Paste your pillar content\u2026\" className=\"w-full min-h-[320px] p-4 rounded-lg bg-surface border border-border focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent-blue resize-y\"/>{error && <div role=\"alert\" className=\"mt-4 flex gap-3 rounded-lg bg-accent-red-soft p-4 text-accent-red\"><FiAlertCircle className=\"shrink-0 mt-1\"/><div><p className=\"text-sm font-medium\">{error}</p><p className=\"text-xs mt-1 opacity-80\">Your pillar text is still here. Check the message and try again.</p></div></div>}<button type=\"submit\" disabled={!voiceProfile || !pillarText.trim() || loading} className=\"mt-6 w-full min-h-12 inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 bg-accent-blue text-white font-semibold rounded-lg disabled:opacity-50\">{loading ? <><FiRefreshCw className=\"animate-spin\"/>Matching voice across five formats\u2026</> : \"Generate All 5 Platforms\"}</button><p className=\"mt-3 text-center text-xs text-secondary-text\">Initial generation uses one batched request. Authenticity scoring uses no API calls.</p></form>\n67 |       <aside className=\"space-y-4\"><div className=\"bg-canvas rounded-xl border border-border p-5\"><p className=\"text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-secondary-text mb-2\">Voice profile</p>{voiceProfile ? <><p className=\"font-medium\">{voiceProfile.tone}</p><p className=\"mt-2 text-sm text-secondary-text\">{voiceProfile.sentence_rhythm}</p><Link href=\"/voice-profile\" className=\"inline-block mt-3 text-sm font-medium text-accent-blue\">Edit profile</Link></> : <p className=\"text-sm text-secondary-text\">No saved profile found.</p>}</div><div className=\"bg-accent-blue-soft rounded-xl p-5 flex gap-3\"><FiShield className=\"text-accent-blue shrink-0 mt-1\"/><div><p className=\"font-semibold\">Cost-aware refinement</p><p className=\"text-sm text-secondary-text mt-1\">A tweak calls Gemini only for that one card. Authenticity checks stay local.</p></div></div></aside>\n68 |     </div>\n69 | \n70 |     {loading && <section className=\"mt-10\" aria-label=\"Generating drafts\"><div className=\"h-7 w-64 rounded bg-surface-2 animate-pulse mb-5\"/><div className=\"grid xl:grid-cols-2 gap-6\">{Array.from({length:5}).map((_,i)=><div key={i} className=\"h-64 rounded-xl border border-border bg-canvas p-6\"><div className=\"h-5 w-40 rounded bg-surface-2 animate-pulse\"/><div className=\"mt-8 space-y-3\">{[1,2,3,4].map(n=><div key={n} className=\"h-4 rounded bg-surface-2 animate-pulse\" style={{width:`${92-n*7}%`}}/>)}</div></div>)}</div></section>}\n71 | \n72 |     {outputs && <section className=\"mt-10\"><div className=\"flex flex-col sm:flex-row sm:items-end justify-between gap-4 mb-5\"><div><div className={`mb-2 inline-flex rounded-md px-2.5 py-1 text-xs font-semibold ${mode === \"ai\" ? \"bg-accent-green-soft text-accent-green\" : \"bg-accent-orange-soft text-accent-orange\"}`}>{mode === \"ai\" ? `AI output${model ? ` \u00b7 ${model}` : \"\"}` : \"Mock output \u00b7 no API call used\"}</div><h2 className=\"text-2xl font-bold\">Your platform-ready drafts</h2><p className=\"text-sm text-secondary-text mt-1\">Use Tweak only where needed\u2014each tweak is one additional call.</p></div><button onClick={exportAll} className=\"min-h-11 inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-lg bg-primary-text text-white px-4 text-sm font-semibold\"><FiDownload/>Export all (.txt)</button></div><div className=\"grid xl:grid-cols-2 gap-6 items-start\">\n73 |       <PlatformOutputCard title=\"LinkedIn post\" subtitle=\"Professional, human, 1,200\u20131,500 characters\" content={outputs.linkedin} icon={<FiLinkedin/>} authenticity={analyzeAuthenticity(outputs.linkedin)} onRegenerate={t=>regenerate(\"linkedin\",t)}/>\n74 |       <PlatformOutputCard title=\"X thread\" subtitle={`${outputs.xThread.length} numbered posts \u00b7 max 280 characters each`} content={formatXThread(outputs.xThread)} icon={<FiTwitter/>} accentClass=\"bg-primary-text text-white\" authenticity={analyzeAuthenticity(formatXThread(outputs.xThread))} onRegenerate={t=>regenerate(\"x\",t)}/>\n75 |       <PlatformOutputCard title=\"Instagram caption\" subtitle=\"Conversational, [...prev, createEmptySample()] : prev));\n53 |   };\n54 | \n55 |   const removeSample = (id: string) => {\n56 |     setSamples((prev) => {\n57 |       if (prev.length <= 1) return prev;\n58 |       return prev.filter((sample) => sample.id !== id);\n59 |     });\n60 |   };\n61 | \n62 |   const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n63 |     e.preventDefault();\n64 |     setError(null);\n65 | \n66 |     const nonEmpty = samples.map((s) => s.text.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n67 |     if (nonEmpty.length < 3) {\n68 |       setError(\"Please paste at least 3 writing samples so we can capture your voice.\");\n69 |       return;\n70 |     }\n71 | \n72 |     const tooLong = nonEmpty.findIndex((s) => s.length > MAX_CHARS);\n73 |     if (tooLong !== -1) {\n74 |       setError(`Sample ${tooLong + 1} exceeds the ${MAX_CHARS}-character limit. Please try again.\");\n99 |     } finally {\n100 |       setLoading(false);\n101 |     }\n102 |   };\n103 | \n104 |   const clearProfile = () => {\n105 |     localStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY);\n106 |     setProfile(null);\n107 |     setSamples([createEmptySample()]);\n108 |   };\n109 | \n110 |   return (\n111 |     <div className=\"max-w-3xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 py-10 sm:py-16\">\n112 |       <div className=\"mb-8\">\n113 |         <h1 className=\"text-3xl sm:text-4xl font-bold text-primary-text tracking-tight mb-3\">\n114 |           Build Your Voice Profile\n115 |         </h1>\n116 |         <p className=\"text-secondary-text text-lg leading-relaxed\">\n117 |           Paste 3\u20135 samples of your writing. EchoDraft will extract the tone, vocabulary, and rhythm that make your voice yours.\n118 |         </p>\n119 |       </div>\n120 | \n121 |       <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"bg-canvas rounded-xl border border-border shadow-sm p-6 sm:p-8\">\n122 |         <div className=\"space-y-4\">\n123 |           {samples.map((sample, index) => (\n124 |             <div key={sample.id}>\n125 |               <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between mb-2\">\n126 |                 <label htmlFor={`${formId}-${sample.id}`} className=\"text-sm font-semibold text-primary-text\">\n127 |                   Sample {index + 1}\n128 |                 </label>\n129 |                 <div className=\"flex items-center gap-3\">\n130 |                   <span className=\"text-xs      <button\n135 |                       type=\"button\"\n136 |                       onClick={() => removeSample(sample.id)}\n137 |                       className=\"text-secondary-text The truth? Most of us are winging it. And that\u2019s okay.\",\n9 |   \"Three things I learned this week: 1) sleep is non-negotiable, 2) your audience can smell authenticity, and 3) done is better than perfect.\",\n10 |   \"Stop writing like you\u2019re pitching a VC. Write like you\u2019re explaining it to the person next to you at a coffee shop. That\u2019s the post that actually connects.\",\n11 | ];\n12 | \n13 | /** Mirrors the validation logic in app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts */\n14 | function validateSamples(raw: unknown): { ok: true; samples: string[] } | { ok: false; status: number; error: string } {\n15 |   const samples = Array.isArray(raw)\n16 |     ? raw.filter((s: unknown) => typeof s === \"string\" && s.trim().length > 0)\n17 |     : [];\n18 | \n19 |   if (samples.length < 3) {\n20 |     return { ok: false, status: 400, error: \"Please provide at least 3 writing samples.\" };\n21 |   }\n22 |   if (samples.length > 5) {\n23 |     return { ok: false, status: 400, error: \"Please provide at most 5 writing samples.\" };\n24 |   }\n25 |   const tooLong = samples.findIndex((s: string) => s.length > MAX_CHARS);\n26 |   if (tooLong !== -1) {\n27 |     return {\n28 |       ok: false,\n29 |       status: 400,\n30 |       error: `Sample ${tooLong + 1} exceeds the ${MAX_CHARS}-character limit. Please shorten it and try again.`,\n31 |     };\n32 |   }\n33 |   return { ok: true, samples };\n34 | }\n35 | \n36 | async function run() {\n37 |   console.log(\"Running Phase 2 tests...\\n\");\n38 | \n39 |   // Test 1: mock profile without API key\n40 |   console.log(\"Test 1: callGeminiForVoiceProfile with no API key returns mock profile\");\n41 |   const profile = await callGeminiForVoiceProfile(samples, undefined);\n42 |   assert(typeof profile.tone === \"string\" && profile.tone.length > 0, \"tone should be a non-empty string\");\n43 |   assert(typeof profile.vocabulary === \"string\" && profile.vocabulary.length > 0, \"vocabulary should be a non-empty string\");\n44 |   assert(typeof profile.sentence_rhythm === \"string\" && profile.sentence_rhythm.length > 0, \"sentence_rhythm should be a non-empty string\");\n45 |   assert(Array.isArray(profile.dos) && profile.dos.length > 0, \"dos should be a non-empty array\");\n46 |   assert(Array.isArray(profile.donts) && profile.donts.length > 0, \"donts should be a non-empty array\");\n47 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n48 | \n49 |   // Test 2: JSON extraction helper\n50 |   console.log(\"Test 2: extractJsonFromText handles markdown wrapping\");\n51 |   const wrapped = `Here is the profile:\\n\\n\\`\\`\\`json\\n{\"tone\":\"friendly\",\"vocabulary\":\"simple\",\"sentence_rhythm\":\"short\",\"dos\":[\"be honest\"],\"donts\":[\"be generic\"]}\\n\\`\\`\\``;\n52 |   const extracted = extractJsonFromText(wrapped) as Record<string, unknown>;\n53 |   assert(extracted.tone === \"friendly\", \"extracted tone should be 'friendly'\");\n54 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n55 | \n56 |   // Test 3: validation rejects <3 samples\n57 |   console.log(\"Test 3: API route validation rejects <3 samples\");\n58 |   const tooFew = validateSamples([\"only one sample\"]);\n59 |   assert(!tooFew.ok && tooFew.status === 400, \"should reject fewer than 3 samples with 400\");\n60 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n61 | \n62 |   // Test 4: MAX_CHARS guard\n63 |   console.log(`Test 4: API route validation rejects samples longer than ${MAX_CHARS} chars`);\n64 |   const oversized = \"x\".repeat(MAX_CHARS + 1);\n65 |   const tooLong = validateSamples([samples[0], samples[1], oversized]);\n66 |   assert(!tooLong.ok && tooLong.status === 400, \"should reject oversized sample with 400\");\n67 |   assert(tooLong.ok === false && tooLong.error.includes(String(MAX_CHARS)), \"error should mention MAX_CHARS\");\n68 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n69 | \n70 |   // Test 5: valid samples pass validation\n71 |   console.log(\"Test 5: valid samples pass validation\");\n72 |   const ok = validateSamples(samples);\n73 |   assert(ok.ok === true && ok.samples.length === 3, \"valid samples should pass\");\n74 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n75 | \n76 |   // Test 6: MAX_CHARS constant is exported and equals 2000\n77 |   console.log(\"Test 6: MAX_CHARS is 2000\");\n78 |   assert(MAX_CHARS === 2000, \"MAX_CHARS should be 2000\");\n79 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n80 | \n81 |   console.log(\"All Phase 2 logic tests passed.\");\n82 | }\n83 | \n84 | function assert(condition: boolean, message: string) {\n85 |   if (!condition) {\n86 |     console.error(\"Assertion failed:\", message);\n87 |     process.exit(1);\n88 |   }\n89 | }\n90 | \n91 | run().catch((err) => {\n92 |   console.error(\"Test failed:\", err);\n93 |   process.exit(1);\n94 | });\n95 | ",

  "role": "tool",
  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/lib/gemini.ts:\n\n1 | export const DEFAULT_VOICE_MODEL = \"gemini-2.5-flash\";\n2 | \n3 | /** Max characters allowed per writing sample (API + UI guard). */\n4 | export const MAX_CHARS = 2000;\n5 | \n6 | export type VoiceProfile = {\n7 |   tone: string;\n8 |   vocabulary: string;\n9 |   sentence_rhythm: string;\n10 |   dos: string[];\n11 |   donts: string[];\n12 | };\n13 | \n14 | export function buildVoiceProfilePrompt(samples: string[]): string {\n15 |   return `Analyze these writing samples and extract a voice profile as a JSON object with exactly these fields:\n16 | - tone (string, e.g. \"conversational, direct, warm\")\n17 | - vocabulary (string, e.g. \"plain English, avoids jargon, uses analogies\")\n18 | - sentence_rhythm (string, e.g. \"short punchy sentences, varies length\")\n19 | - dos (array of strings, style traits to keep)\n20 | - donts (array of strings, things to avoid)\n21 | \n22 | Be specific and concise. Return only valid JSON. Do not wrap it in markdown code fences.\n23 | \n24 | Samples:\n25 | ${samples.map((s, i) => `--- Sample ${i + 1} ---\\n${s}`).join(\"\\n\\n\")}`;\n26 | }\n27 | \n28 | export function extractJsonFromText(text: string): unknown {\n29 |   // Try to find a JSON object inside the text if the model adds extra prose\n30 |   const firstBrace = text.indexOf(\"{\");\n31 |   const lastBrace = text.lastIndexOf(\"}\");\n32 |   if (firstBrace !== -1 && lastBrace !== -1 && lastBrace > firstBrace) {\n33 |     const candidate = text.slice(firstBrace, lastBrace + 1);\n34 |     try {\n35 |       return JSON.parse(candidate);\n36 |     } catch {\n37 |       // fall through to raw parse\n38 |     }\n39 |   }\n40 |   return JSON.parse(text);\n41 | }\n42 | \n43 | export async function callGeminiForVoiceProfile(\n44 |   samples: string[],\n45 |   apiKey: string | undefined,\n46 |   model = DEFAULT_VOICE_MODEL\n47 | ): Promise<VoiceProfile> {\n48 |   if (!apiKey) {\n49 |     return mockVoiceProfile(samples);\n50 |   }\n51 | \n52 |   const prompt = buildVoiceProfilePrompt(samples);\n53 |   const safeModel = model.trim() || DEFAULT_VOICE_MODEL;\n54 |   const endpoint = \"https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/\" + encodeURIComponent(safeModel) + \":generateContent\";\n55 |   // Pass the key via header so it is not written into server access logs as a query string.\n56 |   const res = await fetch(endpoint, {\n57 |     method: \"POST\",\n58 |     headers: {\n59 |       \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n60 |       \"x-goog-api-key\": apiKey,\n61 |     },\n62 |     body: JSON.stringify({\n63 |       contents: [{ parts: [{ text: prompt }] }],\n64 |       generationConfig: {\n65 |         responseMimeType: \"application/json\",\n66 |         temperature: 0.4,\n67 |       },\n68 |     }),\n69 |   });\n70 | \n71 |   if (!res.ok) {\n72 |     const text = await res.text();\n73 |     throw new Error(`Gemini API error (${res.status}): ${text.slice(0, 200)}`);\n74 |   }\n75 | \n76 |   const data = await res.json();\n77 |   const text = data?.candidates?.[0]?.content?.parts?.[0]?.text ?? \"\";\n78 |   if (!text) {\n79 |     throw new Error(\"Gemini returned an empty response.\");\n80 |   }\n81 | \n82 |   const parsed = extractJsonFromText(text) as Partial<VoiceProfile>;\n83 |   return normalizeVoiceProfile(parsed, samples);\n84 | }\n85 | \n86 | function normalizeVoiceProfile(parsed: Partial<VoiceProfile>, samples: string[]): VoiceProfile {\n87 |   const safeString = (val: unknown): string =>\n88 |     typeof val === \"string\" ? val : Array.isArray(val) ? val.join(\" \") : \"\";\n89 | \n90 |   const safeArray = (val: unknown): string[] => {\n91 |     if (Array.isArray(val)) return val.filter((v) => typeof v === \"string\") as string[];\n92 |     if (typeof val === \"string\") return [val];\n93 |     return [];\n94 |   };\n95 | \n96 |   return {\n97 |     tone: safeString(parsed.tone) || \"Conversational and authentic\",\n98 |     vocabulary: safeString(parsed.vocabulary) || \"Plain, accessible language\",\n99 |     sentence_rhythm:\n100 |       safeString(parsed.sentence_rhythm) || \"Mixed sentence length with natural pauses\",\n101 |     dos: safeArray(parsed.dos).length ? safeArray(parsed.dos) : [\"Keep it personal\", \"Use concrete examples\"],\n102 |     donts: safeArray(parsed.donts).length\n103 |       ? safeArray(parsed.donts)\n104 |       : [\"Avoid generic buzzwords\", \"Don't over-polish\"],\n105 |   };\n106 | }\n107 | \n108 | function mockVoiceProfile(samples: string[]): VoiceProfile {\n109 |   const totalLength = samples.reduce((acc, s) => acc + s.length, 0);\n110 |   const avgLength = totalLength / samples.length;\n111 |   const hasQuestions = samples.some((s) => s.includes(\"?\"));\n112 | \n113 |   return {\n114 |     tone: hasQuestions\n115 |       ? \"Conversational, curious, and warm \u2014 asks the reader to think along\"\n116 |       : \"Direct, clear, and confident \u2014 gets to the point without fluff\",\n117 |     vocabulary:\n118 |       avgLength > 400\n119 |         ? \"Detailed vocabulary with full sentences; prefers explanation over shorthand\"\n120 |         : \"Plain, punchy words; favors clarity over jargon\",\n121 |     sentence_rhythm:\n122 |       avgLength > 400\n123 |         ? \"Longer, flowing sentences with occasional short breaks for emphasis\"\n124 |         : \"Short to medium sentences with natural line breaks for readability\",\n125 |     dos: [\n126 |       \"Lead with the main point\",\n127 |       \"Use the same everyday words found in your samples\",\n128 |       \"Write like you're talking to one person\",\n129 |     ],\n130 |     donts: [\n131 |       \"Don't switch to corporate-speak\",\n132 |       \"Don't pad the message with filler\",\n133 |       \"Don't lose the warm, human edge\",\n134 |     ],\n135 |   };\n136 | }\n137 | ",

  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/components/PlatformOutputCard.tsx:\n\n1 | \"use client\";\n2 | \n3 | import { useId, useState, type ReactNode } from \"react\";\n4 | import { FiAlertTriangle, FiCheck, FiCopy, FiRefreshCw, FiSliders } from \"react-icons/fi\";\n5 | import type { AuthenticityResult } from \"@/lib/authenticity\";\n6 | import { MAX_TWEAK_CHARS } from \"@/lib/refinement\";\n7 | \n8 | type Props = {\n9 |   title: string;\n10 |   subtitle: string;\n11 |   content: string;\n12 |   icon: ReactNode;\n13 |   authenticity: AuthenticityResult;\n14 |   onRegenerate: (tweak: string) => Promise<void>;\n15 |   accentClass?: string;\n16 | };\n17 | \n18 | export function PlatformOutputCard({ title, subtitle, content, icon, authenticity, onRegenerate, accentClass = \"bg-accent-blue-soft text-accent-blue\" }: Props) {\n19 |   const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);\n20 |   const [editing, setEditing] = useState(false);\n21 |   const [tweak, setTweak] = useState(\"\");\n22 |   const [working, setWorking] = useState(false);\n23 |   const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n24 |   const titleId = useId();\n25 | \n26 |   const copy = async () => {\n27 |     try { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(content); setCopied(true); window.setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 1800); } catch { setCopied(false); }\n28 |   };\n29 |   const refine = async () => {\n30 |     if (!tweak.trim()) return setError(\"Enter a tweak first.\");\n31 |     setWorking(true); setError(null);\n32 |     try { await onRegenerate(tweak.trim()); setEditing(false); setTweak(\"\"); }\n33 |     catch (err) { setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Could not regenerate this draft.\"); }\n34 |     finally { setWorking(false); }\n35 |   };\n36 |   const scoreClass = authenticity.label === \"Strong\" ? \"bg-accent-green-soft text-accent-green\" : authenticity.label === \"Review\" ? \"bg-accent-orange-soft text-accent-orange\" : \"bg-accent-red-soft text-accent-red\";\n37 | \n38 |   return <section className=\"bg-canvas rounded-xl border border-border shadow-sm overflow-hidden\" aria-labelledby={titleId}>\n39 |     <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between gap-3 px-5 sm:px-6 py-4 border-b border-border\">\n40 |       <div className=\"flex items-center gap-3 min-w-0\"><div className={`w-9 h-9 shrink-0 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-center ${accentClass}`}>{icon}</div><div className=\"min-w-0\"><h2 id={titleId} className=\"font-semibold\">{title}</h2><p className=\"text-xs text-secondary-text truncate\">{subtitle}</p></div></div>\n41 |       <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2\"><button type=\"button\" onClick={() => setEditing(v => !v)} className=\"min-h-11 inline-flex items-center gap-2 rounded-lg border border-border px-3 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-surface\"><FiSliders/><span className=\"hidden sm:inline\">Tweak</span></button><button type=\"button\" onClick={copy} className=\"min-h-11 inline-flex items-center gap-2 rounded-lg border border-border px-3 text-sm font-medium hover:bg-surface\">{copied ? <FiCheck className=\"text-accent-green\"/> : <FiCopy/>}<span className=\"hidden sm:inline\">{copied ? \"Copied\" : \"Copy\"}</span></button></div>\n42 |     </div>\n43 |     {editing && <div className=\"border-b border-border bg-surface p-4\"><label className=\"text-sm font-semibold\">Regenerate with a tweak</label><div className=\"mt-2 flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-2\"><input value={tweak} maxLength={MAX_TWEAK_CHARS} onChange={e => setTweak(e.target.value)} placeholder=\"Make it punchier, shorter, or more casual\u2026\" className=\"min-h-11 flex-1 rounded-lg border border-border bg-white px-3 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent-blue\"/><button type=\"button\" onClick={refine} disabled={working || !tweak.trim()} className=\"min-h-11 rounded-lg bg-accent-blue text-white px-4 font-semibold disabled:opacity-50\">{working ? <span className=\"inline-flex items-center gap-2\"><FiRefreshCw className=\"animate-spin\"/>Rewriting\u2026</span> : \"Apply tweak\"}</button></div>{error && <p className=\"mt-2 text-sm text-accent-red\">{error}</p>}</div>}\n44 |     <div className=\"p-5 sm:p-6\"><div className=\"mb-4 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2\"><span className={`rounded-md px-2 py-1 text-xs font-semibold ${scoreClass}`}>Authenticity {authenticity.score}/100 \u00b7 {authenticity.label}</span>{authenticity.flags.length > 0 && <span className=\"inline-flex items-center gap-1 text-xs text-secondary-text\"><FiAlertTriangle/> {authenticity.flags.length} suggestion{authenticity.flags.length === 1 ? \"\" : \"s\"}</span>}</div>{authenticity.flags.length > 0 && <details className=\"mb-4 text-sm\"><summary className=\"cursor-pointer text-secondary-text\">Review authenticity suggestions</summary><ul className=\"mt-2 list-disc pl-5 text-secondary-text\">{authenticity.flags.map(flag => <li key={flag}>{flag}</li>)}</ul></details>}<p className=\"whitespace-pre-wrap leading-7\">{content}</p></div>\n45 |   </section>;\n46 | }\n47 | ",

  "role": "tool",
  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/lib/authenticity.ts:\n\n1 | export type AuthenticityResult = {\n2 |   score: number;\n3 |   label: \"Strong\" | \"Review\" | \"Generic\";\n4 |   flags: string[];\n5 | };\n6 | \n7 | const GENERIC_PHRASES = [\n8 |   \"in today's fast-paced world\",\n9 |   \"unlock the power of\",\n10 |   \"game-changer\",\n11 |   \"delve into\",\n12 |   \"revolutionize\",\n13 |   \"ever-evolving landscape\",\n14 |   \"it is important to note\",\n15 |   \"in conclusion\",\n16 |   \"leverage synergies\",\n17 |   \"transformative journey\",\n18 | ];\n19 | \n20 | export function analyzeAuthenticity(content: string): AuthenticityResult {\n21 |   const lower = content.toLowerCase();\n22 |   const flags: string[] = [];\n23 |   for (const phrase of GENERIC_PHRASES) {\n24 |     if (lower.includes(phrase)) flags.push(`Generic phrase: \u201c${phrase}\u201d`);\n25 |   }\n26 | \n27 |   const words = content.trim().split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n28 |   const sentences = content.split(/[.!?]+/).map((item) => item.trim()).filter(Boolean);\n29 |   const avgSentenceWords = sentences.length ? words.length / sentences.length : words.length;\n30 |   if (avgSentenceWords > 28) flags.push(\"Several sentences may feel overly long\");\n31 |   if ((content.match(/!/g) || []).length > 6) flags.push(\"Heavy exclamation-mark use may feel automated\");\n32 |   if (words.length > 40 && !/[?]/.test(content)) flags.push(\"Consider adding a natural question or direct reader cue\");\n33 | \n34 |   const score = Math.max(35, 100 - flags.length * 14);\n35 |   return { score, label: score >= 86 ? \"Strong\" : score >= 65 ? \"Review\" : \"Generic\", flags };\n36 | }\n37 | ",

  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/lib/refinement.ts:\n\n1 | import type { VoiceProfile } from \"./gemini\";\n2 | import { DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL, formatXThread, MAX_TWEET_CHARS, type PlatformId } from \"./generation\";\n3 | \n4 | export const MAX_TWEAK_CHARS = 200;\n5 | export type RefineInput = {\n6 |   platform: PlatformId;\n7 |   tweak: string;\n8 |   currentOutput: string | string[];\n9 |   pillarText: string;\n10 |   voiceProfile: VoiceProfile;\n11 | };\n12 | \n13 | const RULES: Record<PlatformId, string> = {\n14 |   linkedin: \"Return one LinkedIn post, 1,200\u20131,500 characters, max 3 hashtags.\",\n15 |   x: \"Return JSON with xThread containing 3\u20135 tweet strings, each <=280 characters and without numbering.\",\n16 |   instagram: \"Return one 150\u2013300 word Instagram caption ending with 5\u201310 hashtags.\",\n17 |   youtube: \"Return one 200\u2013300 word YouTube description with TIMESTAMPS and LINKS headings.\",\n18 |   newsletter: \"Return one newsletter beginning Subject:, followed by a 100\u2013150 word body and one CTA.\",\n19 | };\n20 | \n21 | export function validateRefineInput(value: unknown): { ok: true; value: RefineInput } | { ok: false; error: string } {\n22 |   if (!value || typeof value !== \"object\") return { ok: false, error: \"Invalid refinement request.\" };\n23 |   const v = value as Partial<RefineInput>;\n24 |   if (!([\"linkedin\", \"x\", \"instagram\", \"youtube\", \"newsletter\"] as string[]).includes(String(v.platform))) return { ok: false, error: \"Invalid platform.\" };\n25 |   const tweak = typeof v.tweak === \"string\" ? v.tweak.trim() : \"\";\n26 |   if (!tweak) return { ok: false, error: \"Enter a tweak such as \u201cmake it punchier\u201d.\" };\n27 |   if (tweak.length > MAX_TWEAK_CHARS) return { ok: false, error: `Tweak exceeds ${MAX_TWEAK_CHARS} characters.` };\n28 |   if (!v.voiceProfile || !v.pillarText || !v.currentOutput) return { ok: false, error: \"Refinement context is incomplete.\" };\n29 |   return { ok: true, value: { ...v, tweak } as RefineInput };\n30 | }\n31 | \n32 | export function buildRefinePrompt(input: RefineInput): string {\n33 |   const current = Array.isArray(input.currentOutput) ? formatXThread(input.currentOutput) : input.currentOutput;\n34 |   return `Revise only the ${input.platform} output using the requested tweak.\n35 | \n36 | VOICE LOCK\n37 | Tone: ${input.voiceProfile.tone}\n38 | Vocabulary: ${input.voiceProfile.vocabulary}\n39 | Rhythm: ${input.voiceProfile.sentence_rhythm}\n40 | Always do: ${input.voiceProfile.dos.join(\"; \")}\n41 | Never do: ${input.voiceProfile.donts.join(\"; \")}\n42 | \n43 | REQUESTED TWEAK\n44 | ${input.tweak}\n45 | \n46 | PLATFORM RULE\n47 | ${RULES[input.platform]}\n48 | \n49 | SOURCE FACTS\n50 | ${input.pillarText}\n51 | \n52 | CURRENT OUTPUT\n53 | ${current}\n54 | \n55 | Preserve source facts. Do not invent claims, quotes, links, or experiences. Return only the revised output${input.platform === \"x\" ? \" as JSON\" : \"\"}.`;\n56 | }\n57 | \n58 | export async function refinePlatformOutput(input: RefineInput, apiKey: string | undefined, model = DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL): Promise<{ output: string | string[]; mode: \"ai\" | \"mock\" }> {\n59 |   if (!apiKey) return { output: mockRefine(input), mode: \"mock\" };\n60 |   const endpoint = \"https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/\" + encodeURIComponent(model) + \":generateContent\";\n61 |   const isX = input.platform === \"x\";\n62 |   const response = await fetch(endpoint, {\n63 |     method: \"POST\",\n64 |     headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-goog-api-key\": apiKey },\n65 |     body: JSON.stringify({\n66 |       contents: [{ parts: [{ text: buildRefinePrompt(input) }] }],\n67 |       generationConfig: isX ? { responseMimeType: \"application/json\", responseSchema: { type: \"OBJECT\", properties: { xThread: { type: \"ARRAY\", items: { type: \"STRING\" } } }, required: [\"xThread\"] }, temperature: 0.65, maxOutputTokens: 1800 } : { temperature: 0.65, maxOutputTokens: 1800 },\n68 |     }),\n69 |   });\n70 |   if (!response.ok) throw new Error(response.status === 429 ? \"Gemini rate limit reached. Wait briefly, then try again.\" : `Refinement failed (${response.status}).`);\n71 |   const data = await response.json();\n72 |   const text = data?.candidates?.[0]?.content?.parts?.[0]?.text?.trim();\n73 |   if (!text) throw new Error(\"Gemini returned an empty refinement.\");\n74 |   if (!isX) return { output: text, mode: \"ai\" };\n75 |   const tweets = JSON.parse(text)?.xThread;\n76 |   if (!Array.isArray(tweets) || tweets.length < 3) throw new Error(\"Gemini returned an invalid X thread.\");\n77 |   return { output: tweets.slice(0, 5).map((tweet: unknown) => String(tweet).slice(0, MAX_TWEET_CHARS)), mode: \"ai\" };\n78 | }\n79 | \n80 | function mockRefine(input: RefineInput): string | string[] {\n81 |   const suffix = `\\n\\n[Mock tweak applied: ${input.tweak}]`;\n82 |   return Array.isArray(input.currentOutput) ? input.currentOutput.map((item, index) => index === 0 ? `${item} (${input.tweak})`.slice(0, MAX_TWEET_CHARS) : item) : input.currentOutput + suffix;\n83 | }\n84 | ",

  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/lib/generation.ts:\n\n1 | import type { VoiceProfile } from \"./gemini\";\n2 | \n3 | export const DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL = \"gemini-2.5-flash\";\n4 | export const MAX_PILLAR_CHARS = 12000;\n5 | export const MAX_TWEET_CHARS = 280;\n6 | export type GenerationMode = \"mock\" | \"ai\";\n7 | export type PlatformOutputs = { linkedin: string; xThread: string[]; instagram: string; youtube: string; newsletter: string };\n8 | export type GenerateAllInput = { pillarText: string; voiceProfile: VoiceProfile };\n9 | export type AllPlatformGeneration = { outputs: PlatformOutputs; mode: GenerationMode; model: string | null };\n10 | \n11 | export function isVoiceProfile(value: unknown): value is VoiceProfile {\n12 |   if (!value || typeof value !== \"object\") return false;\n13 |   const v = value as Record<string, unknown>;\n14 |   return typeof v.tone === \"string\" && typeof v.vocabulary === \"string\" && typeof v.sentence_rhythm === \"string\" && Array.isArray(v.dos) && v.dos.every(x => typeof x === \"string\") && Array.isArray(v.donts) && v.donts.every(x => typeof x === \"string\");\n15 | }\n16 | \n17 | export function validateGenerateInput(input: Partial<GenerateAllInput> | null | undefined): { ok: true; value: GenerateAllInput } | { ok: false; error: string } {\n18 |   const pillarText = typeof input?.pillarText === \"string\" ? input.pillarText.trim() : \"\";\n19 |   if (!pillarText) return { ok: false, error: \"Pillar content is required.\" };\n20 |   if (pillarText.length > MAX_PILLAR_CHARS) return { ok: false, error: `Pillar content exceeds the ${MAX_PILLAR_CHARS}-character limit.` };\n21 |   if (!isVoiceProfile(input?.voiceProfile)) return { ok: false, error: \"A valid voice profile is required.\" };\n22 |   return { ok: true, value: { pillarText, voiceProfile: input.voiceProfile } };\n23 | }\n24 | \n25 | export function buildAllPlatformsPrompt({ pillarText, voiceProfile }: GenerateAllInput): string {\n26 |   return `Repurpose one pillar piece into five platform-native outputs while preserving the creator's voice and all factual claims.\n27 | \n28 | VOICE PROFILE\n29 | ${JSON.stringify(voiceProfile, null, 2)}\n30 | \n31 | VOICE MATCH PRIORITY\n32 | - Treat the voice profile as a hard writing constraint, not a loose suggestion.\n33 | - Reuse the profile's characteristic pacing, directness, vocabulary level, and rhetorical habits.\n34 | - Check every draft against every item in dos and don'ts before returning it.\n35 | - Avoid generic AI phrases such as \u201cin today's fast-paced world\u201d, \u201cunlock the power of\u201d, \u201cdelve into\u201d, \u201cgame-changer\u201d, and \u201cin conclusion\u201d.\n36 | - Prefer concrete language from the pillar over polished but vague language.\n37 | \n38 | GLOBAL RULES\n39 | - Never invent facts, statistics, quotes, links, timestamps, or experiences.\n40 | - Match the tone, vocabulary, rhythm, dos, and don'ts.\n41 | - Make every platform structurally distinct; do not copy-paste.\n42 | - Return JSON only.\n43 | \n44 | PLATFORM RULES\n45 | linkedin: 1,200\u20131,500 characters; strong hook; short paragraphs; professional but human; end with a question/CTA; max 3 hashtags.\n46 | xThread: array of 3\u20135 unnumbered tweet strings; each <=280 characters; strong first hook; final tweet has CTA/takeaway.\n47 | instagram: 150\u2013300 words; first-line hook; sparing relevant emoji; final line has 5\u201310 hashtags.\n48 | youtube: 200\u2013300 words; keyword-rich first paragraph; literal TIMESTAMPS and LINKS headings; placeholder timestamps; no invented URLs; end with CTA.\n49 | newsletter: begins Subject:; 100\u2013150 body words; personal opener; one clear CTA.\n50 | \n51 | PILLAR CONTENT\n52 | ${pillarText}`;\n53 | }\n54 | \n55 | const responseSchema = {\n56 |   type: \"OBJECT\",\n57 |   properties: {\n58 |     linkedin: { type: \"STRING\" },\n59 |     xThread: { type: \"ARRAY\", items: { type: \"STRING\" } },\n60 |     instagram: { type: \"STRING\" },\n61 |     youtube: { type: \"STRING\" },\n62 |     newsletter: { type: \"STRING\" },\n63 |   },\n64 |   required: [\"linkedin\", \"xThread\", \"instagram\", \"youtube\", \"newsletter\"],\n65 | };\n66 | \n67 | export async function generateAllPlatformPosts(input: GenerateAllInput, apiKey: string | undefined, model = DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL): Promise<AllPlatformGeneration> {\n68 |   if (!apiKey) return { outputs: buildMockOutputs(input), mode: \"mock\", model: null };\n69 |   const safeModel = model.trim() || DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL;\n70 |   const endpoint = \"https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/\" + encodeURIComponent(safeModel) + \":generateContent\";\n71 |   const response = await fetch(endpoint, {\n72 |     method: \"POST\",\n73 |     headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-goog-api-key\": apiKey },\n74 |     body: JSON.stringify({\n75 |       contents: [{ parts: [{ text: buildAllPlatformsPrompt(input) }] }],\n76 |       generationConfig: { responseMimeType: \"application/json\", responseSchema, temperature: 0.65, maxOutputTokens: 5000 },\n77 |     }),\n78 |   });\n79 |   if (!response.ok) {\n80 |     const body = await response.text();\n81 |     if (response.status === 401 || response.status === 403) throw new Error(\"Gemini authentication failed. Check the server-side API key and restrictions.\");\n82 |     if (response.status === 429) throw new Error(\"Gemini rate limit reached. Wait briefly, then try again.\");\n83 |     if (response.status === 400) throw new Error(\"Gemini rejected the request. Check GEMINI_MODEL and request format.\");\n84 |     throw new Error(`Gemini API error (${response.status}): ${body.slice(0, 160)}`);\n85 |   }\n86 |   const data = await response.json();\n87 |   const raw = data?.candidates?.[0]?.content?.parts?.[0]?.text;\n88 |   if (typeof raw !== \"string\" || !raw.trim()) throw new Error(\"Gemini returned an empty response.\");\n89 |   let parsed: unknown;\n90 |   try { parsed = JSON.parse(raw); } catch { throw new Error(\"Gemini returned invalid JSON. Please try once more.\"); }\n91 |   return { outputs: normalizePlatformOutputs(parsed), mode: \"ai\", model: safeModel };\n92 | }\n93 | \n94 | export function normalizePlatformOutputs(value: unknown): PlatformOutputs {\n95 |   if (!value || typeof value !== \"object\") throw new Error(\"Generation response is not an object.\");\n96 |   const v = value as Record<string, unknown>;\n97 |   const str = (key: \"linkedin\" | \"instagram\" | \"youtube\" | \"newsletter\") => {\n98 |     const x = v[key];\n99 |     if (typeof x !== \"string\" || !x.trim()) throw new Error(`Generation response is missing ${key}.`);\n100 |     return x.trim();\n101 |   };\n102 |   if (!Array.isArray(v.xThread)) throw new Error(\"Generation response is missing xThread.\");\n103 |   const xThread = v.xThread.filter((x): x is string => typeof x === \"string\" && !!x.trim()).slice(0, 5).map(x => clampTweet(x.trim()));\n104 |   if (xThread.length < 3) throw new Error(\"Generation response must contain at least 3 X posts.\");\n105 |   return { linkedin: str(\"linkedin\"), xThread, instagram: str(\"instagram\"), youtube: str(\"youtube\"), newsletter: str(\"newsletter\") };\n106 | }\n107 | \n108 | export function formatXThread(tweets: string[]): string { return tweets.map((t, i) => `${i + 1}/${tweets.length} ${t}`).join(\"\\n\\n\"); }\n109 | export function formatAllOutputs(o: PlatformOutputs): string {\n110 |   return [[\"LINKEDIN\", o.linkedin], [\"X / TWITTER THREAD\", formatXThread(o.xThread)], [\"INSTAGRAM\", o.instagram], [\"YOUTUBE DESCRIPTION\", o.youtube], [\"EMAIL NEWSLETTER\", o.newsletter]].map(([label, text]) => `===== ${label} =====\\n\\n${text}`).join(\"\\n\\n\");\n111 | }\n112 | function clampTweet(tweet: string): string { return tweet.length <= MAX_TWEET_CHARS ? tweet : tweet.slice(0, MAX_TWEET_CHARS - 1).trimEnd() + \"\u2026\"; }\n113 | \n114 | export function buildMockOutputs({ pillarText, voiceProfile }: GenerateAllInput): PlatformOutputs {\n115 |   const source = pillarText.replace(/\\s+/g, \" \").trim();\n116 |   const s = source.match(/[^.!?]+[.!?]+|[^.!?]+$/g)?.map(x => x.trim()) ?? [source];\n117 |   const first = s[0].replace(/[.!?]+$/, \"\");\n118 |   const second = s[1] ?? \"The same message needs a different shape on every platform.\";\n119 |   const third = s[2] ?? \"The voice should stay recognizable even when the format changes.\";\n120 |   const tone = voiceProfile.tone.split(/[,\u2014]/)[0].trim().toLowerCase() || \"human\";\n121 |   return {\n122 |     linkedin: `${first}.\\n\\nCopy-pasting is not repurposing.\\n\\n${second}\\n\\n${third}\\n\\nKeep the idea steady while changing the hook, pacing, and structure. That gives each platform a native version without sanding away the creator's point of view.\\n\\nThe result should feel ${tone}, not generic.\\n\\nWhat would you adapt first?\\n\\n#ContentStrategy #CreatorTools`,\n123 |     xThread: [clampTweet(`${first}. Posting the same copy everywhere is not a distribution strategy.`), clampTweet(`${second} The hook and pacing need to fit where people read it.`), clampTweet(`${third} Adapt the format. Keep the voice.`), \"One source can become many useful posts without becoming generic. What would you repurpose first?\"],\n124 |     instagram: `${first} \u2728\\n\\nHere\u2019s the challenge: ${second.charAt(0).toLowerCase()}${second.slice(1)}\\n\\nA strong repurpose changes the opening, rhythm, and structure for the platform while protecting the idea and the person behind it.\\n\\nLess copy-paste. More content that feels native and still sounds ${tone}.\\n\\nSave this for the next time one good idea needs to travel further.\\n\\n#ContentCreator #ContentStrategy #RepurposeContent #CreatorTools #AuthenticMarketing #SocialMediaTips`,\n125 |     youtube: `${first} \u2014 this video explores how creators can turn one pillar piece into platform-native content without losing their recognizable voice. We cover hooks, pacing, and structure across LinkedIn, X, Instagram, YouTube, and email.\\n\\n${second} ${third}\\n\\nTIMESTAMPS\\n00:00 Introduction\\n00:30 Why copy-paste underperforms\\n01:30 Preserving your voice\\n02:30 Adapting by platform\\n\\nLINKS\\nAdd your project or resource links here.\\n\\nSubscribe and share your approach in the comments.`,\n126 |     newsletter: `Subject: One idea, five native formats \u2014 without losing your voice\\n\\nHi there,\\n\\n${first}.\\n\\n${second} The opportunity is to reshape the hook and pacing while protecting the original point of view.\\n\\nThat makes one pillar piece more useful without making it generic.\\n\\nTry it with one piece you trust: keep the idea, change the structure, and compare the result.\\n\\nReply with the platform you find hardest to write for.`,\n127 |   };\n128 | }\n129 | ",

  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/run-phase3-tests.ts:\n\n1 | import type { VoiceProfile } from \"./lib/gemini\";\n2 | import {\n3 |   buildLinkedInPrompt,\n4 |   generateLinkedInPost,\n5 |   isVoiceProfile,\n6 |   MAX_PILLAR_CHARS,\n7 |   validateGenerateInput,\n8 | } from \"./lib/generation\";\n9 | \n10 | const voiceProfile: VoiceProfile = {\n11 |   tone: \"conversational, direct, and warm\",\n12 |   vocabulary: \"plain English, avoids jargon, uses concrete examples\",\n13 |   sentence_rhythm: \"short punchy sentences with occasional longer reflections\",\n14 |   dos: [\"lead with the point\", \"use natural questions\"],\n15 |   donts: [\"avoid corporate jargon\", \"avoid generic hype\"],\n16 | };\n17 | \n18 | const pillarText =\n19 |   \"Creators are told to post everywhere, but copying one message across platforms rarely works. Each platform rewards a different hook, pace, and structure. AI can help with adaptation, but only if it preserves the creator's recognizable voice rather than flattening it into generic content.\";\n20 | \n21 | async function run() {\n22 |   console.log(\"Running Phase 3 tests...\\n\");\n23 | \n24 |   console.log(\"Test 1: valid voice profile is recognized\");\n25 |   assert(isVoiceProfile(voiceProfile), \"voice profile should be valid\");\n26 |   assert(!isVoiceProfile({ tone: \"warm\" }), \"partial voice profile should be invalid\");\n27 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n28 | \n29 |   console.log(\"Test 2: empty pillar content is rejected\");\n30 |   const empty = validateGenerateInput({ pillarText: \"   \", voiceProfile });\n31 |   assert(!empty.ok && empty.error.includes(\"required\"), \"empty pillar should be rejected\");\n32 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n33 | \n34 |   console.log(\"Test 3: missing voice profile is rejected\");\n35 |   const missingProfile = validateGenerateInput({ pillarText });\n36 |   assert(!missingProfile.ok && missingProfile.error.includes(\"voice profile\"), \"missing profile should be rejected\");\n37 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n38 | \n39 |   console.log(`Test 4: pillar content longer than ${MAX_PILLAR_CHARS} chars is rejected`);\n40 |   const tooLong = validateGenerateInput({ pillarText: \"x\".repeat(MAX_PILLAR_CHARS + 1), voiceProfile });\n41 |   assert(!tooLong.ok && tooLong.error.includes(String(MAX_PILLAR_CHARS)), \"oversized pillar should be rejected\");\n42 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n43 | \n44 |   console.log(\"Test 5: LinkedIn prompt includes platform rules and voice profile\");\n45 |   const prompt = buildLinkedInPrompt({ pillarText, voiceProfile });\n46 |   assert(prompt.includes(\"Platform: LinkedIn\"), \"prompt should name LinkedIn\");\n47 |   assert(prompt.includes(voiceProfile.tone), \"prompt should include voice profile\");\n48 |   assert(prompt.includes(pillarText), \"prompt should include pillar content\");\n49 |   assert(prompt.includes(\"no more than 3 hashtags\"), \"prompt should cap hashtags\");\n50 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n51 | \n52 |   console.log(\"Test 6: no API key uses deterministic local mock\");\n53 |   const result = await generateLinkedInPost({ pillarText, voiceProfile }, undefined);\n54 |   assert(result.mode === \"mock\", \"missing key should select mock mode\");\n55 |   assert(result.linkedInPost.length > 100, \"mock output should be populated\");\n56 |   assert(result.linkedInPost !== pillarText, \"output should adapt rather than copy the source\");\n57 |   assert(result.linkedInPost.includes(\"What would you keep\"), \"mock should end with a CTA\");\n58 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n59 | \n60 |   console.log(\"Test 7: source contains no API key query-string usage\");\n61 |   const source = await import(\"node:fs/promises\").then((fs) => fs.readFile(\"./lib/generation.ts\", \"utf8\"));\n62 |   assert(!source.includes(\"?key=\"), \"API key must not be placed in query string\");\n63 |   assert(source.includes('\"x-goog-api-key\"'), \"API key should be sent in header\");\n64 |   console.log(\"  \u2713 passed\\n\");\n65 | \n66 |   console.log(\"All Phase 3 tests passed without making external API calls.\");\n67 | }\n68 | \n69 | function assert(condition: boolean, message: string) {\n70 |   if (!condition) {\n71 |     console.error(\"Assertion failed:\", message);\n72 |     process.exit(1);\n73 |   }\n74 | }\n75 | \n76 | run().catch((error) => {\n77 |   console.error(\"Test failed:\", error);\n78 |   process.exit(1);\n79 | });\n80 | ",

{
  "role": "tool",
  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/run-phase5-tests.ts:\n\n1 | import type { VoiceProfile } from \"./lib/gemini\";\n2 | import { analyzeAuthenticity } from \"./lib/authenticity\";\n3 | import { buildAllPlatformsPrompt, DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL } from \"./lib/generation\";\n4 | import { buildRefinePrompt, refinePlatformOutput, validateRefineInput } from \"./lib/refinement\";\n5 | \n6 | const voiceProfile: VoiceProfile = { tone: \"direct, warm\", vocabulary: \"plain English\", sentence_rhythm: \"short punchy sentences\", dos: [\"lead with the point\"], donts: [\"avoid jargon\"] };\n7 | const pillarText = \"One strong idea can travel when its structure changes for each platform.\";\n8 | const assert = (v: unknown, m: string): asserts v => { if (!v) throw new Error(m); };\n9 | \n10 | async function run() {\n11 |   console.log(\"Running Phase 5 tests...\");\n12 |   const strong = analyzeAuthenticity(\"Say the useful thing first. Would you use this tomorrow?\");\n13 |   const generic = analyzeAuthenticity(\"In today's fast-paced world, unlock the power of this game-changer. In conclusion, it will revolutionize your transformative journey!\");\n14 |   assert(strong.score > generic.score && generic.flags.length >= 3, \"generic language should score lower\");\n15 | \n16 |   const prompt = buildAllPlatformsPrompt({ pillarText, voiceProfile });\n17 |   assert(prompt.includes(\"VOICE MATCH PRIORITY\") && prompt.includes(\"hard writing constraint\"), \"batch prompt should enforce voice lock\");\n18 | \n19 |   const request = { platform: \"linkedin\" as const, tweak: \"make it punchier\", currentOutput: \"Current post\", pillarText, voiceProfile };\n20 |   assert(validateRefineInput(request).ok, \"valid refinement should pass\");\n21 |   assert(!validateRefineInput({ ...request, tweak: \"\" }).ok, \"empty tweak should fail\");\n22 |   assert(buildRefinePrompt(request).includes(\"Never do: avoid jargon\"), \"refine prompt should include voice donts\");\n23 | \n24 |   const mock = await refinePlatformOutput(request, undefined);\n25 |   assert(mock.mode === \"mock\" && String(mock.output).includes(\"Mock tweak applied\"), \"no key should use mock refinement\");\n26 | \n27 |   const originalFetch = globalThis.fetch; let calls = 0; let url = \"\"; let init: RequestInit | undefined;\n28 |   globalThis.fetch = async (input, options) => { calls++; url = String(input); init = options; return new Response(JSON.stringify({ candidates: [{ content: { parts: [{ text: \"Punchier revised post\" }] } }] }), { status: 200 }); };\n29 |   try {\n30 |     const result = await refinePlatformOutput(request, \"fake-key\", DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL);\n31 |     assert(result.mode === \"ai\" && result.output === \"Punchier revised post\", \"AI refinement should return revised output\");\n32 |     assert(calls === 1, \"one card tweak should make one provider call\");\n33 |     assert(!url.includes(\"fake-key\"), \"key must not be in URL\");\n34 |     assert(new Headers(init?.headers).get(\"x-goog-api-key\") === \"fake-key\", \"key should remain in header\");\n35 |   } finally { globalThis.fetch = originalFetch; }\n36 |   console.log(\"All Phase 5 tests passed without external API calls.\");\n37 | }\n38 | run().catch(error => { console.error(error); process.exit(1); });\n39 | ",

**Tool** (1784507469932):
{
  "role": "tool",
  "content": "Contents of file echodraft/run-phase4-tests.ts:\n\n1 | import type { VoiceProfile } from \"./lib/gemini\";\n2 | import { DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL, formatAllOutputs, formatXThread, generateAllPlatformPosts, MAX_TWEET_CHARS, normalizePlatformOutputs } from \"./lib/generation\";\n3 | \n4 | const voiceProfile: VoiceProfile = { tone: \"conversational, direct, warm\", vocabulary: \"plain English\", sentence_rhythm: \"short punchy sentences\", dos: [\"lead with point\"], donts: [\"avoid jargon\"] };\n5 | const pillarText = \"Creators are told to post everywhere, but copying one message across platforms rarely works. Each platform rewards a different hook, pace, and structure. Voice should stay recognizable.\";\n6 | const assert = (value: unknown, message: string): asserts value => { if (!value) throw new Error(message); };\n7 | \n8 | async function run() {\n9 |   console.log(\"Running Phase 4 tests...\");\n10 |   const mock = await generateAllPlatformPosts({ pillarText, voiceProfile }, undefined);\n11 |   const o = mock.outputs;\n12 |   assert(mock.mode === \"mock\", \"missing key must use mock\");\n13 |   assert([o.linkedin, o.instagram, o.youtube, o.newsletter].every(x => x.length > 40), \"all outputs populated\");\n14 |   assert(o.xThread.length >= 3 && o.xThread.length <= 5, \"X has 3-5 posts\");\n15 |   assert(o.xThread.every(x => x.length <= MAX_TWEET_CHARS), \"X posts <=280\");\n16 |   assert(formatXThread(o.xThread).startsWith(`1/${o.xThread.length}`), \"X is numbered\");\n17 |   assert((o.instagram.trim().split(\"\\n\").at(-1)?.match(/#/g) || []).length >= 5, \"Instagram hashtags\");\n18 |   assert(o.youtube.includes(\"TIMESTAMPS\") && o.youtube.includes(\"LINKS\"), \"YouTube sections\");\n19 |   assert(o.newsletter.startsWith(\"Subject:\"), \"newsletter subject\");\n20 |   assert(formatAllOutputs(o).includes(\"===== EMAIL NEWSLETTER =====\"), \"export contains labels\");\n21 |   const clamped = normalizePlatformOutputs({ ...o, xThread: [\"x\".repeat(400), \"two\", \"three\"] });\n22 |   assert(clamped.xThread[0].length === 280, \"normalizer clamps tweets\");\n23 | \n24 |   const originalFetch = globalThis.fetch; let calls = 0; let url = \"\"; let init: RequestInit | undefined;\n25 |   globalThis.fetch = async (input, options) => { calls++; url = String(input); init = options; return new Response(JSON.stringify({ candidates: [{ content: { parts: [{ text: JSON.stringify(o) }] } }] }), { status: 200 }); };\n26 |   try {\n27 |     const result = await generateAllPlatformPosts({ pillarText, voiceProfile }, \"fake-key\", DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL);\n28 |     assert(result.mode === \"ai\", \"key path returns ai mode\");\n29 |     assert(calls === 1, \"exactly one batched provider call\");\n30 |     assert(!url.includes(\"fake-key\"), \"key absent from URL\");\n31 |     assert(new Headers(init?.headers).get(\"x-goog-api-key\") === \"fake-key\", \"key in header\");\n32 |     const body = JSON.parse(String(init?.body));\n33 |     assert(body.generationConfig.responseMimeType === \"application/json\", \"structured JSON requested\");\n34 |   } finally { globalThis.fetch = originalFetch; }\n35 |   console.log(\"All Phase 4 tests passed without external API calls.\");\n36 | }\n37 | run().catch(e => { console.error(e); process.exit(1); });\n38 | ",
