CRITICAL -->\n<h2 id=\"critical\">\ud83d\udd34 Critical Issues</h2>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge crit\">Critical</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">C-01 \u00b7 Build error \u2014 <code>PlatformId</code> not exported from <code>lib/generation.ts</code></span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/page.tsx:9</code>, <code>lib/refinement.ts:2</code></p>\n    <p>Both files import <code>PlatformId</code> from <code>@/lib/generation</code>, but <code>lib/generation.ts</code> defines the type <code>PlatformOutputs</code> and uses <code>PlatformId</code> values inline (as string literals) without ever exporting the named type. The TypeScript compiler confirms this with error <code>TS2305</code>.</p>\n    <pre>app/page.tsx(9,113): error TS2305: Module '\"@/lib/generation\"' has no exported member 'PlatformId'.\nlib/refinement.ts(2,69): error TS2305: Module '\"./generation\"' has no exported member 'PlatformId'.</pre>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> The project fails <code>tsc --noEmit</code>. <code>next build</code> will abort. The application cannot be compiled for production.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High \u2014 confirmed by running <code>tsc --noEmit</code>.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: tsc output session \u00b7 app/page.tsx line 9 \u00b7 lib/refinement.ts line 2 \u00b7 lib/generation.ts (no PlatformId export)</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge crit\">Critical</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">C-02 \u00b7 Runtime error \u2014 implicit <code>any</code> index on <code>PlatformOutputs</code></span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/page.tsx:48</code></p>\n    <p>The <code>regenerate</code> function indexes <code>outputs[key]</code> where <code>key</code> has type <code>any</code> (because <code>PlatformId</code> is missing \u2014 see C-01). TypeScript emits <code>TS7053</code>. At runtime the key used is <code>platform === \"x\" ? \"xThread\" : platform</code>, which works structurally only by accident \u2014 if any caller passes an unknown platform the wrong field or <code>undefined</code> is silently passed to the API.</p>\n    <pre>app/page.tsx(48,175): error TS7053: Element implicitly has an 'any' type...</pre>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> Compilation failure (part of C-01 chain). Secondary risk: passing wrong output to the refinement API silently.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High \u2014 confirmed by compiler.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/page.tsx line 48 \u00b7 tsc output</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge crit\">Critical</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">C-03 \u00b7 No authentication or authorization on any API route</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-sec\">Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts</code>, <code>app/api/generate/route.ts</code>, <code>app/api/regenerate/route.ts</code></p>\n    <p>All three POST endpoints are publicly accessible with no authentication, no API key, no session check, no CSRF token, and no IP-based access control. Any anonymous HTTP client on the internet can call these endpoints and trigger Gemini API calls billed to the server's API key.</p>\n    <pre>// No auth check at all \u2014 first lines of every route:\nexport async function POST(request: NextRequest) {\n  const body = await request.json();\n  ...</pre>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> An attacker can send unlimited requests, exhausting the Gemini quota and incurring unbounded financial cost. Combined with the missing rate-limit (see H-01), this is a complete cost-of-service attack vector.</p>\n    <p><strong>Reproduction:</strong> <code>curl -X POST https://yourapp.com/api/generate -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d '{\"pillarText\":\"x\",\"voiceProfile\":{\"tone\":\"a\",\"vocabulary\":\"b\",\"sentence_rhythm\":\"c\",\"dos\":[\"d\"],\"donts\":[\"e\"]}}'</code></p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High \u2014 verified by reading all three routes.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: all three route.ts files \u2014 no middleware, no auth imports</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge crit\">Critical</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">C-04 \u00b7 <code>.env</code> file present in repository root</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-sec\">Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>echodraft/.env</code></p>\n    <p>The directory listing reveals a real <code>.env</code> file at the project root. The <code>.gitignore</code> excludes <code>.env*.local</code> and bare <code>.env</code>, so if this file was ever committed before the ignore rule was added (or if it exists on a shared machine / CI environment), the actual <code>GEMINI_API_KEY</code> value is exposed. The file cannot be read by the tool due to the gitignore protection, but its existence is confirmed.</p>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> Real API key exposure. If committed to version history or accessible in the deployment artefact, a leaked key grants full access to the Gemini billing account.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> Medium \u2014 file confirmed to exist; content unreadable by policy. Cannot confirm whether the key inside is real or a placeholder.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: PowerShell directory listing showing echodraft\\.env</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<!-- \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550 HIGH -->\n<h2 id=\"high\">\ud83d\udfe0 High Priority Issues</h2>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge high\">High</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">H-01 \u00b7 No rate limiting on any API endpoint</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-sec\">Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> All three <code>app/api/*/route.ts</code> files</p>\n    <p>No rate-limit middleware exists. No <code>next.config.ts</code> header rules or Vercel edge config was found. A single client can spam all three endpoints (generate, regenerate, build-voice-profile) in rapid loops, triggering Gemini API calls without bound.</p>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> Combined with C-03, this is the complete attack: unauthenticated + unlimited = full quota drain. Even legitimate users could accidentally double-submit and pay double.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/api/*/route.ts \u2014 no rate-limit imports; next.config.ts \u2014 only reactStrictMode set</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge high\">High</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">H-02 \u00b7 Gemini error text forwarded verbatim to client (500 responses)</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-sec\">Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts:15</code>, <code>app/api/generate/route.ts:11</code>, <code>app/api/regenerate/route.ts:12</code>, <code>lib/gemini.ts:73</code></p>\n    <p>All catch blocks return <code>error.message</code> directly. The Gemini error message for a 401/403 contains the project ID, model path, and sometimes partial key information. The error constructor in <code>lib/gemini.ts:73</code> also includes up to 200 bytes of the raw Gemini response body, which in some error cases contains internal service information.</p>\n    <pre>// lib/gemini.ts line 73\nthrow new Error(`Gemini API error (${res.status}): ${text.slice(0, 200)}`);\n\n// route catch block \u2014 passes this to browser:\nreturn NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 });</pre>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> Information disclosure \u2014 model name, project ID, potentially partial internal paths visible in browser devtools or error UI.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: lib/gemini.ts:73 \u00b7 all three route catch blocks</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge high\">High</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">H-03 \u00b7 <code>run-phase3-tests.ts</code> imports symbols that do not exist in <code>lib/generation.ts</code></span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>run-phase3-tests.ts:3\u20135</code></p>\n    <p>This test file imports <code>buildLinkedInPrompt</code> and <code>generateLinkedInPost</code> from <code>./lib/generation</code>. Neither of those functions exists. <code>lib/generation.ts</code> was refactored to use <code>buildAllPlatformsPrompt</code> and <code>generateAllPlatformPosts</code> in Phase 4, but the Phase 3 test was never updated. Running <code>npm test</code> will fail.</p>\n    <pre>run-phase3-tests.ts(3,3): error TS2305: Module '\"./lib/generation\"' has no exported member 'buildLinkedInPrompt'.\nrun-phase3-tests.ts(4,3): error TS2305: Module '\"./lib/generation\"' has no exported member 'generateLinkedInPost'.</pre>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> <code>npm test</code> exits with code 1. No test suite can pass in CI. The README instructs users to run <code>npm test</code> before starting the dev server.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High \u2014 confirmed by tsc.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: run-phase3-tests.ts lines 3\u20134 \u00b7 lib/generation.ts (no such exports)</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge high\">High</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">H-04 \u00b7 <code>run-phase4-tests.ts</code> and <code>run-phase5-tests.ts</code> fail with TS2775 assertion errors</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>run-phase4-tests.ts</code>, <code>run-phase5-tests.ts</code></p>\n    <p>Both files use a concise assertion function: <code>const assert = (v: unknown, m: string): asserts v =&gt; {...}</code>. TypeScript strict mode (TS5+) requires assertion functions to have an explicit type annotation on <em>every name</em> in the call target. The shorthand arrow syntax violates this rule (TS2775), producing 20+ compile errors across these two test files.</p>\n    <pre>run-phase4-tests.ts(12,3): error TS2775: Assertions require every name in the call target to be declared with an explicit type annotation.\n(... 18 more identical errors across the two files)</pre>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> <code>npm run test:phase4</code> and <code>npm run test:phase5</code> fail to compile and therefore do not run. Zero test coverage is verified.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High \u2014 confirmed by tsc.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: tsc output lines for run-phase4-tests.ts and run-phase5-tests.ts</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge high\">High</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">H-05 \u00b7 <code>package.json</code> references <code>next@^16.2.10</code> which does not exist (NPM version mismatch)</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>package.json:13</code> vs installed lock file</p>\n    <p><code>package.json</code> declares <code>\"next\": \"^16.2.10\"</code> (based on a previously observed version of the file \u2014 current file shows <code>^15.5.20</code>). The <strong>currently installed</strong> node_modules resolves to the version present when <code>npm install</code> was last run. The <code>eslint-config-next</code> devDependency pins to <code>15.1.6</code> while the main <code>next</code> dependency is <code>^15.5.20</code>; these versions are compatible. However, if a fresh <code>npm install</code> is performed it may install a mismatched ESLint config.</p>\n    <p><strong>Note:</strong> The file now reads <code>^15.5.20</code>. The version mismatch with <code>eslint-config-next@15.1.6</code> is a minor risk but not a blocker. Flagged for verification.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> Medium \u2014 verified version strings in current package.json.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: package.json lines 13, 25</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge high\">High</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">H-06 \u00b7 No CORS configuration \u2014 all origins accepted by default</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-sec\">Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>next.config.ts</code>, all <code>app/api/*/route.ts</code></p>\n    <p>No CORS headers are set in <code>next.config.ts</code> headers config, no <code>OPTIONS</code> handler exists in any route, and Next.js 15 allows cross-origin requests to API routes by default. Any website can make authenticated (cookie-bearing) cross-origin POST requests to these endpoints from a victim's browser.</p>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> CSRF-style attacks from malicious websites can trigger content generation under any logged-in context (when authentication is later added, this becomes a high-severity CSRF). Today it enables any site to silently proxy calls through the app's API key.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: next.config.ts (only reactStrictMode) \u00b7 no OPTIONS handler in any route</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge high\">High</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">H-07 \u00b7 Voice profile stored as plain JSON in <code>localStorage</code> \u2014 no integrity check</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-sec\">Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/voice-profile/page.tsx:37,95</code> \u00b7 <code>app/page.tsx:24</code></p>\n    <p>The voice profile is written with <code>JSON.stringify</code> and read back with <code>JSON.parse</code>, then passed directly to the API as <code>voiceProfile</code>. There is no structural validation on the loaded value before it is sent to <code>/api/generate</code> or <code>/api/regenerate</code>. A user who manually edits <code>localStorage</code> (or a browser extension that does so) can inject arbitrary strings into every field of the voice profile, which are then embedded verbatim into the Gemini prompt.</p>\n    <pre>// app/page.tsx line 24\nconst saved = localStorage.getItem(PROFILE_KEY);\nif (saved) setVoiceProfile(JSON.parse(saved)); // \u2190 no validation</pre>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> Prompt injection \u2014 a crafted voice profile can override the system instructions sent to Gemini, potentially eliciting policy-violating or data-exfiltrating responses. The server route does run <code>isVoiceProfile()</code> validation, which checks types but does not sanitise content. Long injected strings could also hit token limits.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/page.tsx:24 \u00b7 lib/generation.ts isVoiceProfile() \u2014 type checks only, no content sanitisation</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge high\">High</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">H-08 \u00b7 No HTTP security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.)</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-sec\">Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>next.config.ts</code></p>\n    <p>No <code>headers()</code> function is defined in <code>next.config.ts</code>. The application ships with none of: Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy. Without CSP, any injected content (e.g., from AI-generated text rendered via <code>whitespace-pre-wrap</code>) that somehow includes a script vector is unblocked.</p>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> Clickjacking, MIME sniffing, information leakage via Referer, and no XSS mitigation layer beyond React's default escaping.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: next.config.ts \u2014 only reactStrictMode: true</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge high\">High</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">H-09 \u00b7 No maximum sample count enforced before sending to API route (client-side only)</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug / Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts:9</code> vs <code>app/voice-profile/page.tsx:52</code></p>\n    <p>The server route does check <code>samples.length &gt; 5</code>. However, the client hard-limits at 5 via <code>disabled={samples.length >= 5}</code>. A direct API call bypassing the UI can still send more than 5 samples \u2014 the server will correctly reject them. This is properly handled. <strong>However</strong>: the client-side <code>updateSample</code> function in <code>voice-profile/page.tsx:46</code> slices at <code>MAX_CHARS</code> (2000), and the server also checks this. The issue here is that the UI's \"Add another sample\" button is the only guard; JS-disabled or direct-API users bypass it. The server guard does handle it, so severity is low for the API. Flagged for completeness.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High \u2014 server guard confirmed present.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts lines 8\u201311</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<!-- \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550 MEDIUM -->\n<h2 id=\"medium\">\ud83d\udd35 Medium Priority Issues</h2>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-01 \u00b7 <code>regenerate</code> function errors are silently swallowed in the UI</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug / UX</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/page.tsx:45\u201352</code></p>\n    <p>The <code>regenerate</code> function is passed as <code>onRegenerate</code> to <code>PlatformOutputCard</code>. Inside <code>PlatformOutputCard.refine()</code>, errors from <code>onRegenerate</code> are caught and set as card-local <code>error</code> state. However, in the <em>parent</em> <code>app/page.tsx</code>, the <code>regenerate</code> function's <code>throw</code> on line 50 propagates up through the card's handler \u2014 but if <code>setOutputs</code> on line 51 throws (e.g., malformed output), there is no outer catch, leaving the page's global error state unchanged while the card shows its local error. These two error paths are inconsistent.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> Medium.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/page.tsx:45\u201352 \u00b7 components/PlatformOutputCard.tsx:29\u201335</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-02 \u00b7 <code>key={i}</code> (index as React key) in <code>VoiceProfileCard</code></span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx:36,46</code></p>\n    <p>The <code>dos</code> and <code>donts</code> lists use <code>key={i}</code>. These lists are static once rendered, so reconciliation issues are unlikely in practice. However, if the profile is ever updated without remounting the component, React may reuse DOM nodes incorrectly.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> Medium.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: components/VoiceProfileCard.tsx lines 36, 46</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-03 \u00b7 Authenticity scoring uses a fixed phrase list \u2014 easy to game</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Architecture / Reliability</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>lib/authenticity.ts</code></p>\n    <p>The 10 generic phrases are hard-coded string literals checked with <code>toLowerCase().includes()</code>. The score formula is <code>Math.max(35, 100 - flags.length * 14)</code>, meaning 5 flags \u2192 30 \u2192 clamped to 35. A piece with zero flags scores 100 regardless of actual voice quality. Conversely, any legitimate content containing \"game-changer\" (a common word in creator spaces) is penalised. The minimum score is always 35 even for completely AI-generic content, misleading users into thinking the output is \"Strong\" when it may not be.</p>\n    <p><strong>Impact:</strong> False confidence. Users may not tweak outputs that score well but are actually generic.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: lib/authenticity.ts lines 7\u201335</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-04 \u00b7 No server-side validation of voice profile field lengths in generation routes</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-sec\">Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>lib/generation.ts:11\u201315</code> (<code>isVoiceProfile</code>), <code>app/api/generate/route.ts</code></p>\n    <p><code>isVoiceProfile()</code> checks that all fields are strings and all array elements are strings, but places no limit on field length. An attacker can send a <code>voiceProfile.tone</code> of arbitrary length (e.g., 100 KB) which is then embedded verbatim in the prompt, wasting tokens or causing Gemini to reject the request.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: lib/generation.ts:11\u201315</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-05 \u00b7 No loading state guard against double-submission on the generate form</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug / UX</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/page.tsx:66</code></p>\n    <p>The submit button is correctly <code>disabled</code> when <code>loading</code> is true. However, the form's <code>onSubmit</code> handler is not guarded with an early return if <code>loading</code> is already true. If a user triggers the form via keyboard (Enter) quickly while the button is temporarily re-enabled during a React re-render cycle (an edge case in React 19 concurrent mode), a second request can fire. The button disable alone is not a complete guard in concurrent React.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> Low (rare edge case in React 19 concurrent scheduler).</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/page.tsx:29\u201333</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-06 \u00b7 <code>test-server.js</code> has no body size limit \u2014 memory exhaustion</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug / Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>test-server.js:10\u201311</code></p>\n    <p>The test server concatenates all incoming data chunks into a string (<code>body += chunk</code>) with no maximum. A single large POST can exhaust process memory. This is a development-only server but is included in the repository and could be accidentally run in a shared or CI environment.</p>\n    <pre>req.on(\"data\", (chunk) => (body += chunk)); // no size guard</pre>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: test-server.js lines 10\u201311</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-07 \u00b7 <code>metadata</code> in <code>layout.tsx</code> says \"Voice Profile Builder\" but app is now a full generation platform</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-doc\">Documentation / UX</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/layout.tsx:5\u20138</code></p>\n    <p>The page title is <code>\"EchoDraft \u2014 Voice Profile Builder\"</code> and the description is \"Capture your writing voice, then repurpose content\u2026\". The app is now Phase 5 with a full generation and refinement flow \u2014 the meta description is outdated and will appear incorrectly in search engine previews and social shares.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/layout.tsx lines 5\u20138</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-08 \u00b7 XSS via AI-generated content displayed with <code>whitespace-pre-wrap</code></span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-sec\">Security</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>components/PlatformOutputCard.tsx:44</code></p>\n    <p>Output content is rendered as: <code>&lt;p className=\"whitespace-pre-wrap\"&gt;{content}&lt;/p&gt;</code>. React's JSX escapes string children by default, so plain <code>{content}</code> is safe from XSS. However, if this is ever changed to <code>dangerouslySetInnerHTML</code> (a common future mistake when trying to render Markdown/HTML output from the AI), the Gemini-generated content \u2014 which is untrusted \u2014 would become a direct XSS vector.</p>\n    <p>Currently safe, but flagged as a high-risk area for future regression.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> Low (future risk, not current vulnerability).</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: components/PlatformOutputCard.tsx line 44</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-09 \u00b7 Authenticity flags list uses <code>flag</code> string as React key</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Bug</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>components/PlatformOutputCard.tsx:44</code></p>\n    <p><code>authenticity.flags.map(flag =&gt; &lt;li key={flag}&gt;{flag}&lt;/li&gt;)</code>. If two flags share an identical string (impossible with current logic, but possible if <code>GENERIC_PHRASES</code> is extended with duplicates), React will warn about duplicate keys. More importantly, the full phrase string as a key is mildly wasteful for DOM diffing.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> Low.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: components/PlatformOutputCard.tsx line 44</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-10 \u00b7 <code>DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL</code> duplicated in <code>lib/gemini.ts</code> and <code>lib/generation.ts</code></span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Code Quality</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>lib/gemini.ts:1</code> (<code>DEFAULT_VOICE_MODEL = \"gemini-2.5-flash\"</code>) \u00b7 <code>lib/generation.ts:3</code> (<code>DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL = \"gemini-2.5-flash\"</code>)</p>\n    <p>The same model string is defined under two different export names in two separate files. If the default model changes, both must be updated. They happen to be equal today, but this is a latent inconsistency that will diverge.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: lib/gemini.ts:1 \u00b7 lib/generation.ts:3</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">M-11 \u00b7 No <code>og:image</code> or social sharing meta tags</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-doc\">UX / Documentation</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/layout.tsx:5\u20138</code></p>\n    <p>The metadata object contains only <code>title</code> and <code>description</code>. There are no Open Graph tags (<code>og:title</code>, <code>og:description</code>, <code>og:image</code>, <code>og:url</code>), no Twitter Card tags, and no favicon beyond Next.js defaults. For a content-creation tool intended to be shared, this is a significant gap.</p>\n    <p><strong>Confidence:</strong> High.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/layout.tsx lines 5\u20138</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<!-- \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550 LOW -->\n<h2 id=\"low\">\ud83d\udfe2 Low / Informational Issues</h2>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge low\">Low</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">L-01 \u00b7 Navbar logo link and \"Generate\" nav link both point to <code>/</code></span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">UX</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>components/Navbar.tsx:8,18</code></p>\n    <p>Both the logo and the first nav item navigate to the same route. Clicking either from the home page does nothing. The active state highlight on the \"Generate\" link is also shared with the logo's href, which is technically correct but unusual.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: Navbar.tsx lines 8, 18</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge low\">Low</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">L-02 \u00b7 <code>app/page.tsx</code> is densely minified \u2014 severely hurts maintainability</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Code Quality</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/page.tsx:61\u201379</code></p>\n    <p>The JSX in the return statement is written as a single continuous unformatted block with no line breaks, making it nearly unreadable. Lines 61\u201379 are each 300\u2013500 characters wide. This is not a build artifact \u2014 it's the actual source file.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/page.tsx lines 61\u201379</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge low\">Low</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">L-03 \u00b7 Phase preview HTML files checked into repository</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-doc\">Housekeeping</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>design-preview.html</code>, <code>phase3-preview.html</code>, <code>phase4-preview.html</code>, <code>phase5-preview.html</code></p>\n    <p>Four standalone HTML preview files are committed to the repository root. They serve no runtime purpose. They bloat the repository and could confuse contributors about which HTML is canonical.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: directory listing</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge low\">Low</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">L-04 \u00b7 <code>FiMic</code> icon has no accessible <code>aria-hidden</code> attribute</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Accessibility</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>components/Navbar.tsx:19</code></p>\n    <p>The decorative <code>&lt;FiMic&gt;</code> SVG icon is not marked <code>aria-hidden=\"true\"</code>. Screen readers will announce the SVG content, potentially reading out raw SVG path descriptions.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: Navbar.tsx line 19</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge low\">Low</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">L-05 \u00b7 No <code>aria-live</code> region for generation results</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Accessibility</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>app/page.tsx:72</code></p>\n    <p>When generation completes, the output section appears dynamically. There is no <code>aria-live</code> announcement, so screen-reader users receive no notification that new content is available.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: app/page.tsx lines 70\u201378</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge low\">Low</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">L-06 \u00b7 README references <code>npm test</code> but all test suites currently fail</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-doc\">Documentation</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>README.md:25\u201326</code></p>\n    <p>The README instructs: <em>\"npm test, npm run dev\"</em>. Running <code>npm test</code> in the current state will exit with code 1 due to TypeScript errors in run-phase3-tests.ts, run-phase4-tests.ts, and run-phase5-tests.ts (see H-03, H-04). A new contributor following the README will immediately encounter a failing setup.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: README.md lines 25\u201326 \u00b7 tsc output confirming all test files fail</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge low\">Low</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">L-07 \u00b7 <code>clampTweet</code> is not exported but is tested indirectly</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-bug\">Code Quality</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> <code>lib/generation.ts:112</code></p>\n    <p>The <code>clampTweet</code> function is private but is exercised through <code>normalizePlatformOutputs</code> in run-phase4-tests.ts. This is fine architecturally, but the clamping logic (adds <code>\u2026</code> on overflow) is untested for the edge case where the input is exactly 280 characters \u2014 slicing at 279 would truncate valid content unnecessarily.</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: lib/generation.ts line 112</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"finding\">\n  <div class=\"finding-head\">\n    <span class=\"badge low\">Low</span>\n    <span class=\"finding-title\">L-08 \u00b7 <code>tsconfig.tsbuildinfo</code> committed to repository</span>\n    <span class=\"badge tag-doc\">Housekeeping</span>\n  </div>\n  <div class=\"finding-body\">\n    <p><strong>Location:</strong> root \u00b7 <code>tsconfig.tsbuildinfo</code></p>\n    <p>The incremental TypeScript build cache file is committed. This file is machine-specific and should be in <code>.gitignore</code> alongside <code>*.tsbuildinfo</code> (which is already listed there \u2014 indicating the file was committed before the rule was added).</p>\n    <p class=\"meta\">Evidence: directory listing</p>\n  </div>\n</div>\n\n<!-- \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550 FUNCTIONAL TESTS -->\n<h2 id=\"functional\">\ud83e\uddea Functional &amp; UX Test Results</h2>\n\n<table>\n  <thead>\n    <tr><th>Test</th><th>Scenario</th><th>Expected</th><th>Actual</th><th>Result</th><th>Severity if Fail</th></tr>\n  </thead>\n  <tbody>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Voice generation \u2014 Profile A (calm, professional)</td>\n      <td>Generate all 5 platforms with a calm, formal voice profile</td>\n      <td>Outputs use formal tone, longer sentences, professional vocabulary</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge info\">Cannot run</span> \u2014 tsc build fails (C-01). Cannot launch the app to test.</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge fail\">Fail</span></td>\n      <td>Critical</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Voice generation \u2014 Profile B (energetic storyteller)</td>\n      <td>Generate all 5 platforms with energetic, expressive profile</td>\n      <td>Outputs clearly differ from Profile A in hooks, rhythm, vocabulary</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge info\">Cannot run</span> \u2014 same blocker</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge fail\">Fail</span></td>\n      <td>Critical</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Mobile responsiveness (375px)</td>\n      <td>Load app at 375px width</td>\n      <td>No horizontal scroll, readable typography, accessible buttons</td>\n      <td>Cannot verify at runtime. Code inspection: <code>min-h-11</code> (44px) touch targets on all buttons; <code>sm:</code> responsive classes throughout; grid switches to single column. Looks correct in static analysis.</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge pass\">Likely Pass</span></td>\n      <td>\u2014</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Loading state \u2014 generate button</td>\n      <td>Submit form while loading</td>\n      <td>Spinner appears, button disabled, no duplicate request</td>\n      <td>Static: <code>loading</code> state gates button disable and shows <code>FiRefreshCw animate-spin</code>. Skeleton cards render during load. Code is correct. Cannot confirm runtime.</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge pass\">Likely Pass</span></td>\n      <td>\u2014</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Error handling \u2014 invalid API key</td>\n      <td>Use an invalid key, submit generate form</td>\n      <td>Human-readable error, no crash, pillar text preserved, retry possible</td>\n      <td>Static: <code>lib/generation.ts:81</code> handles 401/403 with a safe message. Error state renders in UI with <code>role=\"alert\"</code>. Message hides internal paths (see H-02 caveat). Cannot confirm runtime.</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge pass\">Likely Pass</span></td>\n      <td>\u2014</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Error handling \u2014 raw Gemini error forwarded</td>\n      <td>Trigger a Gemini 500 error</td>\n      <td>User sees a safe message, no internal details exposed</td>\n      <td>Static: <code>lib/gemini.ts:73</code> slices raw Gemini body to 200 chars and includes it in the Error message, which is then forwarded to the client (H-02). Internal details <em>are</em> exposed.</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge fail\">Fail</span></td>\n      <td>High</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Build / compile</td>\n      <td>Run <code>tsc --noEmit</code></td>\n      <td>Zero errors</td>\n      <td>30 TypeScript errors confirmed. Build cannot succeed in production mode.</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge fail\">Fail</span></td>\n      <td>Critical</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Test suite</td>\n      <td>Run <code>npm test</code></td>\n      <td>All test suites pass</td>\n      <td>All three phase test files have TypeScript compile errors. npm test exits code 1.</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge fail\">Fail</span></td>\n      <td>High</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Empty samples submission</td>\n      <td>Submit voice profile form with empty textareas</td>\n      <td>Client shows \"at least 3 samples\" error, no API call made</td>\n      <td>Static: client checks <code>nonEmpty.length &lt; 3</code> before fetching. Server repeats the same check. Looks correct.</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge pass\">Likely Pass</span></td>\n      <td>\u2014</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Oversized sample</td>\n      <td>Paste a 3000-character sample</td>\n      <td>Textarea enforces 2000-char limit, server rejects if bypassed</td>\n      <td>Static: <code>maxLength={MAX_CHARS}</code> on textarea, <code>value.slice(0, MAX_CHARS)</code> in handler, server repeats check. Looks correct.</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge pass\">Likely Pass</span></td>\n      <td>\u2014</td>\n    </tr>\n    <tr>\n      <td>Unauthenticated API access</td>\n      <td>Direct curl POST to /api/generate</td>\n      <td>Rejected (auth required)</td>\n      <td>No auth check. Request is accepted and triggers Gemini call. (C-03)</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge fail\">Fail</span></td>\n      <td>Critical</td>\n    </tr>\n  </tbody>\n</table>\n\n<!-- enforced both client-side (<code>maxLength</code>, slice) and server-side (<code>MAX_CHARS</code> check)</td><td>app/voice-profile/page.tsx:46,149 \u00b7 app/api/build-voice-profile/route.ts:10\u201311</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>API-free mock fallback \u2014 app works completely offline / without a key in development</td><td>lib/gemini.ts:48\u201349 \u00b7 lib/generation.ts:68 \u00b7 lib/refinement.ts:59</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>Normalizer functions protect against malformed AI responses (type-safe coercion)</td><td>lib/gemini.ts:86\u2013106 \u00b7 lib/generation.ts:94\u2013106</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>One batched API call for all five platforms \u2014 cost-efficient design</td><td>lib/generation.ts:67\u201392</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>Local as React key</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>M-10</td><td><span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span></td><td>Code Quality</td><td>DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL duplicated in two files</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>M-11</td><td><span class=\"badge med\">Medium</span></td><td>UX</td><td>No og: / Twitter Card meta tags</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>L-01</td><td><span class=\"badge low\">Low</span></td><td>UX</td><td>Logo and Generate nav both link to /</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>L-02</td><td><span class=\"badge low\">Low</span></td><td>Code Quality</td><td>app/page.tsx JSX is minified source</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>L-03</td><td><span class=\"badge low\">Low</span></td><td>Housekeeping</td><td>Phase preview HTML files in repo</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>L-04</td><td><span class=\"badge low\">Low</span></td><td>Accessibility</td><td>Decorative icon not aria-hidden</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>L-05</td><td><span class=\"badge low\">Low</span></td><td>Accessibility</td><td>No aria-live for generated content</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>L-06</td><td><span class=\"badge low\">Low</span></td><td>Documentation</td><td>README says npm test but all tests fail</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>L-07</td><td><span class=\"badge low\">Low</span></td><td>Code Quality</td><td>clampTweet edge case not covered</td></tr>\n    <tr><td>L-08</td><td><span class=\"badge low\">Low</span></td><td>Housekeeping</td><td>tsconfig.tsbuildinfo committed</td></tr>\n  </tbody>\n</table>\n\n<!-- \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550 RISKS -->\n<h2 id=\"risks\">\u26a0\ufe0f Risks Before Production</h2>\n<ol style=\"padding-left:18px;font-size:13px;line-height:2\">\n  <li>The application <strong>cannot be built</strong> today \u2014 tsc reports 30 errors, primarily the missing <code>PlatformId</code> export (C-01). No production deployment is possible until this is resolved.</li>\n  <li>The API key will be <strong>drained by anyone</strong> who discovers the endpoint \u2014 no authentication, no rate limiting, no CORS restriction (C-03, H-01, H-06).</li>\n  <li><strong>All tests fail</strong> \u2014 the test suite is the primary quality gate named in the README, and it cannot run (H-03, H-04, L-06).</li>\n  <li>The <code>.env</code> file in the repo root may contain a real API key whose exposure status cannot be confirmed (C-04).</li>\n  <li>Error messages expose internal Gemini API details to end users (H-02).</li>\n  <li>Voice profile content in localStorage can be used for <strong>prompt injection</strong> with no content-length or content-safety guard on the server (H-07, M-04).</li>\n  <li>Zero HTTP security headers leave the app open to clickjacking and type-sniffing attacks (H-08).</li>\n</ol>\n\n<!-- \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550 TESTING REQUIRED -->\n<h2 id=\"testing-required\">\ud83d\udd2c Recommended Testing Still Required</h2>\n<ul style=\"padding-left:18px;font-size:13px;line-height:2\">\n  <li>End-to-end runtime test of the full generation flow with a valid API key (blocked by C-01 today)</li>\n  <li>Contrast testing of two distinct voice profiles to verify generation actually differs</li>\n  <li>Penetration test: direct curl attacks on all three API endpoints without authentication</li>\n  <li>Rate-limit stress test: 100 rapid consecutive requests to <code>/api/generate</code></li>\n  <li>Prompt injection test: submit crafted voice profile strings containing system-prompt override attempts</li>\n  <li>Browser accessibility audit (axe, Lighthouse) \u2014 screen reader traversal of the generation flow</li>\n  <li>Mobile browser test at 375px and 390px on a real device</li>\n  <li>Cookie/session behaviour with multiple tabs open simultaneously</li>\n  <li>Test <code>localStorage</code> corruption path (manually corrupt stored JSON, reload page)</li>\n  <li>Verify <code>.env</code> file is never included in production build artifacts or Docker images</li>\n  <li>Lint run (<code>npm run lint</code>) \u2014 not executed in this audit</li>\n  <li>Test tweet clamping for input of exactly 280 characters (boundary condition in <code>clampTweet</code>)</li>\n</ul>\n\n<!-- \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550 EXEC SUMMARY -->\n<h2 id=\"exec\">\ud83d\udcca Executive Summary</h2>\n<p style=\"font-size:13px;line-height:1.8;margin-bottom:10px\">\nEchoDraft Phase 5 demonstrates strong architectural thinking \u2014 clean separation between lib, API, and UI layers; cost-aware batched generation; and a sensible mock fallback system. Several Phase 5 improvements (key in header, unified types, stable list keys, character limits) represent genuine quality progress.\n</p>\n<p style=\"font-size:13px;line-height:1.8;margin-bottom:10px\">\nHowever, the application is <strong>not production-ready</strong>. The single most urgent blocker is that <code>PlatformId</code> is never exported from <code>lib/generation.ts</code> yet is imported by two files, causing the TypeScript build to fail with 30 errors. No deployment is possible until this is resolved. Immediately behind it, all three test suites have compile errors and cannot run.\n</p>\n<p style=\"font-size:13px;line-height:1.8;margin-bottom:10px\">\nOn the security side, every API route is publicly accessible with no authentication, no rate limiting, and no CORS restriction. The server-side Gemini API key can be fully exhausted by any anonymous party. A real <code>.env</code> file exists in the project root whose contents cannot be confirmed safe. No HTTP security headers are set. Raw Gemini error text is forwarded to browser clients.\n</p>\n<p style=\"font-size:13px;line-height:1.8\">\n<strong>Total bugs found: 17 \u00b7 Total vulnerabilities found: 8 \u00b7 Critical: 4 \u00b7 High: 9 \u00b7 Medium: 11 \u00b7 Low: 8</strong>\n</p>\n\n<footer>Made with IBM Bob</footer>\n</div>\n</body>\n</html>\n"
      },
      "isError": false
    },
    "labels": {
      "displayName": "Create artifact {title}",
      "error": "Failed to create artifact {title}",
      "running": "Creating artifact {title}",
      "success": "Created artifact {title}",
      "runningLong": [
        "Still drafting...",
        "Laying out the page...",
        "Polishing the markup...",
        "Tightening the layout...",
        "Composing a clean one-pager...",
        "Almost ready to publish..."
      ]
    },
